Profiles</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Select to group by profiles.</td></tr><tr><td>Group by Metaclasses</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Select to group by metaclasses.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-grey" data-highlight-colour="grey">Structure Browser</td><td class="highlight-grey" data-highlight-colour="grey"><br /></td><td class="highlight-grey" data-highlight-colour="grey"><br /></td><td class="highlight-grey" data-highlight-colour="grey"><br /></td></tr><tr><td>Show Inherited Structure</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Select to show properties inherited from the general classifiers.</td></tr><tr><td>Nest Satisfied Requirements</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Select to nest requirements within the satisfying elements.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff"><strong title="">Composition Inspection</strong></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7">General</td></tr><tr><td>Removal of the package share confirmation</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to ask for confirmation when a shared package is removed from a used project.</td></tr><tr><td>Removal of shared package element confirmation</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to ask for confirmation when an element is removed from a shared package of a used project.</td></tr><tr><td>Element moved outside shared package confirmation</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to ask for confirmation when an element is moved outside the shared package in a used project.</td></tr><tr><td>Element moved between used projects confirmation</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to ask for confirmation when an element is moved from one used project to another.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff"><strong>Collaboration</strong></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7">General</td></tr><tr><td>Auto Login to Server</td><td>false</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to enable the automatic logging in to the server upon starting the application.</td></tr><tr><td>Show Project Versions Conflict Warning</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to display a warning message when two projects have the same usages.</td></tr><tr><td>Include Project Version Information to Element URL</td><td>Ask User</td><td>Ask User</td><td>Specify the mode of the project version information usage in the element URL.</td></tr><tr><td>Remember User Name Between Sessions</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Select to remember the last entered user name in the login dialog.</td></tr><tr><td>Remember Server Between Sessions</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Select to remember all server names to which a successful connection was established.</td></tr><tr><td>Server Name </td><td>localhost</td><td>localhost</td><td>specify the name of the server. the name is automatically set once you have logged in to the server.</td></tr><tr><td>Single Sign On Server Name</td><td>localhost</td><td>localhost</td><td>Specify the name of the Single Sign On server. The name is automatically set once you have logged in to the server.</td></tr><tr><td>Use Secured Connection (SSL)</td><td>false</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to use a secured connection (SSL) while working with the server.</td></tr><tr><td>Auto-Commit After Project is Migrated to New Version</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to allow the application to auto-commit a project to the server after the project is migrated due to new Standard/System profiles or new model extensions.</td></tr><tr><td>SSO Authentication as Default</td><td>false</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to set Singl Sign On authentication as a default method. If the value is set to false, the basic username/password authentication is set as default.</td></tr><tr><td>Notify About New Project Versions on the Server</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to get a notification when a server project is modified on the server by other users.</td></tr><tr><td>Notify About Newer Project Usages</td><td>Open</td><td><p>Open</p><p>Open, Update</p><p>Never</p></td><td>Specify commands when the notifications about newer project usages should be shown.</td></tr><tr><td>Interval to Check For New Project Version (in seconds)</td><td>120</td><td><p>30</p><p>45</p><p>60</p><p>90</p><p>120</p></td><td>Specify how often to check for a new project version on the server (in seconds).</td></tr><tr><td>Enable Locking Without Forced Project Update </td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Lock elements without updating the project when possible.</td></tr><tr><td>Support Mount Points of Usages on Server</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Create Mount Relationships to connect former mount point packages to the former mounted elements when importing projects to the server.</td></tr><tr><td>Web Application Platform URL</td><td>-</td><td>Custom</td><td>Specify the Web Application Platform URL (for example, <a href="https://example.com:8443).">https://example.com:8443).</a> It is used to quickly access Teamwork Cloud web apps from within the modeling tool.</td></tr><tr><td>Representation of Users</td><td>User Name</td><td><p>User Name</p><p>Full Name</p><p>Email</p></td><td>Select the field to represent the users. If the selected field is left empty, the User Name is shown by default.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"><p title=""><strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE</p></td></tr><tr><td>Enable Collaboration Powered by <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE Platform</td><td>false</td><td><p>true/false</p></td><td>Select to enable a collaborative environment powered by <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE Platform integration.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7">CATIA Systems Synthesis Analysis</td></tr><tr><td>Publish MDZipX Upon Commit</td><td>false</td><td>true/false</td><td>Select to publish the MDZipX file to Teamwork Cloud upon project commit.</td></tr><tr><td>Publish Diagrams from Used Projects</td><td>false</td><td>true/false</td><td>Select to include diagrams from used projects in MDZipX file.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff"><strong>Floating</strong></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7">General</td></tr><tr><td>FLEXNet License Server Name</td><td>localhost</td><td>Custom</td><td>Specify the name of the FLEXNet license server.</td></tr><tr><td>FLEXNet License Server Port</td><td>1101</td><td>Custom</td><td>Specify the port number of the FLEXNet license server.</td></tr><tr><td>DSLS License Server Address</td><td><p>localhost:4085</p></td><td>Custom</td><td>Specify the name of the DSLS license server address.</td></tr><tr><td>DSLS License Server Mode</td><td>ORGANIZATION_DEFINED</td><td>Custom</td><td>Specify the DSLS server mode.</td></tr><tr><td>Auto Login to the License Server</td><td>false</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to enable automatic logging into the license server upon starting the application.</td></tr><tr><td>Borrowing Period (hours)</td><td>24</td><td>Real</td><td>Specify the period in time (in hours) for borrowing licenses from the server and using them offline.</td></tr><tr><td>Ask if Run MagicDraw Without Unavailable Plugins</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to display the question message if the user wants to run the application without an unavailable plugin. An unavailable plugin is one whose license is not available in the license server.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff"><strong>Network</strong></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7">HTTP Proxy</td></tr><tr><td>Use HTTP Proxy Server</td><td>false</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to use the HTTP proxy server when the application is checking for updates. </td></tr><tr><td>HTTP Proxy Server Address</td><td>-</td><td>Custom</td><td>Specify the name or IP address of the HTTP proxy server.</td></tr><tr><td>HTTP Proxy Server Port</td><td><p>8080</p></td><td>Custom</td><td>Specify the port of the HTTP proxy server. The default port is 8080.</td></tr><tr><td>HTTP Proxy Server User</td><td>-</td><td>Custom</td><td>Specify the name of the HTTP proxy server user.</td></tr><tr><td>Authenticate HTTP Proxy Server</td><td>false</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to authenticate with the HTTP proxy server.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7">General</td></tr><tr><td>Timeout (seconds, 0 - infinite)</td><td>30</td><td>Real</td><td>Specify the waiting period (in seconds) before canceling a connection prompt.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff"><strong>Keyboard</strong></td></tr><tr><td colspan="4">Assign new, edit, or remove shortcut keys<span style="color: rgb(0,51,0);"> for the main m</span>enu, shortcut menu items, and main and diagram element toolbars.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff"><strong>Plugins</strong></td></tr><tr><td colspan="4">Review detailed information about all installed plugins.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff"><strong>Resources</strong></td></tr><tr><td colspan="4">Review and configure external resources that are used during project load.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff"><strong>Path Variables</strong></td></tr><tr><td colspan="4"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating path variables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Paths variable]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> is a path used in the code engineering process, project usages, hyperlinks, etc. Specify predefined or create and configure custom variables for referencing to file system objects.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff"><strong>Spelling</strong></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7">Spelling</td></tr><tr><td>Check Spelling As You Type</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to underline incorrectly spelled words and provide the suggestion list of possible corrections in the context menu.</td></tr><tr><td>Check Tagged Values</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to check all tagged values that are of string type.</td></tr><tr><td>Dictionary</td><td>English</td><td><p>English</p><p>French</p><p>Russian</p><p>German</p><p>Thai</p><p>Spanish</p></td><td>Select the language for spelling. All languages supported by the &quot;Open Office&quot; can be additionally added to the existing list of supported languages (Environment Options → Spelling → Add Spelling Dictionaries).</td></tr><tr><td>Case Sensitive</td><td>false</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to differentiate the words in meaning based on differing use of uppercase and lowercase letters. </td></tr><tr><td>Use Camel Case Words</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to spell the compound words or phrases in which the words are joined without spaces and are capitalized within the compound - as in BlackColor, as separated words.</td></tr><tr><td>Ignore Upper Case Words</td><td>false</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to not check the spelling of the words with all uppercase letters. </td></tr><tr><td>Skip Numbers</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to not check the spelling of the words with all uppercase letters.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="4">Add Spelling dictionaries. You can add your custom dictionaries. </td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff"><strong>Launchers</strong></td></tr><tr><td colspan="4">Launchers show which programs will be used for opening different file types in the application. Add new, edit, or remove launchers and specify their properties (file extension, program path or name, program arguments).</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff"><strong>Experience</strong></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7">General</td></tr><tr><td>Main Menu Expert Mode </td><td>false</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to show complete command lists in the main menu. Otherwise, command lists will not be complete, and arrows for expanding menus will be visible at the very end of each menu command list.</td></tr><tr><td>Context Menu Expert Mode</td><td>false</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to show complete command lists in the context menu. Otherwise, command lists will not be complete, and arrows for expanding menus will be visible at the very end of each menu command list.</td></tr><tr><td>Main Toolbar Expert Mode</td><td>false</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to show complete command lists in the main toolbar. Otherwise, command lists will not be complete, and arrows for expanding the toolbar will be visible at the very end of each toolbar command list.</td></tr><tr><td>Diagram Main Toolbar Expert Mode</td><td>false</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to show complete command lists in the diagram main toolbar. Otherwise, command lists will not be complete, and arrows for expanding the diagram main toolbar will be visible at the very end of each diagram main toolbar command list.</td></tr><tr><td>Diagram Elements Toolbar Expert Mode</td><td>false</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to show complete command lists in the diagram element toolbar. Otherwise, command lists will not be complete, and arrows for expanding the diagram element toolbar will be visible at the very end of each diagram element toolbar command list.</td></tr><tr><td>Element Properties Mode</td><td>Standard</td><td><p>Standard</p><p>Expert</p><p>All</p></td><td>Specify the mode of displaying properties in the element specification dialog.</td></tr><tr><td>Symbol Properties Mode</td><td>Standard</td><td><p>Standard</p><p>Expert</p><p>All</p></td><td>Specify the mode of displaying properties in the symbol properties dialog.</td></tr><tr><td>Smart Manipulator Expert Mode</td><td>false</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to show complete command lists in the smart manipulators. Otherwise, command lists will not be complete, and arrows for expanding smart manipulators will be visible at the very end of each smart manipulator command list.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff"><strong>External Tools</strong></td></tr><tr><td colspan="4">Specify external tools to be launched from the application. This allows to easily pass modeling artifacts to 3rd party tools, thus facilitating model transformations, code generation, etc.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff"><strong>Notifications</strong></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7">General</td></tr><tr><td>Notification Display Time (seconds)</td><td>10</td><td>Real</td><td>Specify the notification display time in seconds.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7">Severity</td></tr><tr><td>Error</td><td>RGB [255, 0, 0]</td><td>Custom </td><td>Change the color of the Error text in the Notification window.</td></tr><tr><td>Warning</td><td>RGB [0, 0, 0]</td><td>Custom </td><td>Change the color of the Warning text in the Notification window.</td></tr><tr><td>Information</td><td>RGB [0, 0, 0]</td><td>Custom </td><td>Change the color of the Information text in the Notification window.</td></tr><tr><td>Minimal Severity Level</td><td>Information</td><td><p>Information</p><p>Warning</p><p>Error</p></td><td>Specify the minimum severity level. Notification messages of both the specified and higher severity levels will be displayed in the Notification window. The lowest severity level is Information. The highest severity level is Error.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7">History</td></tr><tr><td>Retain History (days)</td><td>30</td><td>Real</td><td>Specify the number of days for how long notification messages should be kept in history.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff"><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Server Certificates" /></ac:link></strong></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7">Trusted by All Users</td></tr><tr><td colspan="4">Manage server certificates trusted by all users in this application on this machine. Add, remove, or view details of the selected trusted server certificate. You may need system administrator rights to manage these certificates.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7">Trusted by Current User</td></tr><tr><td colspan="4">Manage server certificates trusted by the current user in this application on this machine. Add, remove, or view details of the selected trusted server certificate.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff"><strong>Code Engineering</strong></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7">General</td></tr><tr><td>Show Code Engineering Sets</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Select to show the code engineering set items in the Model Browser.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff"><strong title="">Concept Modeling</strong></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7">Concept Modeling</td></tr><tr><td>Enable experimental features</td><td>false</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to enable experimental Cameo Concept Modeler features.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7">Subproperties Tree</td></tr><tr><td>Invert tree</td><td>false</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to invert the subproperties hierarchy so that at the top of the hierarchy the subproperty is displayed. </td></tr><tr><td>Hide properties directly between top and bottom</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to hide properties directly between top and bottom in the subproperties tree.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff"><strong title="">Eclipse UML2 XMI</strong></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7">Eclipse UML2 (v2.x) XMI</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7">Export</td></tr><tr><td>Export Model to Eclipse UML2 XMI on Project Save</td><td>Do not respond</td><td><p>Do not respond</p><p>Ask before export</p><p>Always export</p></td><td>Select the mode of the application model export on project save action. The model will be exported without diagrams.</td></tr><tr><td>Ask to Overwrite Exported Files</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to display a message requesting confirmation to overwrite previously exported files.</td></tr><tr><td>Element ID Export Policy</td><td>Use Element ID</td><td><p>Generate ID</p><p>Use Element ID</p><p>Use Server ID (if applicable)</p></td><td><p style="text-align: left;">Specify how the element ID should be exported. The Server ID is only available in server projects.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Show Stereotype Property Type Change Warning</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to display warning message warnings when exporting a model to the Eclipse UML2 XMI file after the stereotype property type has been changed.</td></tr><tr><td>Show 'Stereotypes from the Same Profile' Warning</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to display a warning message that the stereotype will not be applied to the element if this element is in the same profile with the stereotype.</td></tr><tr><td>Apply All Profiles to Root Model</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to apply all the exported profiles to the root model. Otherwise, all the exported profiles will be applied to the nearest nesting package.</td></tr><tr><td>Move Stereotype to Nesting Profile</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to move not a directly contained stereotype to a nesting profile.</td></tr><tr><td>Change Stereotype Property Type Stereotype</td><td>Do not change</td><td><p>Do not change</p><p>Single extended metaclass only</p><p>Common super of extended classes</p></td><td>Specify the mode of changing the stereotype's property type's stereotype on model export.</td></tr><tr><td>Change not eCore Datatype to String</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to change the stereotype's property type to String on export if the type is not eCore data type.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7">Import/Export</td></tr><tr><td>Show Element Mapping Warning</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to display the warning message showing the elements' (metaclasses and data types) mappings between the application and Eclipse UML2. A warning message will appear when exporting or importing a model.</td></tr><tr><td>Display Warning in message Window</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true display project exporting warning in the Message Window.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7">Import</td></tr><tr><td>Use Stereotype Keyword as name</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to use keywords as names for the stereotypes when importing a model from the Eclipse UML2.</td></tr><tr><td>Preserve IDs</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td><p>Set to true to preserve element IDs on import.</p></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7">Eclipse UML2 (v3.x) XMI</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7">Export</td></tr><tr><td>Export Model to Eclipse UML2 XMI on Project Save</td><td>Do not respond</td><td><p>Do not respond</p><p>Ask before export</p><p>Always export</p></td><td>Select the mode of the application model export on project save action. The model will be exported without diagrams.</td></tr><tr><td>Ask to Overwrite Exported Files</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to display a message requesting confirmation to overwrite previously exported files.</td></tr><tr><td>Element ID Export Policy</td><td>Use Element ID</td><td><p>Generate ID</p><p>Use Element ID</p><p>Use Server ID (if applicable)</p></td><td><p style="text-align: left;">Specify how the element ID should be exported. The Server ID is only available in server projects.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Show Stereotype Property Type Change Warning</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to display warning message warnings when exporting a model to the Eclipse UML2 XMI file after the stereotype property type has been changed.</td></tr><tr><td>Show 'Stereotypes from the Same Profile' Warning</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to display a warning message that the stereotype will not be applied to the element if this element is in the same profile with the stereotype.</td></tr><tr><td>Apply All Profiles to Root Model</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to apply all the exported profiles to the root model. Otherwise, all the exported profiles will be applied to the nearest nesting package.</td></tr><tr><td>Move Stereotype to Nesting Profile</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to move not a directly contained stereotype to a nesting profile.</td></tr><tr><td>Change Stereotype Property Type Stereotype</td><td>Do not change</td><td><p>Do not change</p><p>Single extended metaclass only</p><p>Common super of extended classes</p></td><td>Specify the mode of changing the stereotype's property type's stereotype on model export.</td></tr><tr><td>Change not eCore Datatype to String</td><td>false</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to change the stereotype's property type to String on export if the type is not eCore data type.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7">Import/Export</td></tr><tr><td>Show Element Mapping Warning</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to display the warning message showing the elements' (metaclasses and data types) mappings between the application and Eclipse UML2. A warning message will appear when exporting or importing a model.</td></tr><tr><td>Display Warning in message Window</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true display project exporting warning in the Message Window.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7">Import</td></tr><tr><td>Use Stereotype Keyword as name</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to use keywords as names for the stereotypes when importing a model from the Eclipse UML2.</td></tr><tr><td>Preserve IDs</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td><p>Set to true to preserve element IDs on import.</p></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7">Eclipse UML2 (v4.x) XMI</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7">Export</td></tr><tr><td>Export Model to Eclipse UML2 XMI on Project Save</td><td>Do not respond</td><td><p>Do not respond</p><p>Ask before export</p><p>Always export</p></td><td>Select the mode of the application model export on project save action. Otherwise, all the exported profiles will be applied to the nearest nesting package.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7">Import/Export</td></tr><tr><td>Show Element Mapping Warning</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to display the warning message showing the elements' (metaclasses and data types) mappings between the application and Eclipse UML2. A warning message will appear when exporting or importing a model.</td></tr><tr><td>Display Warning in message Window</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true display project exporting warning in the Message Window.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7">Import</td></tr><tr><td>Use Stereotype Keyword as name</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to use keywords as names for the stereotypes when importing a model from the Eclipse UML2.</td></tr><tr><td>Preserve IDs</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td><p>Set to true to preserve element IDs on import.</p></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff"><strong>Enterprise Architect Import</strong></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7">General</td></tr><tr><td>Switch aggregation side (Recommended for XMI exported from EA 7.1)</td><td>false</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to switch aggregation side. this option is recommended if the XMI is exported from EA 7.1.</td></tr><tr><td>Exclude EA specific data</td><td>false</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true if EA-specific data is not required to be imported.</td></tr><tr><td>Generate conversion log</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to generate a conversion log file.</td></tr><tr><td>Open project after conversion</td><td>false</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to open the project after finishing import.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7">Activity element mapping</td></tr><tr><td>Convert EA WriteVariable Action to</td><td>AddVariableValueAction</td><td><p>AddVariableValueAction</p><p>RemoveVariableValueAction</p></td><td>Select the element type that you want to convert WriteVariable Action to.</td></tr><tr><td>Convert EA WriteLinkAction to</td><td>CreateLinkAction</td><td><p>CreateLinkAction</p><p>DestroyLinkAction</p></td><td>Select the element type that you want to convert WriteLinkAction to.</td></tr><tr><td>Convert EA WriteStructuralFeatureAction to</td><td>AddStructuralFeatureValueAction</td><td><p>AddStructuralFeatureValueAction</p><p>RemoveStructuralFeatureValueAction</p></td><td>Select the element type that you want to convert EA WriteStructuralFeatureAction to.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff"><strong>Macros</strong></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7">General</td></tr><tr><td>Default Macro Language</td><td>Groovy</td><td><p>Groovy (recommended)</p><p>JavaScript Rhino</p><p>Jython</p><p>Beanshell</p></td><td>Set the default macro language. To ensure the best performance experience, it is strongly suggested that you use the recommended language.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff"><strong>Report Wizard</strong></td></tr><tr><td colspan="4">Change various Report Wizard options and configurations.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff"><strong title="">UAF</strong></td></tr><tr><td><p>System Engineering User Interface Mode</p></td><td>false</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to expand the SysML palettes in the UAF diagrams.</td></tr><tr><td>Suggest to change perspective to preferable one</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to get a suggestion to change the perspective to a preferable one (if it is not set) each time a new UAF project is started, a project is changed, or the existing UAF project is loaded.</td></tr><tr><td>Inform if the Is Capable To Perform relationships are not deleted from the model</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to be informed when the <strong>Is Capable To Perform</strong> relationships are not deleted from the model after removing actions or swimlane representations from the behavior diagrams.</td></tr><tr><td>Show Strategic Phasing Gantt Chart Creation Wizard</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to show the Strategic Phasing/CV-3/StV-3/NCV-3/Cr creation wizard every time when creating a new chart.</td></tr><tr><td>Show Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart Creation Wizard </td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to show the Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart creation wizard every time when creating a new chart. </td></tr><tr><td>Show Personnel Availability Gantt Chart Creation Wizard</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to show the Personnel Availability Gantt Chart creation wizard every time when creating a new chart. </td></tr><tr><td>Show Projects Roadmap Creation Wizard </td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to show the Projects Roadmap/PV-2/AcV-2/NPV-1/Lr creation wizard every time when creating a new chart.</td></tr><tr><td>Show Resources/Personnel Forecast and Standards Roadmap Creation Wizard </td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to show the Resources Forecast/Personnel Forecast/SV-9/NSV-9/P1 and Standards Roadmap/StdV-2/TV-2/NTV-2 diagram creation wizard every time when creating a new chart.</td></tr><tr><td>Show Strategic Actual Deployment Creation Wizard</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to show the Strategic Actual Deployment/CV-5/StV-5/NCV-5 creation wizard every time when creating a new chart.</td></tr></tbody><colgroup><col style="width: 22.4279%;" /><col style="width: 15.9448%;" /><col style="width: 15.5944%;" /><col style="width: 46.0238%;" /></colgroup></table><p <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189142127 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00335: Customizing lists of properties and commands

- page_id: `189142127`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189142127/Customizing+lists+of+properties+and+commands
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:38:52.632+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can customize the following:

- The list of properties in the element Specification window.
- The list of symbol properties in the Symbol Properties dialog.
- The list of properties in menus that opens after clicking the Compartments smart manipulators and also after clicking the Create Element smart manipulator.
- The list of symbol properties in the symbol shortcut menu.

You can customize lists of properties and commands by using the Customize Properties dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To open the **Customize Properties** dialog, do one of the following

- In the element Specification window , expand the Properties list and click Customize .
- In the Model Browser, the Properties > Element tab, at the right-top corner, click to expand the list and click Customize .
- On the diagram, right-click the symbol and select Symbol Properties . In the Symbol Properties dialog, at the right-top corner, click to expand the list, and click Customize .
- Select the symbol on the diagram. In the Model Browser, in the Properties > Symbol tab, at the right-top corner click to expand the list and click Customize .

When the **Customize Properties** dialog is open from the element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] or the **Element** tab from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space=''], you can customize the list of properties in the element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''].

When the **Customize Properties** dialog is opened from the **Symbol Properties** dialog or the **Symbol** tab in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space=''], then you can customize the following:

- The list of properties in the element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] .
- The list of properties in the menus that opens after clicking the Compartments smart manipulator and after clicking the Create Element smart manipulator.
- The list of properties in the symbol shortcut menu.

See the following examples of customizing list of properties in the element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''], in the *Compartments* and *Create Element* menus, and in the symbol shortcut menu.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189139670 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00336: Customizing Perspectives

- page_id: `189139670`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189139670/Customizing+Perspectives
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:16.454+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Getting started > Customizing and Selecting Perspective
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

Perspective customization allows grouping of functional features into standard/expert modes. Customization also allows the user to hide unnecessary commands, making a modeling tool more convenient.

To open the **Customize Perspectives**dialog

- From the **Options**menu, choose **Perspectives**and then **Customize**.
- In the **Perspectives**dialog, click the **Customize**button.

There are seven customizable areas in different perspectives:

- Main menu
- Main toolbars
- Diagram toolbars
- Diagram modeling elements toolbar
- Smart manipulators
- Shortcut menu actions
- Create Diagram dialog

Each of these areas has a set of commands, which can be shown in the standard / expert mode or hidden.

To customize the selected area in a predefined perspective

1. In the **Customize Perspectives**dialog, select the perspective and click the **Edit**button near the selected area. The appropriate **Customize**dialog opens.
2. Expand tree sections and select radio buttons beside the items in the tree depending on your choice for **Standard and Expert**, **Expert**only or **Hidden**modes.
3. Click **OK** when you are done.

To switch between Standard/Expert modes, do one of the following

- From the toolbar shortcut menu, select/clear the **Expert Menu Mode**or **Expert Toolbar Mode**check box.
- From the diagram palette shortcut menu, select/clear **Expert Mode**check box.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189145498 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00337: Customizing Possible Owned Elements

- page_id: `189145498`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189145498/Customizing+Possible+Owned+Elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:41:43.903+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide > Domain Specific Language customization > Using Customization Data
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can define situations when elements of particular predefined stereotypes or metaclasses are created inside other elements.For example:

- Block contains the BlockProperty property, instead of a standard property.
- Block owns InternalBlockDiagram , instead of a standard CompositeStructureDiagram .

Use properties listed in the customization element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''], under the **Owned Elements** category, to customize these cases.

###### Customizing Owned Types

Customize the list of owned types in the DSL element shortcut menu (under the **Create Element**command) in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space=''] and other lists. The properties used in customizing owned types are:

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Suggested Owned Types | Specifies the list of stereotypes and metaclasses used when creating inner elements in the DSL element. |
| Hidden Owned Types | Specifies the list of stereotypes and metaclasses not used as inner elements. |

You cannot customize a standard element when you use standard UML (rather than DSL) to create certain stereotyped elements (such as a package). In this case, you must specify possible owners using the **Possible Owners**property from the customization element Specification window. The **Possible Owners** property specifies the list of stereotypes and metaclasses used when creating owned elements.

###### Customizing Owned Diagrams

Customize the list of owned diagrams in the DSL element shortcut menu (under the **Create Diagram**command) in the Model Browser and in other lists. The following are the properties used in customizing owned diagrams:

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Suggested Owned Diagrams | Specifies types of diagrams appearing in the DSL element shortcut menu when creating possible inner diagrams. |
| Hidden Owned Diagrams | Specifies types of diagrams owned by the DSL element. For example, if the property value is Section, then the Section elements are owned by the customized element. |

#### NOTE: Version

Version

Until version 17.0.3, the **Suggested Owned Type**and **Hidden Owned Types**properties did not function if the customization target was a metaclass.

###### Customizing Suggested Relationships

You can customize the list of relationships created directly in the model from the Model Browser, starting from a source or target element.

Customize the relationships list according to the following rules:

- If a DSL relationship has *no*connection rules for restricted end types and the **Hide Metatype**property value specified is *true*, the DSL relationship will be added into all lists where the extended UML type is selected. For example, the «allocation» relationship in SysML model will be used everywhere Dependency is used.
- If a DSL relationship has connection rules, then it will be created only from or to these restricted types.

#### PANEL: On this page

On this page

44

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Customization Data' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Customization Data' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customization Element Properties Description' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="layoutArea"><div class="column"><p><span>You can define situations when elements of particular predefined stereotypes or metaclasses are created inside other elements. </span>For example:</p><ul><li><em>Block</em> contains the <em>BlockProperty</em> property, instead of a standard property.</li><li><em>Block</em> owns <em>InternalBlockDiagram</em>, instead of a standard <em>CompositeStructureDiagram</em>.</li></ul><p>Use properties listed in the customization element <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>, under the <strong>Owned Elements</strong> category, to customize these cases.</p><p><br /></p><h4>Customizing Owned Types</h4><p>Customize the list of owned types in the DSL element shortcut menu (under the <strong>Create Element </strong>command) in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link> and other lists. The properties used in customizing owned types are:</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><thead><tr><th><p>Property</p></th><th><p>Description</p></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><strong>Suggested Owned Types</strong></td><td><p>Specifies the list of stereotypes and metaclasses used when creating inner elements in the DSL element. </p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Hidden Owned Types</strong></td><td><p>Specifies the list of stereotypes and metaclasses <em>not </em>used as inner elements. </p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p>You cannot customize a standard element when you use standard UML (rather than DSL) to create certain stereotyped elements (such as a package). In this case, you must specify possible owners using the <strong>Possible Owners </strong>property from the customization element Specification window. The <strong>Possible Owners</strong> property s<span>pecifies the list of stereotypes and metaclasses used when creating owned elements.</span></p><p><br /></p><h4>Customizing Owned Diagrams</h4><p>Customize the list of owned diagrams in the DSL element shortcut menu (under the <strong>Create Diagram </strong>command) in the Model Browser and in other lists. The following are the properties used in customizing owned diagrams:</p><p><br /></p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 980.0px;"><colgroup><col style="width: 0.0px;" /><col style="width: 0.0px;" /></colgroup><thead><tr><th><p>Property</p></th><th><p>Description</p></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><strong>Suggested Owned Diagrams</strong></td><td><p>Specifies types of diagrams appearing in the DSL element shortcut menu when creating possible inner diagrams. </p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Hidden Owned Diagrams</strong></td><td><p>Specifies types of diagrams owned by the DSL element. For example, if the property value is <em>Section</em>, then the <em>Section</em> elements are owned by the customized element. </p></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3f06f109-1431-47c7-9fb5-e34a71d92748"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Version</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Until version 17.0.3, the <strong>Suggested Owned Type </strong>and <strong>Hidden Owned Types </strong>properties did not function if the customization target was a metaclass.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><h4>Customizing Suggested Relationships</h4><p>You can customize the list of relationships created directly in the model from the Model Browser, starting from a source or target element.</p><p>Customize the relationships list according to the following rules:</p><ul><li><p>If a DSL relationship has <em>no </em>connection rules for restricted end types and the <strong>Hide Metatype </strong>property value specified is <em>true</em>, the DSL relationship will be added into all lists where the extended UML type is selected. For example, the «allocation» relationship in SysML model will be used everywhere Dependency is used.</p></li><li><p>If a DSL relationship has connection rules, then it will be created only from or to these restricted types.</p></li></ul></div></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e6b65c85-becb-4bdd-a2fd-c5d4d9f93f65"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">On this page</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="997334aa-2247-4e35-b1f5-d1af641d3743"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b0ccb40e-6e11-4ccd-baa7-51ecf465051e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating Customization Data" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using Customization Data" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customization Element Properties Description" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189143368 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00338: Customizing severity levels

- page_id: `189143368`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143368/Customizing+severity+levels
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:40:10.282+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Validation > Custom validation > Creating new validation rules > Specifying the validation rule properties
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

You can customize the default severity levels (such as **debug**, **info**, **warning**, **error**, **fatal**) by creating new and and additional levels. For this you must edit the *SeverityKind* [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enumeration' space='']element, located in the UML Standard Profile.

We do not recommend changing the UML Standard Profile.

To customize severity levels

1. 
2. 
3. 
4. 

After you create the custom severity level or modify the default severity, you can select it from from the drop-down list of the **Severity**property when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying the validation rule properties' space='']. In the following figure the new severity level named *critical* can be selected as a value of the **Severity** property in the Specification window of validation rule (Constraint element). The value order in the **Severity** drop-down list is the same as they are listed in the *SeverityKind*Enumerationelement.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The custom severity level named critical in the drop-down list of the Severity property in the validation rule specification.

##### editEditing the UML Standard Profile

To edit the UML Standard Profile

1. Open a project.
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space=''] , click [ATTACHMENT filename='options_button_in_model_browser.png'] and enable to show the Show Auxiliary Resources in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space=''] .
3. Right-click the UML Standard Profile Package.
4. Select Project Usages > Open . [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing project usages' space='']>]]> The UML Standard Profile is now editable.

##### createCreating new or modifying default severity levels

You can create new or modify default severity levels only if the UML Standard Profile is editable. To see how to edit the profile read the .

Each Enumeration Literal created for the Enumeration element corresponds to available severity levels.

To modify default severity levels (Enumeration Literals)

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space=''] , click + to expand the UML Standard Profile Package > Validation Profile Package.
2. Double-click the SeverityKind Enumeration element to open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] .
3. On the left side of the Specification window, select the Enumeration Literals [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] . [ATTACHMENT filename='default_severity_levels.png']
4. modifyOn the right side of the Specification window, select severity level from the list: 
 - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to open [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='']. 
 - Click the **Up**/**Down** button to move the severity level up/down in the list. Severity levels are listed in ascending order according to severity importance. - Click the **Clone** button to create the same severity level. The Specification window of newly created Enumeration Literal opens. Type its unique name. 
 - Click the **Delete**button to remove the selected severity level from the model.
5. Click Close . The default severity levels are modified.

To create a new severity level (Enumeration Literal)

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space=''] , click + to expand the UML Standard Profile Package > Validation Profile Package.
2. Double-click the SeverityKind Enumeration element to open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] .
3. On the left side of the Specification window, select the Enumeration Literals [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] .
4. On the right side of the Specification window, click the Create button. The Specification window of the newly created Enumeration Literal opens.
5. In the Name property, type the name of the new severity level (e.g. critical).
6. Click **Close**. 
The new severity level is created and can be. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### specifyDefining new icon for severity level

You can define a different icon for a custom or default severity levels (Enumeration Literals). For this, you must create a new stereotype and specify its **Icon** and **Metaclass** properties.

To define a new icon for the severity level (Enumeration Literals)

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space=''] , right-click the Validation Profile Package, and select Create Element > Stereotype .
2. Type its name (e.g. criticalIcon) and press Enter .
3. Double-click newly created Stereotype.
4. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] of Stereotype specify the following properties: - Select the Icon property value, click [ATTACHMENT filename='image_library.png'] to select an image from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Image Library tab' space=''] , or [ATTACHMENT filename='custom_image.png'] to select an image from your file system. - Select the Metaclass property value, click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button_in_specification_window.png'] and select EnumerationLiteral. [ATTACHMENT filename='new_icon_for_severity_level.png']
5. Click Close . The new stereotype with a new icon is created and ready to be applied to a severity level. >]]>

##### applyApplying new icon for severity level

After you define the new icon for severity level, you can apply it to custom or default severity levels (Enumeration Literals). This icon is shown when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation' space=''] of your model.

To apply a new icon to a severity level

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space=''] , click + to expand the UML Standard Profile Package > Validation Profile Package > SeverityKind Enumeration element.
2. Double-click the severity level (Enumeration Literals element) to which you want to apply a new icon.
3. In its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] , select the Applied Stereotype property value.
4. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button_in_specification_window.png'] and select the newly created stereotype (e.g. criticalIcon) dedicated for this severity level.
5. Select Apply .
6. Click Close . The new icon for the severity level is applied. In the figure below, the criticalIcon stereotype is applied to the new critical EnumerationLiteral element. [ATTACHMENT filename='new_icon_applied_for_new_severity_level.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e1e990b9-2f5d-4cc0-8da3-c53a9d6877e4"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can customize the default severity levels (such as <strong>debug</strong>, <strong>info</strong>, <strong>warning</strong>, <strong>error</strong>, <strong>fatal</strong>) by creating new and and additional levels. For this you must edit the <em>SeverityKind</em> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Enumeration" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Enumeration ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>element, located in the UML Standard Profile.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2cadc8c8-d89b-4462-89b6-3df3573c91a9"><ac:rich-text-body><p>We do not recommend changing the UML Standard Profile.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To customize severity levels</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link ac:anchor="edit"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Edit the UML Standard Profile.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="create"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create new or modify default severity levels.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><p><ac:link ac:anchor="specify"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Define new icon for severity level.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></li><li><p><ac:link ac:anchor="apply"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Apply new icon for severity level.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></li></ol><p>After you create the custom severity level or modify the default severity, you can select it from from the drop-down list of the <strong>Severity </strong>property when <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specifying the validation rule properties" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[specifying the validation rule properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. In the following figure the new severity level named <em>critical</em> can be selected as a value of the <strong>Severity</strong> property in the Specification window of  validation rule (Constraint element). The value order in the <strong>Severity</strong> drop-down list is the same as they are listed in the <em>SeverityKind</em><span class="confluence-link"> Enumeration </span>element.</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="custom_severity_level.png" /></ac:image><br />The custom severity level named critical in the drop-down list of the Severity property in the validation rule specification.</h6><p><br /></p><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="88d746be-bf68-4add-b19a-efc25d908fbc"><ac:parameter ac:name="">edit</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Editing the UML Standard Profile</h3><p>To edit the UML Standard Profile</p><hr /><ol><li>Open a project.</li><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="22"><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_button_in_model_browser.png" /></ac:image> and enable to show the <strong>Show Auxiliary Resources</strong> in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link>.</li><li>Right-click the <em>UML Standard Profile</em> Package.</li><li>Select <strong>Project Usages</strong> &gt; <strong>Open</strong>. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing project usages" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn how to manage project usages >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br />The UML Standard Profile is now editable.</li></ol><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0f356329-9909-4f1d-a833-6a8370a78627"><ac:parameter ac:name="">create</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Creating new or modifying default severity levels</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f75f0bb8-b7ea-43b4-af1e-e687498fc49a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can create new or modify default severity levels only if the UML Standard Profile is editable. <span class="confluence-link">To see how to edit the profile read</span> the <ac:link ac:anchor="edit"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[procedure above]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Each Enumeration Literal created for the Enumeration element corresponds to available severity levels.</p><p><br /></p><p>To modify default severity levels (Enumeration Literals)</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, click + to expand the <em>UML Standard Profile</em> Package &gt; <em>Validation Profile</em> Package.</li><li class="confluence-link">Double-click the <em>SeverityKind</em><span class="confluence-link"> Enumeration </span>element to open its <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</li><li>On the left side of the Specification window, select the <strong>Enumeration Literals</strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ property group]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="default_severity_levels.png" /></ac:image></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="028719e0-b30e-44b4-b318-2ce340166151"><ac:parameter ac:name="">modify</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>On the right side of the Specification window, select severity level from the list:<br />  - Click <ac:image ac:title="Open Specification" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Open Specification" ac:height="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="open_specification.png" /></ac:image> to open <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.<br />  - Click the <strong>Up</strong>/<strong>Down</strong> button to move the severity level up/down in the list.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1ea85a93-0d00-4548-8d50-7655576bb1f1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Severity levels are listed in ascending order according to severity importance.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">  - Click the <strong>Clone</strong> button to create the same severity level. The Specification window of newly created Enumeration Literal opens. Type its unique name.<br />  - Click the <strong>Delete </strong>button to remove the selected severity level from the model.</p></li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong>.<br />The default severity levels are modified.<br /><br /></li></ol><p>To create a new severity level (Enumeration Literal)</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, click + to expand the <em>UML Standard Profile</em> Package &gt; <em>Validation Profile</em> Package.</li><li class="confluence-link">Double-click the <em>SeverityKind</em><span class="confluence-link"> Enumeration </span>element to open its <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</li><li>On the left side of the Specification window, select the <strong>Enumeration Literals</strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ property group]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>On the right side of the Specification window, click the <strong>Create</strong> button. The Specification window of the newly created Enumeration Literal opens.</li><li>In the <strong>Name</strong> property, type the name of the new severity level (e.g. critical).</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <strong>Close</strong>.<br />The new severity level is created and can be<ac:link ac:anchor="modify"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ modified by using buttons]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="custom_severity_level_in_the_specification_of_enumeration_SeverityKind.png" /></ac:image></p></li></ol><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e13b5d38-57f0-462a-8f5b-5a272aa7c4ff"><ac:parameter ac:name="">specify</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Defining new icon for severity level</h3><p>You can define a different icon for a custom or default severity levels (Enumeration Literals). For this, you must create a new stereotype and specify its <strong>Icon</strong> and <strong>Metaclass</strong> properties.</p><p>To define a new icon for the severity level (Enumeration Literals)</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, right-click the<em> Validation Profile</em> Package, and select <strong>Create Element</strong> &gt; <strong>Stereotype</strong>.</li><li>Type its name (e.g. criticalIcon) and press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li><li class="confluence-link">Double-click newly created Stereotype.</li><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link> of Stereotype specify the following properties:<br />  - Select the <strong>Icon</strong> property value, click <ac:image ac:title="Image Library" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Image Library" ac:height="11"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image_library.png" /></ac:image> to select an image from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Image Library tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Image Library]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, or <ac:image ac:title="Custom Image" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Custom Image" ac:height="11"><ri:attachment ri:filename="custom_image.png" /></ac:image> to select an image from your file system.<br />  - Select the <strong>Metaclass</strong> property value, click <ac:image ac:title="Edit" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Edit" ac:height="17"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_button_in_specification_window.png" /></ac:image> and select EnumerationLiteral.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_icon_for_severity_level.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong>.<br />The new stereotype with a new icon is created and ready to be applied to a severity level. <ac:link ac:anchor="apply"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to apply it >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ol><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aae655a1-0d9a-4276-9e43-3e86888e4b9c"><ac:parameter ac:name="">apply</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Applying new icon for severity level</h3><p>After you define the new icon for severity level, you can apply it to custom or default severity levels (Enumeration Literals). This icon is shown when <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Validation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[representing invalid elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of your model.</p><p>To apply a new icon to a severity level</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, click + to expand the <em>UML Standard Profile</em> Package &gt; <em>Validation Profile</em> Package &gt; <em>SeverityKind</em><span class="confluence-link"> Enumeration </span>element.</li><li class="confluence-link">Double-click the <span class="confluence-link">severity level (Enumeration Literals element) to </span>which you want to apply a new icon.</li><li class="confluence-link">In its <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>, select the <strong>Applied Stereotype</strong> property value.</li><li class="confluence-link">Click <ac:image ac:title="Edit" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Edit" ac:height="17"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_button_in_specification_window.png" /></ac:image> and select the newly created stereotype (e.g. criticalIcon) dedicated for this severity level.</li><li class="confluence-link">Select <strong>Apply</strong>.</li><li class="confluence-link">Click <strong>Close</strong>.<br />The new icon for the severity level is applied. In the figure below, the <em>criticalIcon</em> stereotype is applied to the new <em>critical</em> EnumerationLiteral element.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_icon_applied_for_new_severity_level.png" /></ac:image></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189145454 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00339: Customizing Specification Window

- page_id: `189145454`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189145454/Customizing+Specification+Window
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:41:41.601+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide > Domain Specific Language customization > Using Customization Data
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The main purpose of DSL is to hide UML, so that DSL elements would look standard or as new type elements in models. DSL element properties are specified in the DSL element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] and the **Properties** panel (at the bottom of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='']) as regular properties.

There are several customizations and grouping cases for properties:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The main purpose of DSL is to hide UML, so that DSL elements would look standard or as new type elements in models. </span> <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">DSL element properties are specified in the DSL element <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link> and the <strong>Properties</strong> panel (at the bottom of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model Browser" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Browser window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) as regular properties. </span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">There are several customizations and grouping cases for properties:</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> <ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="d368bd14-56cc-4bf1-9843-3d76bc07dfd3" /> <br /></span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189142113 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00340: Customizing stereotype style properties

- page_id: `189142113`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189142113/Customizing+stereotype+style+properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:38:51.839+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Formatting symbols > Stereotype properties
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can customize properties like colors, line styles, and line width, for your stereotypes. To be able to do it, you need to:

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype properties' space='']
2. Once the stereotype is added, customize stereotype style properties either via the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='']** or **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='']** dialog.

In the**Project Options** dialog, after specifying the stereotype style properties, click**Apply** to apply changes to existing shapes or the **OK** button to apply changes to the new shapes only.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Customizing stereotype style properties using the Project Options dialog

In the **Symbol Properties** dialog, click**Make Default** after setting the desired style properties for your stereotype.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Customizing stereotype style properties using the Symbol Properties dialog

The best practice is to create styles for stereotypes in the Profile, loading it as a project. When a project uses the Profile, styles for stereotypes will also be loaded and used. These styles will be applied right after applying the stereotype to an element and will be used in all diagrams.

Style for stereotype in custom diagram

- The custom diagram wizard allows specifying styles for stereotyped elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can customize properties like colors, line styles, and line width, for your stereotypes. To be able to do it, you need to:</p><ol><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Adding stereotype"><ri:page ri:content-title="Stereotype properties" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add a stereotype to the Stereotypes option group.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><p class="_mce_tagged_br">Once the stereotype is added, customize stereotype style properties either via the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Setting project options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Project options]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> or <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Symbol Properties dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog.</p></li></ol><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4ec8a5cf-e0da-4f76-b655-ead647229c1b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>In the<strong> Project Options</strong> dialog, after specifying the stereotype style properties, click<strong> Apply</strong> to apply changes to existing shapes or the <strong>OK</strong> button to apply changes to the new shapes only. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="customizing_stereotype_style_properties.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Customizing stereotype style properties using the Project Options dialog</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="82668d2b-6f62-47f6-8cd2-ff570c20f015"><ac:rich-text-body><p>In the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog, click<strong> Make Default</strong> after setting the desired style properties for your stereotype.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="symbol_properties_stereotyped_relationship.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Customizing stereotype style properties using the Symbol Properties dialog</h6><p>The best practice is to create styles for stereotypes in the Profile, loading it as a project. When a project uses the Profile, styles for stereotypes will also be loaded and used. These styles will be applied right after applying the stereotype to an element and will be used in all diagrams.</p><p><br /></p><p>Style for stereotype in custom diagram</p><hr /><ul><li>The custom diagram wizard allows specifying styles for stereotyped elements.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189143449 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00341: Customizing suspect links tracking

- page_id: `189143449`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143449/Customizing+suspect+links+tracking
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:40:13.263+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Suspect Links
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1398447377

1398447388

1398447375

**On this page**

**53**

1398447378

In addition to tracking the changes directly affecting Requirements linked to other model elements, you can create your own custom suspect link configurations to keep track of the changes applied to selected model elements and their properties at the chosen end (source or target) of the specified dependencies.

A newly created suspect links configuration comes into effect immediately and overrides a configuration previously used in the project.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Suspect links tracking in the model

##### Customizing tracking

To create a custom suspect link configuration

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space=''] , right-click a package to create your configuration in, and select Create Element > Class . Name it.
2. Right-click the configuration [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space=''], select**Stereotype,**and apply the«SuspectLinkConfiguration»stereotype. To apply the «SuspectLinkConfiguration» stereotype, suspect links tracking must be [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tracking elements affected by Requirement changes' space=''] in your project (**Analyze** > **Suspect Links** > **Enable Tracking**).If you use Cameo Systems Modeler, create a Block instead of a Class in step 1, remove the «Block» stereotype from the Block, then apply the«SuspectLinkConfiguration»stereotype to that Block.
3. Open the created element's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] .
4. Specify the following :
  - Tracked Dependency Type
  - Tracked End
  - Tracked Element Type
  - Tracked Element Properties
5. Close the window when you are done and press F5 to refresh your project. 
The configuration is created. Please note that it is not automatically applied to relationships that existed in the model prior to creating a configuration for suspect links tracking. In such a case, you need to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tracking elements affected by Requirement changes' space=''] links manually.

##### Suspect Links configuration propertiesConfiguration properties

These are the propertiesyou need to specify for your custom suspect linkconfiguration:

| Property name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Tracked Dependency Type | Specify the types of dependencies to keep track of their source/target element changes. |
| Tracked End | Specify whether the source or the target element of the selected dependencies should be tracked. The target end-related changes are tracked by default. |
| Tracked Element Type | Specify the element types to keep track of their changes. |
| Tracked Element Properties | Specify the properties of the selected model elements to keep track of their changes. If left empty, all element properties are tracked by default. |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189145506 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00342: Customizing Symbols

- page_id: `189145506`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189145506/Customizing+Symbols
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:46:15.222+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide > Domain Specific Language customization > Using Customization Data
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Setting default symbol size

The **Default Shape Size** property defines the default size of a shape when you create a symbol in a diagram. Use the **Default Shape Size**property in the Customization element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] to define the default symbol size in DSL customization:

- The first value is the width (x) in pixels. Value= 0 is used if a default symbol width should stay, but the height should change.
- The second value is a height (y) in pixels.

A default shape size is used everywhere a new symbol is created - using [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing Drag-and-Drop' space=''], diagram toolbar button, diagram wizards, etc.

###### Setting custom path style

Customize the custom path style by changing:

- Line style (dashes, dots, solid line, etc).
- Arrow style at the first end and arrow style at the second end.
- Custom scalable icon for the first end and for the second style.

To create the custom path style

1. In a profile, create a stereotype.
2. Right-click either the stereotype or a created stereotype (usually created in the profile diagram). Select Metaclass . In the Select Metaclass dialog, clear all selections. Select the Relationships metaclass.
3. Customize the path style. In the stereotype Specification window, click the Icon property and then click the [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] button.
4. Click Yes to open the Path Icon Customization dialog. [ATTACHMENT filename='path_icon_customization_dialog.png']
5. Specify the path style.
6. Click OK when you are done.
7. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space=''] to a relationship.

#### INFO: Symbol Properties

Symbol Properties

Change the line width and the rest of the path properties in the [**Symbol Properties**dialog](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Symbol+Properties+dialog).

Path icon usage rules:

- Custom appearance is used only after applying the stereotype.
- End icons are rotated according to path position and direction.
- The end icon size is scaled according to font size (as with standard arrows).

#### PANEL: On this page

On this page

44

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customization Element Properties Description' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='']

Use the <strong>Default Shape Size </strong>property in the Customization element <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b0df89a0-346e-4c82-9599-d370b96840e2"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link> to define the default symbol size in DSL customization</ac:inline-comment-marker>:</p><p><br /></p><ul><li>The first value is the width (x) in pixels.
