<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189139748 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00343: Customizing the conversion options

- page_id: `189139748`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189139748/Customizing+the+conversion+options
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:24.031+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Importing data from other UML tools and formats > Enterprise Architect Import > Working with Enterprise Architect Import
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In addition to the options that you have in the **Import Enterprise Architect File**dialog, several other conversion options exist in the modeling tool, and you can specify it in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='']**Environment Options**dialog.

To customize Enterprise Architect Import options in the **Environment Options**dialog

1. On the main menu, click Options > Environment to open the Environment Options dialog.
2. In the options group list, select Enterprise Architect Import . [ATTACHMENT filename='Environment Options.png']
3. The transformation options are classified into two groups: General and Activity element mapping :
  - Options in the General group are the same as those in the Import Enterprise Architect Files dialog.
  - The Activity element mapping group provides options to convert the EA elements to other element types as it is described in the following table:

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Convert EA WriteVariableAction to | To convert EA WriteVariableAction to either AddVariableValueAction or RemoveVariableValueAction. |
| Convert EA WriteLinkAction to | To convert EA WriteLinkAction to either CreateLinkAction or DestroyLinkAction. |
| Convert EA WriteStructuralFeatureAction to | To convert EA WriteStructuralFeatureAction to either AddStructuralFeatureValueAction or RemoveStructuralFeatureValueAction. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>In addition to the options that you have in the <strong>Import Enterprise Architect File </strong>dialog, several other conversion options exist in the modeling tool, and you can specify it in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customizing environment options" /><ac:link-body><strong>Environment Options </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><br /></p><p>To customize Enterprise Architect Import options in the <strong>Environment Options </strong>dialog</p><hr /><ol><li>On the main menu, click <strong>Options </strong>&gt; <strong>Environment </strong>to open the <strong>Environment </strong><strong>Options </strong>dialog.</li><li>In the options group list, select <strong>Enterprise Architect Import</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Environment Options.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>The transformation options are classified into two groups: <strong>General</strong> and <strong>Activity element mapping</strong>:<br /><ul><li>Options in the <strong>General </strong>group are the same as those in the <strong>Import Enterprise Architect Files </strong>dialog.</li><li>The <strong>Activity element mapping</strong> group provides options to convert the EA elements to other element types as it is described in the following table:<br /><br /></li></ul></li></ol><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Option</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Convert EA WriteVariableAction to</strong></p></td><td><p>To convert EA WriteVariableAction to either AddVariableValueAction or RemoveVariableValueAction.</p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Convert EA WriteLinkAction to</strong></td><td><p>To convert EA WriteLinkAction to either CreateLinkAction or DestroyLinkAction.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Convert EA WriteStructuralFeatureAction to</strong></p></td><td><p>To convert EA WriteStructuralFeatureAction to either AddStructuralFeatureValueAction or RemoveStructuralFeatureValueAction.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189140377 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00344: Cutting element names

- page_id: `189140377`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189140377/Cutting+element+names
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:55.309+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Analysis diagrams > Relation Map
- labels: ['relation-map-cut-element-name']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The element names can be cut if they are too long, which makes the relation map difficult to read.

To cut element names

1. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='options_button.png'] in the Diagram toolbar.
2. Select the Cut Element Names option.

To see the entire name of an element, move the pointer over the element name. A tooltip showing the entire name appears.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The element names can be cut if they are too long, which makes the relation map difficult to read.</p><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b0a93e26-c193-4133-bace-ef02f45419b9">To cut element names</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><hr /><ol><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Options" ac:alt="Options"><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the Diagram toolbar.</li><li>Select the <strong>Cut Element Names </strong>option.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f601b47d-de99-4300-843a-0078e572e13f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To see the entire name of an element, move the pointer over the element name. A tooltip showing the entire name appears.</p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="cut_element_names.png" /></ac:image></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 00345: Data file

- page_id: `189144583`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189144583/Data+file
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:41:01.457+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Report Wizard template editor
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Template Editor allows you to create a new data file and import it to the Report Wizard Template Editor dialog. Click the Import button to import the data file. The data file is written in the *XML* format. The following is the example of an *XML* data file schema, see the code snippet below.

```text
]]>
```

The table below lists the XML Data File Schema Elements and Their Descriptions

| Element | Attribute / Element | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| data |  | A root element. |
| name : string | A data name. This value will be used as a category name in (3) Category. |  |
| elements : complexType | A group of <element> elements. |  |
| elements |  | A group of <element> elements. |
| element |  | An element display on (4) List of fields. |
|  | name : string | An element’s name. |
|  | description : string | An element description. |
|  | often : boolean | If an attribute is true, the element will be managed as a suggested element. |
|  | members : complexType | A group of <function> or <attribute>. |
| function |  | A function member. |
|  | name : string | A function’s name. |
|  | description : string | A function description. |
|  | param : complexType | A group of function parameters. |
| param |  | A function parameter. |
|  | name : string | A parameter’s name. |
|  | description : string | A parameter description. |
|  | type : string | A parameter type. |
|  | direction : simpleType | The direction of a parameter:in indicates this parameter is an input.outindicates this parameter is an output. |
| attribute |  | An attribute member. |
|  | name : string | An attribute’s name. |
|  | description : string | An attribute description. |
|  | type : string | An attribute type. |

The following code snippet is an example of an XML data file.

```text
array
      Use for creating the Array or HashSet instance.
      
        
          subList
          Create ArrayList of the portion of list in given size.
          
            list
            in
            List
            a original list.
          
          
            size
            in
            int
            view size of given list
          
          
            list
            out
            
            a of view of the specified size within given list.
          
        
      
    
  
]]>
```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Template Editor allows you to create a new data file and import it to the Report Wizard Template Editor dialog. Click the Import button to import the data file. The data file is written in the <em>XML</em> format. The following is the example of an <em>XML</em> data file schema, see the code snippet below.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dfb09981-ad79-417e-a30b-ebf4dc100ed4"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<xs:schema elementFormDefault="qualified" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema">
  <xs:element name="data">
   <xs:complexType>
    <xs:sequence>
     <xs:element minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1" name="elements">
      <xs:complexType>
       <xs:sequence>
        <xs:element minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded" name="element">
         <xs:complexType>
          <xs:all>
           <xs:element minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1" name="name" type="xs:string" />
           <xs:element minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1" name="description" type="xs:string" />
           <xs:element minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1" name="members">
            <xs:complexType>
             <xs:sequence>
             <xs:element minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded" name="function">
              <xs:complexType>
               <xs:sequence>
                <xs:element minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1" name="name" type="xs:string" />
                <xs:element minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1" name="description" type="xs:string" />
                <xs:element minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded" name="param">
                 <xs:complexType>
                  <xs:all>
                   <xs:element minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1" name="name" type="xs:string" />
                   <xs:element minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1" name="description" type="xs:string" />
                   <xs:element minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1" name="type" nillable="true" type="xs:string" />
                   <xs:element minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1" name="direction">
                    <xs:simpleType>
                     <xs:restriction base="xs:string">
                      <xs:enumeration value="in" />
                      <xs:enumeration value="out" />
                     </xs:restriction>
                    </xs:simpleType>
                   </xs:element>
                  </xs:all>
                 </xs:complexType>
                </xs:element>
               </xs:sequence>
              </xs:complexType>
             </xs:element>
              <xs:element minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded" name="attribute">
               <xs:complexType>
                <xs:all>
                 <xs:element minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1" name="name" type="xs:string" />
                 <xs:element minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1" name="description" type="xs:string" />
                 <xs:element minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1" name="type" nillable="true" type="xs:string" />
                </xs:all>
               </xs:complexType>
              </xs:element>
             </xs:sequence>
            </xs:complexType>
           </xs:element>
          </xs:all>
          <xs:attribute name="often" type="xs:boolean" />
         </xs:complexType>
        </xs:element>
       </xs:sequence>
      </xs:complexType>
     </xs:element>
   </xs:sequence>
   <xs:attribute name="name" type="xs:string" />
  </xs:complexType>
 </xs:element>
</xs:schema>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The table below lists the XML Data File Schema Elements and Their Descriptions</p><table><tbody><tr><th>Element </th><th>Attribute / Element </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="3"> data  </td><td> </td><td>A root element.</td></tr><tr><td>name : string</td><td>A data name. This <br />value will be used as <br />a category name in (3) <br />Category. </td></tr><tr><td>elements : complexType</td><td>A group of &lt;element&gt; <br />elements. </td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">elements</td><td colspan="1"> </td><td colspan="1">A group of &lt;element&gt; <br />elements.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">element</td><td colspan="1"> </td><td colspan="1">An element display on (4) <br />List of fields.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"> </td><td colspan="1">name : string</td><td colspan="1">An element’s name.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"> </td><td colspan="1">description : string</td><td colspan="1">An element description.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"> </td><td colspan="1">often : boolean</td><td colspan="1">If an attribute is true, the <br />element will be managed <br />as a suggested element.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"> </td><td colspan="1">members : complexType</td><td colspan="1">A group of &lt;function&gt; or <br />&lt;attribute&gt;.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">function</td><td colspan="1"> </td><td colspan="1">A function member.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"> </td><td colspan="1">name : string</td><td colspan="1">A function’s name.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"> </td><td colspan="1">description : string</td><td colspan="1">A function description.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"> </td><td colspan="1">param : complexType</td><td colspan="1">A group of function <br />parameters.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">param</td><td colspan="1"> </td><td colspan="1">A function parameter.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"> </td><td colspan="1">name : string</td><td colspan="1">A parameter’s name.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"> </td><td colspan="1">description : string</td><td colspan="1">A parameter description.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"> </td><td colspan="1">type : string</td><td colspan="1">A parameter type.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"> </td><td colspan="1">direction : simpleType</td><td colspan="1"><p>The direction of a parameter:</p><ul><li>in <br />indicates this parameter is an input.</li><li>out<br />indicates this parameter is an output.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">attribute</td><td colspan="1"> </td><td colspan="1">An attribute member.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"> </td><td colspan="1">name : string</td><td colspan="1">An attribute’s name.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"> </td><td colspan="1">description : string</td><td colspan="1">An attribute description.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"> </td><td colspan="1">type : string</td><td colspan="1">An attribute type.</td></tr></tbody></table><p> <br /> The following code snippet is an example of an XML data file.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4294de46-0f48-44ed-a51b-cb9564e16a84"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<data name="Built-in Tools" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchemainstance"
xsi:noNamespaceSchemaLocation="fields.xsd">
  <elements>
    <element often="true">
      <name>array</name>
      <description>Use for creating the Array or HashSet instance.</description>
      <members>
        <function>
          <name>subList</name>
          <description>Create ArrayList of the portion of list in given size.</description>
          <param>
            <name>list</name>
            <direction>in</direction>
            <type>List</type>
            <description>a original list.</description>
          </param>
          <param>
            <name>size</name>
            <direction>in</direction>
            <type>int</type>
            <description>view size of given list</description>
          </param>
          <param>
            <name>list</name>
            <direction>out</direction>
            <type/>
            <description>a of view of the specified size within given list.</description>
          </param>
        </function>
      </members>
    </element>
  </elements>
</data>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 00346: Data Store Node

- page_id: `189141634`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141634/Data+Store+Node
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:45:28.712+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Node
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Data Store Node keeps all tokens that enter it, copying them when they are chosen to be moved downstream. Incoming tokens containing a particular object replace any tokens in the object node that contains the particular object.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create a Data Store Node

1. Expand the Activity diagram palette, click the downward arrow button next to the Object Node icon and select **Data Store Node** from the shortcut menu . [IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Click on the Activity diagram pane. The Data Store Node is created.
3. (Optional) To specify the Data Store Node by changing its property values, right-click its symbol and open its Specification window .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A Data Store Node keeps all tokens that enter it, copying them when they are chosen to be moved downstream. Incoming tokens containing a particular object replace any tokens in the object node that contains the particular object.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Data_store_node.png" /></ac:image></p><p>To create a Data Store Node </p><hr /><ol><li>Expand the Activity diagram palette, click the downward arrow button next to the <strong>Object Node</strong><strong><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span></strong><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">icon and select <strong>Data Store Node</strong> from the shortcut menu</span>.<br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_data_store_node.png" /></ac:image></span></li><li>Click on the Activity diagram pane. The Data Store Node<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span>is created. </li><li>(Optional) <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To specify the Data Store Node by changing its property values, right-click its symbol and open its </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Specification+window">Specification window</a><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. </span></li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141406 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00347: Data Type

- page_id: `189141406`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141406/Data+Type
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:38:10.546+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Data Type is a type whose values have no identity; that is, they are pure values. It is a classifier and inherits the general features of the classifier: visibility, generalizable element properties, and operations.

The modeling tool provides the following predefined data types: boolean, byte, char, date, double, float, int, Integer, Real, long, short, void, and String.

You can also create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enumeration' space='']or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Primitive' space='']Data Types.

To create a new Data Type, including Enumeration or Primitive

- From the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space=''] , select New from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space=''] or Create Element from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space=''] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Subsystem' space=''] , or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model' space=''] shortcut menu, and then select Data Type, Enumeration, or Primitive.
- In the Class, Package, Subsystem, and Model [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] , the Inner Elements property group, click Create and select a Data Type.

A Data Type can be specified by changing its property values in the Data Type [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='']. Each property is described in the description area on this window.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='']

Each property is described in the description area on this window.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a3eecbb8-7b24-4e78-a0fb-5c818aadc291"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="d73cd36e-f4ee-4087-888b-57f1c9aed597"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1b2025c5-70c1-47b6-9a1b-4fb242548002"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Class diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Class diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Use Case" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Use Case diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

## PAGE 00348: Data Type From Old Project Versions

- page_id: `189145795`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189145795/Data+Type+From+Old+Project+Versions
- version_number: 1

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1398641584

1398641587

1398641586

##### bool

There are five cases of using the bool data type: Attribute type, Parameter type, Return type, Actual in Template Parameter Substitution and Default in Classifier Template Parameter. Our modeling tool will change the old bool data type to new bool data type in a new profile.

**Attribute type**

The old bool data type in an Attribute type is shown in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Old bool data type in old profile (C++ Profile).

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Change to the new bool data type in new profile (c++ ANSI profile).

**Parameter type**

The old and new bool data types in the Parameter type are shown in the figures below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Old bool data type in old profile (C++ Profile)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Change to the new bool data type in new profile (c++ ANSI profile).

**Return type**

The old bool data type in the Return type is shown in the figure below.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Old bool data type in old profile (C++ Profile).

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Change to the new bool data type in new profile (c++ ANSI profile).

**Actual in Template Parameter Substitution**

The old bool data type in the Actual in Template Parameter Substitution is shown in the figure below.

| Old value | Translation |
| --- | --- |
| Old bool data type in old profile (C++ Profile). | Change to the new bool data type in new profile (c++ ANSI profile). |

**Default in Classifier Template Parameter**

The old bool data type in the Default in Classifier Template Parameter is shown in the figure below.

| Old value | Translation |
| --- | --- |
| Old bool data type in old profile (C++ Profile). | Change to the new bool data type in new profile (c++ ANSI profile). |

/></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189144539 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00349: Date tool

- page_id: `189144539`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189144539/Date+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:40:55.222+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Helper tools
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

2049623106

2049623118

2049623105

**On this page**

43

2049623108

$date is a method to display and format the current date and time.

###### **$date**

Display the current date and time, for example, Oct 19, 2003 and 9:54:50 PM respectively.

###### **$date.long**

Display the current date and time in a long form, for example, October 19, 2003 and 9:54:50 PM. respectively.

###### **$date.full_date**

Display the current date in a full form, for example, Sunday, October 19, 2003.

###### **$date.get('format')**

Display the current date in a specific format, for example, $date.get('yyyy-M-d H:m:s') is displayed as 2003-10-19 21:54:50.

| Letter | Date or Time Component | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| G | Era designator | AD |
| y | Year 1996 | 96 |
| M | Month in year | July; Jul; 07 |
| w | Week in year | 27 |
| W | Week in month | 2 |
| D | Day in year | 189 |
| d | Day in month | 10 |
| F | Day of week in month | 2 |
| E | Day in week | Tuesday; Tue |
| a | Am/pm marker | PM |
| H | Hour in day (0-23) | 0 |
| k | Hour in day (1-24) | 24 |
| K | Hour in am/pm (0-11) | 0 |
| h | Hour in am/pm (1-12) | 12 |
| m | Minute in hour | 30 |
| s | Second in minute | 55 |
| S | Millisecond | 978 |
| z | Time zone | Pacific Standard Time; PST; GMT-08.00 |
| Z | Time zone | Time zone -0800 |

###### **Year**

- If the number of letters in a pattern letters is four or more, a calendar specific long form is used.
- Otherwise, a calendar specific short or abbreviated form is used.

| Date and Time Pattern | Result |
| --- | --- |
| yy | 10 |
| yyyy | 2010 |

###### **Month**

- If the number of letters in a pattern is less than 3, the number form is used.
- If the number of letters in a pattern is 3, a short or abbreviated form is used.
- If the number of letters in a pattern is 4 or more, the full form is used.

| Date and Time Pattern | Result |
| --- | --- |
| M | 4 |
| MM | 04 |
| MMM | Apr |
| MMMM | April |

###### **Days**

- If the number of letters in a pattern is four or more, the full form is used.
- Otherwise, a calendar specific short or abbreviated form is used.

| Date and Time Pattern | Result |
| --- | --- |
| E | Mon |
| EEEE |  |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189144890 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00350: Debug report template

- page_id: `189144890`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189144890/Debug+report+template
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:41:16.118+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1336400640

1336400641

1336400641

The Debug report template features warning messages that describe template errors. Warning messages appear in a Message dialog or in the terminal while the report template is being processed in UI or command line. Warning messages are intended only for the informative purpose and have no effect on output reports.

The warning message types are as follows:

- Invalid Property
- Invalid Reference
- Invalid Method Reference
- Exception
- Invalid Syntax

1336400640

**Related pages**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141409 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00351: Decision and Merge

- page_id: `189141409`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141409/Decision+and+Merge
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:45:25.759+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: ['decision', 'merge']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

943280858

943280861

943280859

A Decision Node in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space=''] is used much like a choice or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Junction' space=''] point in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='']. Decision nodes allow you to separate the Activity Edges. Merge nodes allow you to combine the Activity Edges together.

Decision Nodes are created using guard conditions. They help protect Activity Edges that depend on guard conditions.

1. The symbol used for the Decision Node is a large diamond shape. It can have one or two incoming Activity Edges, and at least one outgoing Activity Edge
2. The symbol used for the Merge Node is a large diamond shape. It can have exactly one outgoing Activity Edge but may have multiple incoming Activity Edges.

The Decision and Merge are represented by the same symbol. Although, you can set the **Use different Fork/Join and Decision/Merge****notations** [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project options' space=''] to draw different notations for the**Decision**and**Merge**.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Activity diagram illustrates that when an order cancellation request is made (only while filling, receiving or shipping orders) - the Cancel Order behavior is invoked.

943280856

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction Overview diagram' space='']
- Activity diagram
- Junction
- Model Elements

Decision nodes allow you to separate the Activity Edges. Merge nodes allow you to combine the Activity Edges together. </p><p>Decision Nodes are created using guard conditions. They help protect Activity Edges that depend on guard conditions.</p><ol><li>The symbol used for the Decision Node is a large diamond shape. It can have one or two incoming Activity Edges, and at least one outgoing Activity Edge</li><li>The symbol used for the Merge Node is a large diamond shape. It can have exactly one outgoing Activity Edge but may have multiple incoming Activity Edges.</li></ol><p>The Decision and Merge are represented by the same symbol. Although, you can set the <strong>Use different Fork/Join and Decision/Merge</strong><span><strong> notations</strong> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Project options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[project option]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to draw different notations for the </span><strong>Decision</strong><span> and </span><strong>Merge</strong><span>.</span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="decision-merge.png" /></ac:image></h6><h6 style="text-align: center;">Activity diagram illustrates that when an order cancellation request is made (only while filling, receiving or shipping orders) - the Cancel Order behavior is invoked.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="874d329f-6bcc-4364-b901-b6155101dd17"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">943280856</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Interaction Overview diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Activity+diagram">Activity diagram</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Junction">Junction</a></li><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Model+elements">Model Elements</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189139981 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00352: Decomposing model

- page_id: `189139981`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189139981/Decomposing+model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:36.893+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Project partitioning
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

305246055

305246057

305246056

Model decomposition has a package level granularity. Smaller elements cannot be split into separate projects. Basically, each project package could be partitioned into separate projects; however, this is excessive.

The decision on how to split a model into parts should be made carefully. You should isolate model parts that form logically complete pieces of a structure (e.g., a subsystem, code library, or profile) and are not very dependent on each other.

When there are many one-way dependencies to a model part (e.g., parts **A**, **B**, **C** depend on a part **D**, but part **D** does not depend on any of the parts **A**, **B**, **C**), this part is a good candidate for placement into a separate project.

When one big project is used to store all modeling information (e.g., use case models, high level architectural models, detailed implementation level class, sequence, state, and other information), it may be useful to partition the models according to the modeling domains (i.e., use cases in one used project, architectural models in another, implementation level models in yet another). This allows unloading unnecessary used projects while working on one part (and improving the performance), while retaining the relationships between domains and loading used projects on demand.

Avoid decomposing a model into parts which have circular dependencies. That is, A ↔ B or A → B → C → A situations.

Usually programmers are very adept at splitting large code bases into libraries. The very same criteria should be applied for splitting large models into separate projects.

The project decomposition functionality allows two important options:

- Working without all used projects loaded.
- Using the project in the read-write accessibility mode.

Used projects are often exploited for storing profiles. However, a used project is not a profile, and it is important not to mix them. Any model part can be stored in a separate project.

305246054

**Related pages**

****

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Smaller elements cannot be split into separate projects. Basically, each project package could be partitioned into separate projects; however, this is excessive.</p><p>The decision on how to split a model into parts should be made carefully. You should isolate model parts that form logically complete pieces of a structure (e.g., a subsystem, code library, or profile) and are not very dependent on each other.</p><p>When there are many one-way dependencies to a model part (e.g., parts <strong>A</strong>, <strong>B</strong>, <strong>C</strong> depend on a part <strong>D</strong>, but part <strong>D</strong> does not depend on any of the parts <strong>A</strong>, <strong>B</strong>, <strong>C</strong>), this part is a good candidate for placement into a separate project.</p><p>When one big project is used to store all modeling information (e.g., use case models, high level architectural models, detailed implementation level class, sequence, state, and other information), it may be useful to partition the models according to the modeling domains (i.e., use cases in one used project, architectural models in another, implementation level models in yet another). This allows unloading unnecessary used projects while working on one part (and improving the performance), while retaining the relationships between domains and loading used projects on demand.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b77ac731-7a31-4bfe-9436-2348fb7622de"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Avoid decomposing a model into parts which have circular dependencies. That is, A ↔ B or A → B <span>→</span> C <span>→</span> A situations.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Usually programmers are very adept at splitting large code bases into libraries. The very same criteria should be applied for splitting large models into separate projects.</p><p>The project decomposition functionality allows two important options:</p><ul><li>Working without all used projects loaded.</li><li>Using the project in the read-write accessibility mode.</li></ul><p>Used projects are often exploited for storing profiles. However, a used project is not a profile, and it is important not to mix them. Any model part can be stored in a separate project.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ae3e38bf-16af-4488-bb2f-d7bc238ddeeb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">305246054</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="26a99b5b-7c20-44f1-8461-6c24f9ac88eb" /><br /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141747 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00353: Deep History

- page_id: `189141747`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141747/Deep+History
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:38:28.179+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > State Machine > Pseudo States
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Deep History represents the most recent active configuration of the composite state that directly contains this pseudo state; e.g. the state configuration that was active when the composite state was last exited. A composite state can have at most one deep history vertex.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='']

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Pseudo States' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

A composite state can have at most one deep history vertex.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36e20852-22dd-420b-8d4b-52b136b22981"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification Window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f01b84d9-d9d1-4049-8315-15a6a05d850d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="7e19d119-2f65-4f2a-b944-60c34f7c4ba8"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Pseudo States" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141980 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00354: Default property values

- page_id: `189141980`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141980/Default+property+values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:38:43.982+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

60307427

60307430

60316126

**On this page**

60307429

The modeling tool allows you to define the initial (default) properties for elements.

The Default element properties can be defined for:

- The whole project
- The specific diagram

To set the default properties for the whole project

1. From the Options menu, select Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. Expand Default model properties . Select the desired elements and change their property values.

After creating a new element it will have new property values. Values for previously created elements will not be changed.

To reset element properties to the default value

- In he Project Options dialog, Default model properties group, click the Reset to Defaults button.

Exception: interface attribute default visibility will always be #public, no matter what your settings are.

To set default properties for the specific diagram

1. From the Diagrams menu, select Customize . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing diagrams' space=''] dialog opens.
2. Select the diagram type you want to customize.
3. Click the Edit button. The Customize Diagram Wizard opens.
4. In the fourth step Specify toolbar buttons , click the Add button and select New Button . The Edit Button dialog opens.
5. In the **General** > **Model Element Type** drop-down list, select the element, which default properties you want to customize.
6. In the **Edit Button** dialog, open the **Element Properties** tab.
7. Select the **Specify own values** radio button. Now change the default property values of the chosen element.

Create a new element from the customized diagram toolbar and the element default properties will be the same as you had defined.

##### Sharing the default property values

You can share the default properties with other users for their new project. After changing the property values in the **Project Options** dialog, create a project template.

To create a template

1. From the File menu select Export > Template
2. Enter the template name and description.
3. Click OK . A template will be created in <moleding tool installation directory>\ templates .

To import a template

1. From the File menu, select New Project . The New Project dialog opens.
2. From the Other domain, select the Project from Template icon.
3. In the Select template tree, choose your template. The project options are imported to the project together with the template.

60307425

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189142165 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00355: Default settings of hyperlinks to model elements

- page_id: `189142165`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189142165/Default+settings+of+hyperlinks+to+model+elements
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:45:38.734+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Manage Navigation > Defining hyperlinks > Hyperlinks in texts
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

61862155

61862158

61862156

When adding hyperlinks in text to refer to model elements, the values of **Text to display box**is **Name** and **Update Mode**is **Automatically updated** are selected by default. You can change the default settings in the **Project Options** dialog.

To change the default settings of a hyperlink to model element

1. From the main menu, select Options > Project .
2. In the **Project Options** dialog , expand the General property group in the tree.
3. Select Element References .
4. Select the option for the value you want to change:
  - Select one of the Default Element Hyperlink Display Mode option values: - **Name**(Default). Displays the element name from its Specification window. 
- **Representation text.** Displays the full element title visible in the Containment tree. 
- **Icon+Representation text*.*** Displays the element icon together with the full title from the Containment tree. 
- **Custom text**. Allows you to type your own text in the text box; however, then you cannot specify the **Update mode** option.
  - Select one of the Default Element Hyperlink Update Mode option values: - Automatically updated (Default) . Updates hyperlink text or the referenced element name automatically. - Automatically checked . Enables active validation rules which check if the hyperlink and the referenced element are coherent. - Manually checked. Allows you to run validation manually to check if the hyperlink and the referenced element are coherent. - Do not update . Does not update if the hyperlink text and the referenced element name become inconsistent. Validation will not find any inconsistencies.
5. Click OK . The default settings of hyperlinks to elements are changed.

61862154

3[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Defining hyperlinks' space='']

- Managing hyperlinks
- Linking model elements to OSLC resources

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189144588 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00356: Default templates

- page_id: `189144588`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189144588/Default+templates
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:41:03.092+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Generating Reports from Report Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are 12 default templates that come with the report engine.

1. Class Specification Report describes the specification of class, interface, and enumeration type of the project.
2. Data Dictionary Report enables you to print elements of data dictionary.
3. IEEE 1233 the standard template based on the IEEE 1233 recommendation.
4. Model Extension describes common UML model extension mechanisms (stereotypes, tagged values, and constraints). It is useful for reporting custom UML profiles.
5. Use Case (Simple) describes system functionalities and actors in a simple format.
6. Use Case (Modern) describes system functionalities and actors that are useful in support of use-case driven analysis.
7. Web Publisher (Simple HTML) enables you to publish models (including diagrams) in simple HTML. The report is viewable using a standard browser such as Internet Explorer or Firefox.
8. Web Publisher 2.0 enables you to publish models (including diagrams) in HTML with rich features. The report is viewable using a standard browser such as Internet Explorer or Firefox.
9. Activity Diagram provides a standard report for activity diagrams. The content of the report is a list of all the nodes (ordered by node hierarchy in each activity diagram).
10. Activity Diagram Specification provides a standard report for activities. The content of the report is a list of all the nodes in each activity.
11. Sequence Diagram provides a report that shows sequence diagrams and a list of all the messages in the diagrams.
12. Sequence Diagram Specification provides a report that shows sequence diagrams, and a list of all the messages and lifelines in the diagrams.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>There are 12 default templates that come with the report engine.</p><ol><li><strong>Class Specification Report</strong> <br />describes the specification of class, interface, and enumeration type of the project.</li><li><strong>Data Dictionary Report</strong> <br />enables you to print elements of data dictionary.</li><li><strong>IEEE 1233</strong> <br />the standard template based on the IEEE 1233 recommendation.</li><li><strong>Model Extension</strong> <br />describes common UML model extension mechanisms (stereotypes, tagged values, and constraints). It is useful for reporting custom UML profiles.</li><li><strong>Use Case (Simple)</strong> <br />describes system functionalities and actors in a simple format.</li><li><strong>Use Case (Modern)</strong> <br />describes system functionalities and actors that are useful in support of use-case driven analysis.</li><li><strong>Web Publisher (Simple HTML)</strong> <br />enables you to publish models (including diagrams) in simple HTML. The report is viewable using a standard browser such as Internet Explorer or Firefox.</li><li><strong>Web Publisher 2.0</strong> <br />enables you to publish models (including diagrams) in HTML with rich features. The report is viewable using a standard browser such as Internet Explorer or Firefox.</li><li><strong>Activity Diagram</strong> <br />provides a standard report for activity diagrams. The content of the report is a list of all the nodes (ordered by node hierarchy in each activity diagram).</li><li><strong>Activity Diagram Specification</strong> <br />provides a standard report for activities. The content of the report is a list of all the nodes in each activity.</li><li><strong>Sequence Diagram</strong> <br />provides a report that shows sequence diagrams and a list of all the messages in the diagrams.</li><li><strong>Sequence Diagram Specification</strong> <br />provides a report that shows sequence diagrams, and a list of all the messages and lifelines in the diagrams.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189145516 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00357: Default visibility of property groups and subgroups

- page_id: `189145516`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189145516/Default+visibility+of+property+groups+and+subgroups
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:41:45.160+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide > Domain Specific Language customization > Using Customization Data > Creating Property Groups and Subgroups
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create property groups and subgroups for both standard UML metaclasses and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='']. The group will be added to either the metaclass or to the stereotype depending on the customization target of the customized property group.

If the customization target is a stereotype and the **Hide Metatype**property is set to true, the specified property group in this customization is visible only for the elements with this stereotype applied.

If the customization target is a metaclass, the specified group in this customization will be visible for the elements of this particular type. This group is also visible for the elements with applied stereotypes, except when the **Hide Metatype**property in this stereotype customization is set to true.

The table below lists the default property group visibility in the element’s [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''].

| Customization target | Property group visibility in element’s Specification window and Properties panel |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | Element of metaclass type | Element with stereotype with DSL customization Hide Metatype=false | Element with stereotype with DSL customization Hide Metatype=true |
| Stereotype | Invisible | Visible in standard mode | Visible in standard mode |
| Metaclass | Visible in standard mode | Visible in standard mode | Visible in all modes |

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Customization Data' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Customization Data' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Property Groups and Subgroups' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189143379 space=MD2024xR2 version=3 -->
## PAGE 00358: Defining Constraint condition

- page_id: `189143379`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143379/Defining+Constraint+condition
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-05-28T15:09:08.132+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Validation > Custom validation > Creating new validation rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

You must specify the validation condition for a validation rule. This condition must be true when evaluated in order for the Constraint to be satisfied. The result of a validation rule must be of the boolean type.

To define a Constraint condition

1. Open the Constraint Specification window. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='']>]]>
2. Find the Specification property and select its value box.
3. Click the [...] button. The Specification dialog opens.
4. From the Language list, select one of the following languages:
  1. OCL 2.0 is used for validation rules specified in the OCL language. **OCL Header**If you select the OCL2.0 language, the header of the expression from the constraint information is generated automatically according to the following rules: context <constrained element> <constraint type> <constraint name if any>:. See the following image.[IMAGE alt='' src='']**Constraint types**Since the Constraint is stereotyped by «validationRule» which is derived from «invariant » stereotype, inv is shown in the header. Only inv constraints can be evaluated. Other types of Constraints are not evaluated, but can be modeled for documentation purposes:**def**– for the expression of the constraint with «definition» stereotype applied.**init, derive**- for the expression of the default value of the property.The **derive** expressions can be evaluated indirectly when the validation rule (**inv** constraint) is referencing the property and the validation rule is evaluated.**pre, post, body**- for the expression of the appropriate fields of operation.**OCL Performance**When evaluating the validation rule defined in OCL language, the validation on the first run can have a delay of 20-30 seconds (depending on the computer performance) while the Java compiler is loading. Subsequent validations will run faster than the first one.If the validation process is run heavily on medium-large projects, increasing the default Java VM size is advisable. By default, the VM size is set to 400MB in the modeling tool; increasing this to 600 (or 800 if the computer has sufficient RAM) might improve the performance.
  2. Binary is used for more advanced expressions not easily expressed in OCL. These expressions are written in Java, compiled, and specified in the modeling tool's classpath. These expressions can then be specified as validation rule expressions.
  3. Scripts of JavaScript, Jython, Groovy , and BeanShell . Learn more about script writing in Creating executable opaque behaviors. Since constraints cannot have parameters, you may skip the information about managing parameters.
  4. StructuredExpression . [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying criteria for querying model' space='']>]]>
5. In the Body box, type the expression of the selected language syntax. The modeling tool can evaluate only those validation rules whose expression is defined in one of the languages listed above. The other languages can be used only for documentation purposes. You can add hyperlinks in the **Body**box. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Defining hyperlinks' space='']>]]>You can define expressions for global validation rules. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Global validation rules' space='']>]]>
6. (Optional) Click the Evaluation Mode button to execute an expression on the actual testing model while editing. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Expression Evaluation' space='']>]]>
7. Click OK . The Constraint condition is defined.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4de8cb5d-9a7e-4bca-a25e-64130f3d8b80"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You must specify the validation condition for a validation rule. This condition must be true when evaluated in order for the Constraint to be satisfied. The result of a validation rule must be of the boolean type.</p><p>To define a Constraint condition</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="62d0bcfe-33b3-486b-a276-cbd5e5de3d66">Open the Constraint <strong>Specification </strong>window. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to open the Specification window >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="e99cbb97-3e44-4d4e-ac63-79ba2fe1be3e">Find the <strong>Specification</strong> property and select its value box.</li><li data-uuid="3f7f5660-663e-4608-adcf-d145d7c5d19f">Click the <strong>[...]</strong> button. The <strong>Specification </strong>dialog opens.</li><li data-uuid="b8c70138-2029-4403-b27f-2902a298e686">From the <strong>Language </strong>list, select one of the following languages:<ol><li><strong>OCL 2.0</strong> is used for validation rules specified in the OCL language.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="33001904-6d9f-4343-96f1-bdbc9e0de79b"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>OCL Header </strong></p><p>If you select the OCL2.0 language, the header of the expression from the constraint information is generated automatically according to the following rules: context &lt;constrained element&gt; &lt;constraint type&gt; &lt;constraint name if any&gt;:.  See the following image.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="specification.png" /></ac:image></p><p><strong>Constraint types </strong></p><p>Since the Constraint is stereotyped by «validationRule» which is derived from «invariant » stereotype, inv is shown in the header. Only inv constraints can be evaluated. Other types of Constraints are not evaluated, but can be modeled for documentation purposes:</p><ul><li><strong>def </strong>– for the expression of the constraint with «definition» stereotype applied.</li><li><strong>init, derive </strong>- for the expression of the default value of the property.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9d0ff826-a8f7-41fd-ae64-7fd15ed1b889"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>derive</strong> expressions can be evaluated indirectly when the validation rule (<strong>inv</strong> constraint) is referencing the property and the validation rule is evaluated.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li><strong>pre, post, body </strong>- for the expression of the appropriate fields of operation. </li></ul><p><strong>OCL Performance </strong></p><p>When evaluating the validation rule defined in OCL language, the validation on the first run can have a delay of 20-30 seconds (depending on the computer performance) while the Java compiler is loading. Subsequent validations will run faster than the first one. </p><p>If the validation process is run heavily on medium-large projects, increasing the default Java VM size is advisable. By default, the VM size is set to 400MB in the modeling tool; increasing this to 600 (or 800 if the computer has sufficient RAM) might improve the performance.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><strong>Binary</strong> is used for more advanced expressions not easily expressed in OCL. These expressions are written in Java, compiled, and specified in the modeling tool's classpath. These expressions can then be specified as validation rule expressions.</li><li>Scripts of <strong>JavaScript, Jython, Groovy</strong>, and <strong>BeanShell</strong>. Learn more about script writing in Creating executable opaque behaviors. Since constraints cannot have parameters, you may skip the information about managing parameters.</li><li><strong>StructuredExpression</strong>. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specifying criteria for querying model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about specifying criteria for querying model >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="1db5c078-183d-4d2f-8063-4c5cdea9478b">In the <strong>Body </strong>box, type the expression of the selected language syntax.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2b86ab07-bc39-41d5-8eb4-c3080e72e935"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The modeling tool can evaluate only those validation rules whose expression is defined in one of the languages listed above. The other languages can be used only for documentation purposes.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a99b9755-0acf-444c-ad9c-5ea350f97a74"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can add hyperlinks in the <strong>Body </strong>box. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Defining hyperlinks" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to define hyperlinks >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p>You can define expressions for global validation rules. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Global validation rules" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about global validation rules >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="353e76f0-f716-49cf-b31b-4f741ada3524">(Optional) Click the <strong>Evaluation Mode </strong>button to execute an expression on the actual testing model while editing. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Expression Evaluation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to use the Evaluation Mode >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="485462a7-bbcd-4471-8802-ec7d88326f63">Click <strong>OK</strong>. The Constraint condition is defined.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189145553 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00359: Defining expressions

- page_id: `189145553`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189145553/Defining+expressions
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:46:16.472+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide > Domain Specific Language customization > Using Customization Data > Extending Metamodel with Derived Properties
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1852034054

1852034059

1852034058

Each derived property must have one or more expressions defining the criteria for searching the result elements.

It is strongly recommended that you specify the customization target in the customization element before defining the expression for its derived property.

To open the dialog for a new expression definition

1. Select the attribute with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and specifying derived properties' space=''].
2. Open the attribute’s [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''].
3. In the general specification pane (opened by default), click the **Expression** property and then click the **...** button. The **Expression**dialog opens, and you can then construct predefined expressions or define your custom operation for calculating derived property values. To learn more about derived properties, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Custom traceability rules' space=''] page. For more information on expression specification, see the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying criteria for querying model' space='']page.

1852034052

**Related pages**

- Extending Metamodel with Derived Properties

## PAGE 00360: Defining hyperlinks

- page_id: `189142136`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189142136/Defining+hyperlinks

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

754380129

754380132

1428983844

**On this page**

32

754380131

INLINE

#### Introduction

The hyperlink is a reference to another location that you access by clicking a highlighted text or element. Hyperlinks ensure fast access to information directly from your model. You can create hyperlinks in text, on elements, or on the entire diagram to link them to external or internal resources. For example, a hyperlink can open a file from your file system. The following figure illustrates all available hyperlink types.

The hyperlinks to OSLC resources are available only if the Cameo DataHub Plugin is installed. Please see[how to install the Cameo DataHub Plugin >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/CDH2024xR2/Installation)

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Hyperlink types in modeling tools.

#### Hyperlinks representation

Visual representation of hyperlinks is outlined below:

#### Adding hyperlinks

For more information on how to add and manage hyperlinks, please see:

2

- Linking model elements to OSLC resources

754380128

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with model elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='HTML editor' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing hyperlinks' space='']

Hyperlinks ensure fast access to information directly from your model. You can create hyperlinks in text, on elements, or on the entire diagram to link them to external or internal resources. For example, a hyperlink can open a file from your file system. The following figure illustrates all available hyperlink types.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="adccf501-3612-4cd8-a12d-2f4cdd547da8"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The hyperlinks to OSLC resources are available only if the Cameo DataHub Plugin is installed. Please see<a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/CDH2024xR2/Installation" rel="nofollow"> how to install the Cameo DataHub Plugin &gt;&gt;</a></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:queryparams="effects=border-simple,shadow-kn"><ri:attachment ri:filename="hyperlinks_illustration.png" /></ac:image>Hyperlink types in modeling tools.</h6><h2>Hyperlinks representation</h2><p>Visual representation of hyperlinks is outlined below:</p><ul><ul><li>Hyperlink in text is underlined and blue. For example, <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="12"><ri:attachment ri:filename="hyperlink_in_text_.png" /></ac:image>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Hyperlink on element or diagram:<br />         - In the Model Browser, the <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="hyperlinkElementAdornment.gif" /></ac:image></span> icon appears on the element. For example, <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="hyperlink_on_element_in_model_browser.png" /></ac:image>, <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="hyperlink_on_diagram_in_model_browser.png" /></ac:image>.<br />         - On the diagram pane, on the symbol, the icon representing the subject to which the hyperlink is added appears. For example:<br />                      - To a webpage:<br />                                 <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="59"><ri:attachment ri:filename="hyperlink_to_webpage.png" /></ac:image><br />                      - To a file:<br />                                   <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="69"><ri:attachment ri:filename="hyperlink_to_pdf.png" /></ac:image><br />                      - To an element or diagram:<br />                                     <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="48"><ri:attachment ri:filename="hyperlink_to_ibd.png" /></ac:image> <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="49"><ri:attachment ri:filename="hyperlink_to_block.png" /></ac:image><br />                      - To OSLC resources, wizards, validation, and reports:<br />                                      <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="65"><ri:attachment ri:filename="hyperlink_to_oslc_wizards_validation_reports.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a98e1b75-a0ea-4c29-a2e2-1c8b6c7d9669"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">If the element has an active navigation target then an active navigation target icon is show on a symbol. You can choose whether to show the icon by changing a value of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customizing environment options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Environment Option]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> <strong>Show Active Navigation Icons</strong>. By default, this option is set to <em>true</em>. </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></ul><h2 class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Adding hyperlinks<br /></span></h2><p class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">For more information on how to add and manage hyperlinks, please see:</span></p><p class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="ae49459b-75ed-4bf2-b663-f60525fd97db"><ac:parameter ac:name="depth">2</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><ul><li><a class="current" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Linking+model+elements+to+OSLC+resources" rel="nofollow">Linking model elements to OSLC resources</a></li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="46ae756c-d3da-48fc-a566-9191c406428d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">754380128</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Manage Navigation" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with model elements" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="HTML editor" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing hyperlinks" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141150 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00361: Defining layout template

- page_id: `189141150`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141150/Defining+layout+template
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:37:57.091+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Layout templates
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Composite Structure Diagram as a layout template

57880485

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Composite Structure Diagram as a layout template

57880487

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Composite Structure Diagram as a layout template

57880486

If you want to reuse the style and layout of Class with its Ports in another diagram, you need to define the appearance of the Class, the style, and position of its Ports in the Class diagram. It means that you define a new layout template. When defining the appearance of the Class, you can change its color, font, size, layout, and other style properties. To do this, follow the procedure below.

#### WARNING: Important

Important

Only one Class shape with its Ports can be defined in one layout template diagram.

To define the layout template

1. Create the Class diagram. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='']]]>
2. Create a new or represent an existing Class with its Ports on the diagram pane.
3. Specify the appearance of all shapes by using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='']Symbol Properties dialog and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space=''] .
4. Set the positions of Ports on Class shape manually. 
The appearance of Class and its Ports shapes are defined (see the figure below). [IMAGE alt='' src='']The comparison of default and defined appearance of the Modem Card Block, style, and the position of its ports. The illustration displays concepts from SysML Plugin.

#### INFO: Composite Structure Diagram as a layout template

Composite Structure Diagram as a layout template

- From the version 19.0 SP1, you can define the appearance and layout of a Part Property with its Ports in the Composite Structure Diagram and set this diagram as layout template.
- If a Composite Structure Diagram displays several Part Properties, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating layout template' space=''] directly from a particular Part Property. It's appearance and layout will be copied to the template automatically.

57880484

**Sample model**

The sample model used in the figure of this page is the **Modem Cable** sample model. To open this sample do one of the following:

- Download *[Modem Cable Layout Templates.mdzip](https://docs.nomagic.com/download/attachments/9915051/Modem%20Cable%20Layout%20Templates.mdzip?version=1&modificationDate=1481817717837&api=v2).*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

It means that you define a new layout template. When defining the appearance of the Class, you can change its color, font, size, layout, and other style properties. To do this, follow the procedure below.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e8579265-ff01-4631-a7bb-2ec607d5bbd0"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Only one Class shape with its Ports can be defined in one layout template diagram.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To define the layout template</p><hr /><ol><li>Create the Class diagram. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating diagrams" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to create diagrams >]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>Create a new or represent an existing Class with its Ports on the diagram pane.</li><li>Specify the appearance of all shapes by using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Symbol Properties dialog" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Symbol Properties</span> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram toolbar]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li><p>Set the positions of Ports on Class shape manually.<br />The appearance of Class and its Ports shapes are defined (see the figure below).<br /><br /></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="comparison_of_default_and_user_defined_appearance_of_block_and_ports.png" /></ac:image><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span>The comparison of default and defined appearance of the Modem Card Block, style, and the position of its ports. The illustration displays concepts from SysML Plugin.</h6></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="136bf21e-c771-40e3-b5f7-62d0f1b41818"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Composite Structure Diagram as a layout template</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>From the version 19.0 SP1, you can define the appearance and layout of a Part Property with its Ports in the Composite Structure Diagram and set this diagram as layout template.</li><li>If a Composite Structure Diagram displays several Part Properties, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating layout template" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create a layout template]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> directly from a particular Part Property. It's appearance and layout will be copied to the template automatically.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2701dce5-9e6b-496d-8490-1d529de1b398"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">57880484</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The sample model used in the figure of this page is the <strong>Modem Cable</strong> sample model. To open this sample do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Download<span class="confluence-link"><em> <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/download/attachments/9915051/Modem%20Cable%20Layout%20Templates.mdzip?version=1&amp;modificationDate=1481817717837&amp;api=v2">Modem Cable Layout Templates.mdzip</a>.</em></span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189145491 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00362: Defining Preferred Metatype

- page_id: `189145491`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189145491/Defining+Preferred+Metatype
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:41:43.554+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide > Domain Specific Language customization > Using Customization Data
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The DSL customization engine allows specification of a preferred metatype if more than one metatype is defined for the DSL element.

To define a preferred metatype for the DSL type

1. Select the appropriate [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customization Elements' space=''] and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] .
2. Click the value specification cell of the Preferred Metatype property and do one of the following: The selected metatype will be assigned as the **Preferred Metatype** property value. falseMultiple selection is not allowed. You must define a single value.
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting single property value from editable list' space=''] and close the Specification window.
  - Click the [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] button [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new elements' space=''] . In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting elements' space=''] , select the created metatype and click OK. Close the Specification window.

After updating the customization element specification, you must either [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using other projects in a project' space=''] in the model or reopen the model.

From now on, all DSL elements will be created with the preferred metatype specified in the customization.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Customization Data' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Customization Data' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Close the Specification window.</li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target">The selected metatype will be assigned as the <strong>Preferred Metatype</strong> property value.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d15e29e9-b1b6-4318-af4e-392c34391b6f"><ac:parameter ac:name="icon">false</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Multiple selection is not allowed. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189142545 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00363: Defining properties of the customized element to be spell checked

- page_id: `189142545`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189142545/Defining+properties+of+the+customized+element+to+be+spell+checked
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:39:21.655+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools > Spelling Checker
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

266235839

266235842

266235840

You can check both DSL customization classes and their properties. You can choose which properties of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customization Elements' space=''] ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='']with a «Customization» [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='']) to be spell checked.

You accomplish this by defining a customization class [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing tagged value' space='']*checkSpelling* value, found in the properties tag group. By creating a value for this tag, you can choose String properties to check spelling for. By default, there are no properties marked as checkable.

266235838

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing tagged value' space='']

**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Spelling Checker' space='']**
