You can choose which properties of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customization Elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[customized element]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Class" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Class ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>with a «Customization» <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Stereotype" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[stereotype]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) to be spell checked.</p><p>You accomplish this by defining a customization class <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Editing tagged value" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[tag ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><em>checkSpelling</em> value, found in the properties tag group. By creating a value for this tag, you can choose String properties to check spelling for. By default, there are no properties marked as checkable.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="51a209f8-ecd9-4549-ad2e-bb3893e72321"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">266235838</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Editing tagged value" /></ac:link><strong><br class="_mce_tagged_br" /></strong></li></ul><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="75f757a6-f50b-4e54-a1ca-bbeb27b839bb"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Spelling Checker" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189145922 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00364: Deleting and executing Macros

- page_id: `189145922`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189145922/Deleting+and+executing+Macros
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:42:05.131+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Macro Engine Documentation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

851813206

851813217

851813204

**On this page:**

851813216

You can click the **[IMAGE alt='' src='']** or **[IMAGE alt='' src='']** button in the **Organize Macros** dialog to delete or execute a selected macro. You can also press the pre-designed mnemonic keys to delete or run a macro as described in****Organizing Macros Dialog Mnemonic Keys below.

##### Deleting a Macro

To delete a macro from the **Organize Macros** dialog

1. Click Tools > Macros > Organize Macros to open the Organize Macros dialog.
2. Select a macro from the table and either click Delete or press Alt + D . A dialog will open, asking whether you want to delete the macro, see figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

3. Click **Yes** and the macro will be deleted from the **Organize Macros** dialog.

##### Executing a Macro

To execute a macro from the **Organize Macros** dialog

1. Click Tools > Macros > Organize Macros The Organize Macros dialog will open, see figure below. [ATTACHMENT filename='organize-macros.png']
2. Select a macro from the table and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] . After the macro has been executed, a message will open: The macro <macro name> has been executed .
3. If you have the parameters in the **Macro Information** dialog, you need to specify the value in the **Macro Arguments** dialog, see figure below, before running the macro. For an array datatype, you need to click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button in the **Value** column in the **Macro Arguments** dialog and enter each value into each line. The value of the first line will be the value in array index 0.An ElementPath is a Qualified Name. You can find this information in the specification dialog of each element, see figure below.The **Macro Arguments** dialog, see the figure below, will be displayed if the**[IMAGE alt='' src='']** check box is not selected.If you want to save argument values in the **Macro Arguments** dialog, you need to select the **Set as default values** check box before you click **OK**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- You can also execute a macro from the main browser by pressing the shortcut keys that you have defined in the Organize Macros dialog.
- You can only run macro one at a time.
- If there is an error while running a macro, for example, syntax error, the following message will open: <Modeling tool name> cannot execute the <macro language> macro, please make sure that <path, filename, extension> is correct. <error description> .
- If the modeling tool cannot find a macro file in the location that you have specified in the Open dialog, the following message will open: <Modeling tool name> cannot find the macro: <path, filename, extension> .

##### Organizing Macros Dialog Mnemonic Keys

Macro Engine also provides mnemonic keys to add, edit, delete, and run a macro from the **Organize Macros** dialog. The table below shows the **Organize Macros** dialog mnemonic keys and their function.

*Table listing of Organize Macros Dialog Mnemonic Keys*

| Mnemonic keys | Button | Function |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Alt + A |  | To add a macro in the Macro Information dialog. |
| Alt + E |  | To edit a macro in the Macro Information dialog. |
| Alt + D |  | To delete a macro from the Organize Macros dialog. |
| Alt + O |  | Code To edit source code in Macro Editor. |
| Alt + R |  | To run a macro from the Organize Macros dialog. |
| Alt + C |  | To click the Close button. |
| Alt + H |  | To click the Help button. |

- You can click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button in the Organize Macros dialog, see figure above, to edit and save source code in the Macro Editor dialog, see figure below.
- You can click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to save the source code or click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to run the macro in the Macro Editor dialog, see figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141412 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00365: Dependency

- page_id: `189141412`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141412/Dependency
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:45:25.908+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Editing property values

745065613

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Editing property values

745065615

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Editing property values

745065614

A dependency is a relationship signifying that a [model element](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Model+elements) requires other model elements for its specification or implementation. The client element is either semantically or structurally dependent on the definition of the supplier element(s).

A dependency is shown as a dashed arrow between classes or packages. The client element (at the tail of the arrow) depends on the supplier element (at the arrowhead). You can label the arrow with an optional stereotype and an individual name.

You can also draw a dependency between a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space=''] and other Class elements, such as [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Attribute' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operation' space=''].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of dependency relationships: «access» and «import».

Dependency, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Navigating between different levels of abstraction' space=''], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Analyzing usages and dependencies' space=''] are defined in the dialog of the same structure. They differ from one another only by the corresponding Specification name. You can specify a dependency by changing its property values in the dependency [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='']. Each property is described in the description area on this window.

#### TIP: Editing property values

Editing property values

For more information about specifying property values, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space=''].

There are several kinds of dependencies:

| Dependency kind | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Template binding | Represents a relationship between a templatable element and a template. A template binding specifies the substitutions of actual parameters for the formal parameters of the template.You can specify template binding dependency properties and find each property's description in the template binding dependency Specification window. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the Specification window. |
| Abstraction | Relates two elements or sets of elements that represent the same concept at different levels of abstraction or from different viewpoints. In the metamodel, an abstraction is a dependency in which there is a mapping between the supplier and the client.Define an abstraction relationship in the Abstraction Specification window. |
| Usage | A relationship in which one element requires another element (or set of elements) for its full implementation or . In the metamodel, a usage is a dependency in which the client requires the presence of the supplier.Define a usage relationship in the Usage Specification window. |
| Package merge | A directed relationship between two , indicating that the contents of the two packages are to be combined. It has a dependency relationship with the applied stereotype «merge».Define a merge relationship in the Dependency Specification window. |
| Element import | A directed relationship between an importing namespace and a packageable element. The name of the packageable element or its alias is added to the namespace of the importing namespace. It has a dependency relationship with the applied stereotype «import».Define an import relationship in the Dependency Specification window. To draw the Element Import link, select the Element Import path to draw in the Class diagram toolbar from the Abstraction group. |
| Access | Shows that elements can only be accessed from a , and it cannot be referenced. |
| Deployment | The allocation of a deployment target to an artifact or artifact instance. |
| Role Binding | A relationship in a . Role Binding is used to connect with . |
| Mount | A mount dependency represents a former location of a shared package; that is, a location where the package was mounted in a local project.A mount dependency is created:After you export a project from Teamwork Cloud; orWhen a local project is added to Teamwork Cloud.Mounted packages in the Containment tree are adorned with an icon and have their project usage name in brackets, as shown in the following figure:The purpose of establishing the mount dependency is to ensure the same containment structure during project transformation to Teamwork Cloud, to maintain the correct scope in utilities like smart packages, validation rules, tables etc. |

745065612

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f239384e-b503-4fb4-9f1e-b7211948cf8b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">745065613</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b8e87a6e-081f-4e53-812d-9265a4881a1b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">745065615</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6eab5bf6-1140-4dc9-8eeb-0a1f913f5911"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">745065614</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>A dependency is a relationship signifying that a <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Model+elements">model element</a> requires other model elements for its specification or implementation. The client element is either semantically or structurally dependent on the definition of the supplier element(s).</p><p>A dependency is shown as a dashed arrow between classes or packages. The client element (at the tail of the arrow) depends on the supplier element (at the arrowhead). You can label the arrow with an optional stereotype and an individual name.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="478fd602-4249-41b7-b0b9-3453d0416c17"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can also draw a dependency between a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Class" /></ac:link> and other Class elements, such as <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Attribute" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[attributes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[operations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> <ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="example_of_dependencies.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of dependency relationships: «access» and «import».</h6><p>Dependency, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Navigating between different levels of abstraction" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[abstraction]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Analyzing usages and dependencies" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[usage relationships]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> are defined in the dialog of the same structure. They differ from one another only by the corresponding Specification name. You can specify a dependency by changing its property values in the dependency <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>. Each property is described in the description area on this window.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="be0793a2-8907-4c1a-bc47-4d1ec1f673c3"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Editing property values</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">For more information about specifying property values, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Editing property values" /></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p>There are several kinds of dependencies:</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Dependency kind</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><h4>Template binding</h4></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Represents a relationship between a templatable element and a template. A template binding specifies the substitutions of actual parameters for the formal parameters of the template.</p><p>You can specify template binding dependency properties and find each property's description in the template binding dependency Specification window. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the Specification window. </p></div></td></tr><tr><td><h4>Abstraction</h4></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Relates two elements or sets of elements that represent the same concept at different levels of abstraction or from different viewpoints. In the metamodel, an abstraction is a dependency in which there is a mapping between the supplier and the client.</p><p>Define an abstraction relationship in the Abstraction Specification window. </p></div></td></tr><tr><td><h4>Usage</h4></td><td><p>A relationship in which one element requires another element (or set of elements) for its full implementation or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[operation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. In the metamodel, a usage is a dependency in which the client requires the presence of the supplier.</p><p>Define a usage relationship in the Usage Specification window. </p></td></tr><tr><td><h4>Package merge</h4></td><td><p>A directed relationship between two <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Package" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[packages]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, indicating that the contents of the two packages are to be combined. It has a dependency relationship with the applied stereotype <em>«merge»</em>.</p><p>Define a merge relationship in the Dependency Specification window. </p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><h4>Element import</h4></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>A directed relationship between an importing namespace and a packageable <span style="line-height: 1.42857;">element. The name of the packageable element or its alias is added to the namespace of the importing </span>namespace. It has a dependency relationship with the applied stereotype <em>«import»</em>.</p><p>Define an import relationship in the Dependency Specification window. </p><p>To draw the Element Import link, select the Element Import path to draw in the Class diagram toolbar from the Abstraction group. </p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="element_import.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><h4>Access</h4></td><td colspan="1"><p>Shows that elements can only be accessed from a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Package" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[package]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and it cannot be referenced.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><h4>Deployment</h4></td><td colspan="1"><p>The allocation of a deployment target to an artifact or artifact instance.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><h4><span>Role Binding </span></h4></td><td colspan="1"><p>A relationship in a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram" /></ac:link>. Role Binding is used to connect <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Part" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Parts]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> with <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Collaboration Use" /></ac:link>.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><h4>Mount</h4></div></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>A mount dependency represents a former location of a shared package; that is, a location where the package was mounted in a local project.</p><p>A mount dependency is created:</p><ul><li>After you export a project from Teamwork Cloud; or</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">When a local project is added to Teamwork Cloud.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="41e837f4-1df3-443e-b319-ad93d7c8ca3c"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Mounted packages in the Containment tree are <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a58e89da-d5aa-4ecd-aa7e-a435ec3ba003">adorned</ac:inline-comment-marker> with an icon and have their project usage name in brackets, <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">as shown in the following figure:</span></span></p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Mounted_projects.png" /></ac:image></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><p><span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a1597fed-3bf8-4a17-95a7-b2b374fabeb2">The purpose of establishing the mount dependency is to ensure the same containment structure during project transformation to Teamwork Cloud,</ac:inline-comment-marker></span> to maintain the correct scope in utilities like smart packages, validation rules, tables etc.</p></div></td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="306dc793-e505-4955-9705-9586072be117"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">745065612</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="194a0c61-0fa3-49ba-bbd9-e99868580996" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189140263 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00366: Dependency Matrix

- page_id: `189140263`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189140263/Dependency+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:49.503+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Analysis diagrams
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

308903551

308903553

308903552

Demo introducing Dependency Matix.

This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions only.

The Dependency Matrix is a quick method for representing dependencies compactly. Diagrams, UML elements, and extended UML elements serve as row and column entries. The cells in the matrix show where these elements are associated (related).

Dependency matrices can show dependency criteria, such as

- UML relationships
- BPML, SysML, UPDM, and other DSL relationships
- Semantic dependencies (dependencies through properties)
- Relationships through tags (tags allow for relating UML elements that cannot be represented on the same diagram)
- Transitive relationships (through the OCL or meta chain)

Dependency matrices are useful for

- Quickly visualizing and creating dependency criteria.
- Compactly visualizing relations of a large system whose system relations cannot be represented by a diagram on a single sheet of paper due to the size of the system.
- Studying relations from a particular scope and type of element by filtering the unimportant data. Hierarchical columns allow for browsing directly in the created matrix through the model scope and visualize the required area.
- Showing relations that cannot be represented on diagrams, such as a representation (a class by the lifeline), a behavior representation in other diagrams, an operation representation by Call Behavior Action, Use Case relations with describing activities through the property Owned Behavior, etc. The semantic dependency matrix is needed for deeper model analysis. The Dependency Matrix allows the representation of any kind of relations through element properties.

To learn about Dependency Matrix properties, please click [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new dependency matrix type' space=''].

308903550

**Related pages**

The Dependency Matrix allows the representation of any kind of relations through element properties.</p></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="525fa5a5-ef0d-406e-9c44-f625e82fc12d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To learn about Dependency Matrix properties, please click <ac:link ac:anchor="Step 3: Specify Dependency Matrix Properties"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating new dependency matrix type" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[here]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="53766e9c-a7c0-421a-97bb-ea064e7f682b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">308903550</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f41e54f0-f8c9-4488-b75e-638453ab112b" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189140348 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00367: Dependency Matrix Criteria area

- page_id: `189140348`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189140348/Dependency+Matrix+Criteria+area
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:53.198+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Analysis diagrams > Dependency Matrix > Dependency Matrix environment
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To select the specified value in the Model Browser or to open its Specification window, you can right-click on the value boxes (except for the Dependency Criteria) in the **Criteria** area.

See the descriptions of the dependency matrix Criteria area in the following table.

| Element Name | Element Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Row Element Type | Text box with | Click the ... button to select an element type or multiple element types to show in rows of the dependency matrix. |
| Column Element Type | Text box with | Click the ... button to select an element or multiple element types to show in columns of the dependency matrix. |
| Row Scope | Text box with | Click the ... button to define a scope of the model (packages/profiles) from which elements should be displayed in rows of the dependency matrix.Click to to open the Query dialog and specify the criteria that define row scope. |
| Column Scope | Text box with | Click the ... button to define a scope of the model (packages/profiles) from which elements should be displayed in columns of the dependency matrix.Click to to open the Query dialog and specify the criteria that define column scope. |
| Dependency Criteria | Text box with | Click the ... button to define what relationships between row and column elements you wish to display in the dependency matrix cells. |
| Direction | Drop-down list | Select a direction of relationships for the relationship analysis and representation in the dependency matrix. Be aware that new relationships will be created with the selected direction. |
| Show Elements | Drop-down list | Select to show only related (by a selected dependency criteria), only non-related, or all elements. |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189140351 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00369: Dependency Matrix shortcut menu

- page_id: `189140351`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189140351/Dependency+Matrix+shortcut+menu
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:53.411+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Analysis diagrams > Dependency Matrix > Dependency Matrix environment
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To open the dependency matrix shortcut menu

- Right-click an empty space on one of the following [ATTACHMENT filename='Dep_matrix_shortcut_menu.png']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency Matrix Criteria area' space='']
  - Matrix pane

| Command | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Specification (Enter) | Opens the of a dependency matrix. |
| Go To | Navigates back or forward to the diagram that was open in the current diagram tab, finds and navigates to diagrams wherein the matrix is used, or to the matrix owner in the , or to a hyperlinked object, or to an element to which the selected element is related through a traceability relationship. |
| Select in Containment Tree (Alt+B) | Selects the dependency matrix in the of the . |
| Related Elements | Allows selecting whether to create a for the matrix or search for model elements which depends on the matrix or are used by the matrix. |
| Refactor | the matrix to a diagram indicated by the user. |
| Tools | Opens a list of that are available for the matrix. |
| Print Active Diagram | Prints a matrix. |
| Show Tabs in Full Screen (F11) | Shows a matrix in the full screen mode. Click Close Full Screen to return to the previous view. |
| Close Tab (Ctrl+F4) | Closes an active matrix. |
| Close All Tabs But Current (Ctrl+Shift+F4) | Closes all diagrams except an active one. |
| Close All Tabs(Ctrl+Alt+F4) | Closes all opened diagrams. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

/></ac:link></li><li>Matrix pane</li></ul><ac:image ac:title="Dependency Matrix shortcut menu" ac:alt="Dependency Matrix shortcut menu"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Dep_matrix_shortcut_menu.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><p> </p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Command</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Specification (Enter)</strong></td><td>Opens the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link> of a dependency matrix.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Go To</strong></td><td><p>Navigates back or forward to the diagram that was open in the current diagram tab, finds and navigates to diagrams wherein the matrix is used, or to the matrix owner in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, or to a hyperlinked object, or to an element to which the selected element is related through a traceability relationship.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Select in Containment Tree (Alt+B)</strong></p></td><td><p>Selects the dependency matrix in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Related Elements</strong></td><td><p>Allows selecting whether to create a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Relation Map" /></ac:link> for the matrix or search for model elements which depends on the matrix or are used by the matrix.</p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Refactor</strong></td><td><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Refactoring" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Converts or replaces]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the matrix to a diagram indicated by the user.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Tools</strong></td><td>Opens a list of <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Tools" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[tools ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>that are available for the matrix.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Print Active Diagram</strong></td><td>Prints a matrix.</td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Show Tabs in Full Screen (F11)</strong></p></td><td><p>Shows a matrix in the full screen mode. Click <strong>Close Full Screen</strong> to return to the previous view.</p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Close Tab (Ctrl+F4)</strong></td><td>Closes an active matrix.</td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Close All Tabs But Current (Ctrl+Shift+F4)</strong></p></td><td>Closes all diagrams except an active one.</td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Close All Tabs</strong><br /><strong>(Ctrl+Alt+F4)</strong></p></td><td>Closes all opened diagrams.</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189145009 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00370: Dependency Matrix tool

- page_id: `189145009`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189145009/Dependency+Matrix+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:41:23.436+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1352667888

1352667888

1352667888

The Dependency Matrix tool allows templates to:

- Access data from Dependency Matrix.
- Use diagrams to get data from Dependency Matrix.
- Get row nodes and elements.
- Get column nodes and elements.
- Get dependencies in a cell (between specific row and column nodes).

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The Dependency Matrix diagram.

Like other Custom tools, the Generic Table tool '*dependencymatrixtool.jar*' must be presented in the 'extensions' folder of the Report Wizard plugin. For further information on Custom tools and the installation, see section *[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Custom tool' space='']*.

To import the Dependency Matrix tool to a template, type the following code in the template:

```text

```

1352667887

For further information on Custom tools and the installation, see section <em><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Custom tool" /></ac:link></em>.</p><p>To import the Dependency Matrix tool to a template, type the following code in the template:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="60b1715a-4f98-4144-8e2f-fe560aeabf6a"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import("depmatrix","com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.DependencyMatrixTool")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1206f0ed-5098-4881-8bdb-c970db001d9a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1352667887</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="58a9d83e-5455-4a23-8fa0-22a48a2c3fda" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189145011 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00371: Dependency matrix tool API

- page_id: `189145011`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189145011/Dependency+matrix+tool+API
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:41:23.544+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Dependency Matrix tool
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1352819143

1352819146

1352819140

**On this page**

4

1352819146

Dependency Matrix tool allows templates to access data from a Dependency Matrix, use diagrams to get data, row elements, column names, or relations between row and column elements from the matrix.

You can use the following Dependency Matrix tool API in the template.

**Class DependencyMatrixTool**

Use the following methods to use diagrams to get data.

- +getMatrix( diagram : Diagram) : Matrix
- +getMatrix( diagramName : String) : Matrix

**Class Matrix**

Use the following methods to get matrix row elements or nodes, column elements or nodes, and dependencies between these nodes/elements.

- +getRowNodes() : List<ElementNode>
- +getColumnNodes() : List<ElementNode>
- +getCellDependencies( rowNode: ElementNode, columnNode: ElementNode)
- +getRowElements() : List<Element>
- +getColumnElements() : List<Element>
- +getRelation( row: Element, column : Element) : List<Relation>

**Class Relation**

Describes dependency in a Dependency Matrix cell. Use the following methods for class relations.

- +getName() : String
- +getElement() : Element
- +getSemanticType() : String
- +getDirection() : String

##### Using Diagrams to Get Data from Dependency Matrix

A Dependency Matrix is a special diagram. You can retrieve it using the $Diagram variable. Thus, every method must accept a diagram instance or a diagram’s name. Use the following methods to return a Dependency Matrix instance. You can retrieve rows and columns from the Dependency Matrix instances.

##### Getting Dependency Matrix Instances from Diagram Elements

**getMatrix(diagram : Diagram) : Matrix** 
To get a Dependency Matrix instance from a specified diagram element, use the following code.

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- diagram – a diagram element

Return an instance of Dependency Matrix.

For example:

```text

```

##### Getting Dependency Matrix Instances from Diagram Names

**getMatrix(diagramName : String) : Matrix** 
To get a Dependency Matrix instance from a specified diagram’s name.

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- diagram – a diagram’s name

Return a Dependency Matrix instance.

Example code:

```text

```

##### Getting Rows and Columns

The Matrix consists of rows and columns. Each row or column is an instance of ElementNode object. Each ElementNode represents a single model Element. There might be multiple ElementNodes for the same model Element in a Dependency Matrix diagram, since the same model Element can be represented multiple times in a diagram.

##### Getting All Rows

**getRowNodes() : List<ElementNode>**

Use this method to retrieve a list of rows, ordered in the same way as actually displayed in the diagram. Nodes representing grouping packages, that do not belong to the actual matrix data, are excluded.

```text

```

The returned value is a list of ElementNodes. Method **getElement() : Element** can then be used to retrieve the model element represented by a row.

To print all row elements' names, for example, type the following code:

```text

```

##### Getting all Columns

**getColumnNodes() : List<ElementNode>**

Use this method to retrieve a list of columns, ordered in the same way as actually displayed in the diagram. Nodes representing grouping packages, that do not belong to the actual matrix data, are excluded.

```text

```

The returned value is a list of ElementNodes. Method **getElement() : Element** can then be used to retrieve the model element represented by a column.

To print all column elements' names, for example, type the following code:

```text

```

##### Getting Dependencies between Rows and Columns

**getCellDependencies(rowNode: ElementNode, columnNode: ElementNode)**

Use this method to retrieve the relations in a specific matrix cell, which is identified by provided row and column nodes.

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- rowNode – ElementNode of a row
- columnNode – ElementNode of a column

The returned value is a list of Relations.

The Relation class contains the following methods:

- getSemanticType : String Return the semantic type
- getElement : Element Return a relationship element or null if the relationship is not an element, for example, tag name.
- getName : String Return a relationship name. If the relationship is a tag name, it will return the tag name. If the relationship is NamedElement, it will return the element name; otherwise, return a human name.
- getDirection : String Return the direction.

To print the row, column, and its relationship name, for example, type the following code:

```text

```

To print rows, columns, and relations in a spreadsheet file format, for example, type the following code:

| #import('depmatrix','com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.DependencyMatrixTool')#forpage ($diagram in $report.filterDiagram($Diagram,["Dependency Matrix"]))#set($matrix=$depmatrix.getMatrix($diagram))$bookmark.create($diagram.name) |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | #forcol ($col in $matrix.columnNodes)$col.element.name#endcol |  |
| #forrow($row in $matrix.rowNodes)$row.element.name | #forcol ($col in $matrix.columns)#if(!$report.isEmpty($matrix.getCellDependencies($row,$col)))->#end#endcol | #endrow |
| #endpage |  |  |

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d65960db-9283-4000-b9b5-50182873066d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1352819143</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="051b0d55-0930-4a8b-a13e-a2c93806dcc7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1352819146</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9090caa7-1df9-4465-b750-53f7a15bfa70"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1352819140</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="84dddb78-0faa-4e33-a4a2-c2b3cb578630"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="88281c9b-9444-466f-8a45-e22db9b7496c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1352819146</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Dependency Matrix tool allows templates to access data from a Dependency Matrix, use diagrams to get data, row elements, column names, or relations between row and column elements from the matrix.</p><p>You can use the following Dependency Matrix tool API in the template.</p><p><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="42219764-7874-4d90-8605-96c5f23289a9">Class DependencyMatrixTool</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong></p><p>Use the following methods to use diagrams to get data.</p><ul><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">+getMatrix( diagram : Diagram) : Matrix</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">+getMatrix( diagramName : String) : Matrix <br /><br /></li></ul><p><strong>Class Matrix</strong></p><p>Use the following methods to get matrix row elements or nodes, column elements or nodes, and dependencies between these nodes/elements.</p><ul><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">+getRowNodes() : List&lt;ElementNode&gt;</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">+getColumnNodes() : List&lt;ElementNode&gt;</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">+getCellDependencies( rowNode: ElementNode, columnNode: ElementNode)</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">+getRowElements() : List&lt;Element&gt;</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">+getColumnElements() : List&lt;Element&gt; </li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">+getRelation( row: Element, column : Element) : List&lt;Relation&gt; <br /><br /></li></ul><p><strong>Class Relation</strong></p><p>Describes dependency in a Dependency Matrix cell. Use the following methods for class relations.</p><ul><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">+getName() : String</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">+getElement() : Element </li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">+getSemanticType() : String </li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">+getDirection() : String</li></ul><h3>Using Diagrams to Get Data from Dependency Matrix</h3><p>A Dependency Matrix is a special diagram. You can retrieve it using the $Diagram variable. Thus, every method must accept a diagram instance or a diagram’s name. Use the following methods to return a Dependency Matrix instance. You can retrieve rows and columns from the Dependency Matrix instances.</p><h3>Getting Dependency Matrix Instances from Diagram Elements</h3><p><strong>getMatrix(diagram : Diagram) : Matrix</strong><br />To get a Dependency Matrix instance from a specified diagram element, use the following code.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8fb3ec99-7085-4d08-a2ce-b6ee4e90c5aa"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($matrix = $depmatrix.getMatrix($diagram))]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameter is:</p><ul><li>diagram – a diagram element</li></ul><p>Return an instance of Dependency Matrix.</p><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d1eeac84-576f-424b-a258-933e831a39f0"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($diagram in $project.getDiagrams("Dependency Matrix"))
#set($matrix = $depmatrix.getMatrix($diagram)) 
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Getting Dependency Matrix Instances from Diagram Names</h3><p><strong>getMatrix(diagramName : String) : Matrix</strong> <br />To get a Dependency Matrix instance from a specified diagram’s name.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="076989fa-ed89-4186-9c35-315bb3c52b5f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($matrix = $depmatrix.getMatrix($diagram))]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameter is: </p><ul><li>diagram – a diagram’s name</li></ul><p>Return a Dependency Matrix instance.</p><p>Example code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2cb801ad-449e-4a77-8e55-9262a5d0f37b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($diagram = "diagram name")
#set($matrix = $depmatrix.getMatrix($diagram))]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Getting Rows and Columns</h3><p>The Matrix consists of rows and columns. Each row or column is an instance of ElementNode object. Each ElementNode represents a single model Element. There might be multiple ElementNodes for the same model Element in a Dependency Matrix diagram, since the same model Element can be represented multiple times in a diagram.</p><h3>Getting All Rows</h3><p><strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">getRowNodes() : List&lt;ElementNode&gt;</span></strong></p><p>Use this method to retrieve a list of rows, ordered in the same way as actually displayed in the diagram. Nodes representing grouping packages, that do not belong to the actual matrix data, are excluded.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0a742c90-fbaa-40f9-8fc1-fc0ab63d716a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$matrix.getRowNodes()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The returned value is a list of ElementNodes. Method <strong>getElement() : Element</strong> can then be used to retrieve the model element represented by a row.</p><p>To print all row elements' names, for example, type the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="65eabb1b-3fbe-49bd-bbc9-a58dd91223f1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($rowNode in $matrix.rowNodes) 
$rowNode.element.name 
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Getting all Columns</h3><p><strong>getColumnNodes() : List&lt;ElementNode&gt;</strong></p><p>Use this method to retrieve a list of columns, ordered in the same way as actually displayed in the diagram. Nodes representing grouping packages, that do not belong to the actual matrix data, are excluded.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="778b1227-f91a-4dca-929f-7e764a388888"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$matrix.getRowNodes()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The returned value is a list of ElementNodes. Method <strong>getElement() : Element</strong> can then be used to retrieve the model element represented by a column.</p><p>To print all column elements' names, for example, type the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b930c55b-1af1-4d2d-a759-56410c12be65"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($col in $matrix.columns) 
$colNode.element.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Getting Dependencies between Rows and Columns</h3><p><strong>getCellDependencies(rowNode: ElementNode, columnNode: ElementNode)</strong></p><p>Use this method to retrieve the relations in a specific matrix cell, which is identified by provided row and column nodes.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f3cfac07-2d4a-4984-9227-d082e318c001"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$matrix.getCellDependencies($rowNode, $columnNode)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameter is:</p><ul><li>rowNode –  ElementNode of a row</li><li>columnNode –  ElementNode of a column</li></ul><p>The returned value is a list of Relations.</p><p>The Relation class contains the following methods:</p><ul><li><strong>getSemanticType</strong> : String<br />             Return the semantic type</li><li><strong>getElement</strong> : Element <br />             Return a relationship element or null if the relationship is not an element, for example, tag name.</li><li><strong>getName</strong> : String <br />             Return a relationship name. If the relationship is a tag name, it will return the tag name. If the relationship is NamedElement, it will return the element name;              otherwise, return a human name.</li><li><strong>getDirection</strong> : String <br />             Return the direction.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To print the row, column, and its relationship name, for example, type the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="722e0feb-cfdd-4eea-b81e-ddf75989285c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($row in $matrix.rowNodes) 
   #foreach($col in $matrix.columnNodes)
      #foreach($rel in $matrix.getCellDependencies($row, $col))
         $row.element.name has $rel.name with $col.element.name
      #end
   #end
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p>To print rows, columns, and relations in a spreadsheet file format, for example, type the following code:</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 90.779%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 53.1113%;" /><col style="width: 40.3155%;" /><col style="width: 6.57318%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><pre>#import('depmatrix','com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.DependencyMatrixTool')</pre><pre>#forpage ($diagram in $report.filterDiagram($Diagram,[&quot;Dependency Matrix&quot;]))</pre><pre>#set($matrix=$depmatrix.getMatrix($diagram))</pre><pre>$bookmark.create($diagram.name)</pre></td><td><br /></td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><br /></td><td class="highlight-grey" colspan="1" data-highlight-colour="grey"><pre>#forcol ($col in $matrix.columnNodes)$col.element.name#endcol</pre></td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-grey" data-highlight-colour="grey"><pre><br /></pre><pre><br /></pre><pre>#forrow($row in $matrix.rowNodes)$row.element.name</pre></td><td><pre>#forcol ($col in </pre><pre>$matrix.columns)#if(!$report.isEmpty($matrix.getCellDependencies($row,$col)))</pre><pre>-&gt;#end#endcol</pre></td><td colspan="1"><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><pre>#endrow</pre></td></tr><tr><td><pre>#endpage</pre></td><td><br /></td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189140342 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00372: Dependency Matrix toolbar

- page_id: `189140342`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189140342/Dependency+Matrix+toolbar
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:52.982+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Analysis diagrams > Dependency Matrix > Dependency Matrix environment
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

See the detailed descriptions of the dependency matrix toolbar buttons in the following table.

| Button | Button name | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Back (Alt+Left) | Navigates back to the previous diagram that was open in the current diagram tab. |
|  | Forward (Atl+Right) | Navigates forward to the diagram in the current diagram tab. |
|  | Select in Containment Tree (Alt+B) | Opens the in the and selects the current diagram or symbol in the Containment tree. |
|  | Delete (Ctrl+D) | Deletes the selected element from the matrix and model.If you undo the deletion, you should refresh the matrix to make the deleted elements appear in the matrix again. |
|  | Remove From Matrix (Delete) | Removes the selected element from the matrix. The selected element is not removed from the model. |
|  | Change Axes | Transposes rows and columns. |
|  | Export | Exports a matrix as a Comma Separated Values ( .csv) file. The file can be opened with Microsoft Excel. |
|  | Refresh | Refreshes the matrix after updating the criteria or data. |
|  | Validate Diagram | Validates the matrix against all validation rules stored in the model, except those that are ignored. |
|  | Options | Expands the menu with other options. |
|  | Specification - opens the matrix Specification window wherein you can specify matrix properties.Legend Location - lists legend location options and selection to not show it.Include Additional Content - includes additional content of elements that fall within the specified row/column scope. For example, if a Smart Package comes within the scope of the dependency matrix, its content is collected and represented in the matrix. |  |
|  | Suppress/Expand Criteria Area | Suppresses or expands the matrix Criteria area. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

tree.</p></td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></td><td><p>Delete (Ctrl+D)</p></td><td><p>Deletes the selected element from the matrix and model.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="69000517-6a46-447b-8863-24bdb19a77a5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you undo the deletion, you should refresh the matrix to make the deleted elements appear in the matrix again.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_from_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></td><td>Remove From <span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Matrix (Delete)</span></td><td><p>Removes the selected element from the matrix. The selected element is not removed from the model.</p></td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Change_axes.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Change Axes</td><td>Transposes rows and columns.</td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Save_as_cvs.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Export</td><td>Exports a matrix as a Comma Separated Values ( .csv) file. The <span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">file can be opened with Microsoft Excel.</span></td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh_button.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Refresh</td><td>Refreshes the matrix after updating the criteria or data.</td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="validate_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></td><td colspan="1">Validate Diagram</td><td colspan="1">Validates the matrix against all validation rules stored in the model, except those that are ignored.</td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></td><td>Options</td><td><p>Expands the menu with other options.</p></td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;"> </td><td colspan="2"><ul><li><strong>Specification</strong> - opens the matrix Specification window wherein you can specify matrix properties.</li><li><strong>Legend Location</strong> - l<span>ists legend location options and selection to not show it.</span></li><li><strong>Include Additional Content</strong> - includes additional content of elements that fall within the specified row/column scope. For example, if a Smart Package comes within the scope of the dependency matrix, its content is collected and represented in the matrix.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Suppress_expand_crit_area.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td colspan="1"><p>Suppress/Expand Criteria Area</p></td><td colspan="1">Suppresses or expands the matrix <strong>Criteria</strong> area.</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141295 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00373: Deployment

- page_id: `189141295`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141295/Deployment
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:45:24.082+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Artifact
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

907499974

907499976

907499975

The Deployment Specification is a type of Artifact. The Deployment Specification is a general mechanism to parameterize a Deployment relationship.

To draw the Deployment Specification on the diagram pane

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Component diagram' space=''] (or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Deployment diagram' space=''] ) diagram toolbar, click the Deployment Diagram button group to access the drop-down menu.
2. In the opened list, select Deployment Specification Instance and draw it on your diagram pane.

To specify the Deployment relationship

1. Create the Deployment Specification on the diagram pane.
2. Between the Deployment relationship and Deployment Specification, draw the Dependency relationship. The Dependency is drawn without an arrow. [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-23 at 5.52.38 PM.png']

907499973

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Component diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Deployment diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Artifacts' space='CBMP2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with dependencies' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189140227 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00374: Deployment diagram

- page_id: `189140227`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189140227/Deployment+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:47.233+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > UML diagrams
- labels: ['implementation', 'implementation-diagram']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

308620457

308620459

308620458

**Overview**

A Deployment diagram falls under the structural diagramming family. It focuses on showing the physical layout of various hardware components ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Node' space='']), e.g. CPU, a database server, and an application server that compose a system as well as the distribution of executable programs (software components) on hardware. For instance, to describe a website, the Deployment diagram would show what hardware components exist, what software components run, and how the different pieces are interconnected. Deployment diagrams are crucial when dealing with distributed systems.

**Purpose**

Using a Deployment diagram, you can show the actual computers and devices (Nodes), along with the connections they have to each other, thus specifying a system topology. Inside the Nodes, executable [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Component' space=''] and objects are located in a way that it shows where the software units are residing and on which Nodes they are executed. You may also show dependencies between Components.

**Usage**

A Deployment diagram can be used to:

- show the structure of the run-time system
- capture the hardware that is going to be used to implement the system and links between different hardware items
- model physical hardware elements and the communication paths between them
- plan the system architecture
- document the deployment of software components or nodes.

**Summary**

Deployment diagrams are valuable because they can be:

- used to model the hardware platform for a system
- used to identify hardware capabilities that have an impact on the performance planning and software configuration.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of a Deployment diagram

308620456

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='']

It focuses on showing the physical layout of various hardware components (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Node" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Nodes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>), e.g. CPU, a database server, and an application server that compose a system as well as the distribution of executable programs (software components) on hardware. For instance, to describe a website, the Deployment diagram would show what hardware components exist, what software components run, and how the different pieces are interconnected. Deployment diagrams are crucial when dealing with distributed systems.</p><p><strong>Purpose</strong></p><p>Using a Deployment diagram, you can show the actual computers and devices (Nodes), along with the connections they have to each other, thus specifying a system topology. Inside the Nodes, executable <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Component" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Components]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and objects are located in a way that it shows where the software units are residing and on which Nodes they are executed. You may also show dependencies between Components.</p><p><strong>Usage</strong></p><p>A Deployment diagram can be used to:</p><ul><li>show the structure of the run-time system</li><li>capture the hardware that is going to be used to implement the system and links between different hardware items</li><li>model physical hardware elements and the communication paths between them</li><li>plan the system architecture</li><li>document the deployment of software components or nodes. </li></ul><p><strong>Summary</strong></p><p>Deployment diagrams are valuable because they can be:</p><ul><li>used to model the hardware platform for a system</li><li>used to identify hardware capabilities that have an impact on the performance planning and software configuration. </li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="deployment_diagram_example.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of a Deployment diagram</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="292ab185-bc83-4d52-bf5f-8dc8aad28c4b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">308620456</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating diagrams" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00375: Deprecated JavaScript

- page_id: `189143249`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143249/Deprecated+JavaScript
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:39:51.913+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Validation > UML validation rules > Deprecated JavaScript rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Abbreviation**

JS

**Description**

This validation rule checks if the specified expression does not use Javascript Nashorn scripting language that is deprecated.

**Severity**

error

**Constrained Element**

Element

**Solvers**

- Change to JavaScript Rhino - changes the Javascript Nashorn scripting language to JavaScript Rhino.

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The *Parameter Direction and Type*Generic Table has a custom Column *Parameter Direction and Type using StringConcat* that implements a Javascript Nashorn script. 
The error is resolved by changing the scripting language to JavaScript Rhino via the **Change to JavaScript Rhino** solver.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Abbreviation</strong></p><p>JS</p><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>This validation rule checks if the specified expression does not use Javascript Nashorn scripting language that is deprecated.</p><p><strong>Severity</strong></p><p>error</p><p><strong>Constrained Element</strong></p><p>Element</p><p><strong>Solvers</strong></p><ul><li><strong>Change to JavaScript Rhino</strong> - changes the Javascript Nashorn scripting language to JavaScript Rhino.</li></ul><p><strong>Example</strong></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Deprecated JavaScript.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The <em>Parameter Direction and Type </em>Generic Table has a custom Column <em>Parameter Direction and Type using StringConcat</em> that implements a Javascript Nashorn script.<br />The error is resolved by changing the scripting language to JavaScript Rhino via the <strong>Change to JavaScript Rhino</strong> solver.</h6><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189145972 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00376: Deprecated JavaScript Nashorn

- page_id: `189145972`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189145972/Deprecated+JavaScript+Nashorn
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:42:08.175+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Macro Engine Documentation > Supported scripting languages
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

965417895

INLINE

965417916

INLINE

965417905

INLINE

The JavaScript Nashorn language has been removed from the modeling tool. You can learn more about deprecated JavaScript Nashorn [here](https://openjdk.java.net/jeps/335). Please use JavaScript Rhino instead. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='JavaScript migration from Nashorn to Rhino engine' space=''] page.

All JavaScript Nashorn usages need to be changed to JavaScript Rhino in projects to work correctly. Below are the recommended steps for achieving that:

1. Use one of the following options to run the modeling tool:
  - Proceeding with this option means that none of your project's JavaScript Nashorn scripts will work until they are changed to or rewritten in JavaScript Rhino.
  - Proceeding with this option means that your project's JavaScript Nashorn scripts will be executed using the JavaScript Rhino engine; thus, the majority of the scripts will work, but they will still need to be changed to or rewritten in JavaScript Rhino.
2. 
3. 

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Diagram showing the JavaScript Nashorn usages' change to JavaScript Rhino.

##### Running the modeling tool

###### Option 1. Run modeling tool without executing JavaScript Nashorn scripts (default)

Following this option means:

- The JavaScript Nashorn scripts found in the project are not executed, meaning that the model will not function as expected due to script failures.
- This is the default option; simply run the modeling tool and open a project containing JavaScript Nashorn usages.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']For example, if you open a table containing a custom column that has an expression with a JavaScript Nashorn script, an 'Expression evaluation failure' error notification is displayed.

###### Option 2. Run modeling tool executing JavaScript Nashorn scripts with JavaScript Rhino engine

Following this option means:

- The JavaScript Nashorn scripts found in the project are executed using the JavaScript Rhino engine. Due to the similarities between Rhino and Nashorn, most of the project's scripts are likely to work.
- To proceed with this option, you need to add an additional system property, as described below.

To enable JavaScript Nashorn script execution with JavaScript Rhino engine

1. Open the <modeling tool installation directory>\bin\<modeling tool name>.properties file.
2. Locate the JAVA_ARGS= line
3. Add the following system property: -Dexecute.legacy.javascript.as.rhino=true
4. Restart the modeling tool.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']For example, if you open a table containing a custom column that has an expression with a JavaScript Nashorn script, the 'JavaScript Nashorn (deprecated)' error notification is displayed.

##### Opening a project containing JavaScript Nashorn usages

Opening a project that contains JavaScript *Nashorn* scripts immediately displays a notification about the removal of the JavaScript *Nashorn* scripting language. The notification is displayed only once upon the first opening of the project with the new version of the tool. You can click the **Find Usages**link to run the **Deprecated JavaScript** validation suite to locate all usages of JavaScript Nashorn (for more information, see the section below)*.*

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### **Finding and resolving JavaScript Nashorn**usages**

The recommended steps to find and resolve JavaScript Nashorn usages:

1. Run the Deprecated JavaScript validation suite to .
2. Evaluate each script failing the validation and rewrite it in JavaScript Rhino if necessary. Due to the similarities between JavaScript Rhino and JavaScript Nashorn, most of the project's Nashorn scripts are likely to work with the Rhino engine. However, a review is recommended as to whether the Nashorn scripts' execution with the Rhino engine are accurate. If not, the Nashorn script must be modified to work in Rhino.
3. via the **Change to JavaScript Rhino**solver.

###### Finding JavaScript Nashorn**usages

To locate all usages of JavaScript Nashorn in a project, we recommend running the**Deprecated JavaScript**validation suite (**Analyze**>**Validation**>**Validate**). The validation suite contains the **Deprecated JavaScript** validation rule, which:

- detects all JavaScript Nashorn**usages;
- allows you to change the JavaScript Nashorn**language to JavaScript Rhino**for implemented expressions via the **Change to JavaScript Rhino**solver.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### After running the **Deprecated JavaScript** validation suite, hold Ctrl+A to select all detected instances of JavaScript *Nashorn* and resolve them via the **Change to JavaScript Rhino** solver in the Validation Results panel.

For a detailed description of the validation rule, see the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Deprecated JavaScript' space='']** page.

###### Resolving JavaScript Nashorn**usages

The **Change to JavaScript Rhino**solver changes the used scripting language without affecting the script itself. In many cases, changing the used scripting language will not affect the functionality of the script. Otherwise, the 'Expression evaluation failure' error notification (shown below) will appear, requiring you to rewrite the script in JavaScript Rhino.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="76c40243-6bcb-4aa7-982c-d6bf34474681"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">965417895</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="df36ab11-b297-44d1-831d-bfa188e162ca"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">965417916</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3947d9fd-b60f-4e41-aee4-b0123a7f849b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">965417905</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="823a0d75-ba18-4882-8e57-2f5a7658ce80"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The JavaScript Nashorn language has been removed from the modeling tool. Run modeling tool without executing JavaScript Nashorn scripts (default)</h4><p>Following this option means:</p><ul><li>The JavaScript Nashorn scripts found in the project are not executed, meaning that the model will not function as expected due to script failures.</li><li>This is the default option; simply run the modeling tool and open a project containing JavaScript Nashorn usages.</li></ul><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="expression_evaluation_error.png" /></ac:image>For example, if you open a table containing a custom column that has an expression with a JavaScript Nashorn script, an 'Expression evaluation failure' error notification is displayed.</h6><h4>Option 2. Run modeling tool executing JavaScript Nashorn scripts with JavaScript Rhino engine</h4><p>Following this option means:</p><ul><li>The JavaScript Nashorn scripts found in the project are executed using the JavaScript Rhino engine. Due to the similarities between Rhino and Nashorn, most of the project's scripts are likely to work.</li><li>To proceed with this option, you need to add an additional system property, as described below.</li></ul><p>To enable JavaScript Nashorn script execution with JavaScript Rhino engine</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\bin\&lt;modeling tool name&gt;.properties</em> file.</li><li>Locate the <em>JAVA_ARGS=</em> line</li><li>Add the following system property:<br /><em>-Dexecute.legacy.javascript.as.rhino=true</em></li><li><span>Restart the modeling tool.</span></li></ol><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Deprecated_Nashorn_error.png" /></ac:image>For example, if you open a table containing a custom column that has an expression with a JavaScript Nashorn script, the 'JavaScript Nashorn (deprecated)' error notification is displayed.</h6><h3>Opening a project containing JavaScript Nashorn usages</h3><p>Opening a project that contains JavaScript <em>Nashorn</em> scripts immediately displays a notification about the removal of the JavaScript <em>Nashorn</em> scripting language. The notification is displayed only once upon the first opening of the project with the new version of the tool. You can click the <strong>Find Usages </strong>link to run the <strong>Deprecated JavaScript</strong> validation suite to locate all usages of JavaScript Nashorn (for more information, see the <ac:link><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Finding and resolving JavaScript Nashorn usages]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> section below)<em>.</em></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Discontinued_Nashorn_notification.png" /></ac:image></p><h3><strong>Finding and resolving <span>JavaScript Nashorn<em> </em>usages</span></strong></h3><p>The recommended steps to find and resolve JavaScript Nashorn usages:</p><ol><li class="auto-cursor-target">Run the <strong>Deprecated JavaScript</strong><span> validation suite to <ac:link><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[find all JavaScript Nashorn usages]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li class="auto-cursor-target"><p>Evaluate each script failing the validation and rewrite it in JavaScript Rhino if necessary.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="00e77b0b-72b5-4a81-87c2-56b74adc6f65"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Due to the similarities between JavaScript Rhino and JavaScript Nashorn, most of the project's Nashorn scripts are likely to work with the Rhino engine. However, a review is recommended as to whether the Nashorn scripts' execution with the Rhino engine are accurate. If not, the Nashorn script must be modified to work in Rhino.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:link><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Resolve the validation error]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> via the <span><strong style="text-align: left;">Change to JavaScript Rhino </strong>solver.</span></li></ol><h4>Finding JavaScript Nashorn<em> </em>usages</h4><p class="auto-cursor-target">To locate all <span>usages of JavaScript Nashorn in a project, we recommend running the </span><strong>Deprecated JavaScript</strong><span> validation suite (</span><strong>Analyze</strong><span> &gt; </span><strong>Validation</strong><span> &gt; </span><strong>Validate</strong><span>). The validation suite contains the <strong>Deprecated JavaScript</strong> validation rule, which:</span></p><ul><li><span> detects all JavaScript Nashorn<em> </em>usages;</span></li><li><span>allows you to change the JavaScript Nashorn<em> </em>language to JavaScript Rhino<em> </em>for implemented expressions via the <strong style="text-align: left;">Change to JavaScript Rhino </strong>solver.</span></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="validation_results.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span>After running the <strong>Deprecated JavaScript</strong> validation suite, hold Ctrl+A to select all detected instances of JavaScript <em>Nashorn</em> and resolve them via the <strong>Change to JavaScript Rhino</strong> solver in the Validation Results panel.</span></h6><p><span>For a detailed description of the validation rule, see the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Deprecated JavaScript" /></ac:link></strong> page.</span></p><h4>Resolving JavaScript Nashorn<em> </em>usages</h4><p><span>The <strong style="text-align: left;">Change to JavaScript Rhino </strong>solver changes the used scripting language without affecting the script itself. In many cases, changing the used scripting language will not affect the functionality of the script. Otherwise, the 'Expression evaluation failure' error notification (shown below) will appear, requiring you to rewrite the script in JavaScript Rhino.</span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Expression_evaluation_failure.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````
