ac:name="id">1427255413</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1bd6197d-9018-4098-9db2-b5377972a0c7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1427255415</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="94a8a1d2-eeaf-4d65-8dc0-e060e9ee18dc"><ac:parameter issue.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d4602c53-adae-4ca0-9514-aa04dc3c211f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Useful information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you disable inbound network activity, double-clicking a project file (.mdzip) opens the project in a new modeling tool window.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To disable inbound network activity</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\bin</em> and open the properties file, e.g., <em>&lt;modeling tool name&gt;.properties</em>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the JAVA_ARGS line, add the following argument:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7702a6ab-72a0-4154-bcc8-c64d588df4f1"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[-DDISABLEINBOUNDCON=true]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Save and close the file.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ri:content-title="Getting started" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Most common shortcut keys" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customizing environment options" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customizing and Selecting Perspective" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Configuration files" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Support_obsolete" ri:space-key="NMDOC" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=216268953 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00393: Disconnected team collaboration

- page_id: `216268953`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/216268953/Disconnected+team+collaboration
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-03-04T15:14:33.712+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can collaborate with people working on different Teamwork Cloud and **3D**EXPERIENCE platform instances by using the server project file format (.szip). When a project is exported to a server project file, you can use it to update an existing project or import it as a new project on another server. Server project files can be used in the following way:

- To import projects from one Teamwork Cloud server to another Teamwork Cloud server.
- To import projects from the Teamwork Cloud server to the 3D EXPERIENCE platform.
- To import projects from one 3D EXPERIENCE platform to another 3D EXPERIENCE platform.

You cannot open server project files locally. To view the contents of a server project file, you must import it to the server as a new project or use it to update an existing project.

For more information, see the following topics:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can collaborate with people working on different Teamwork Cloud and <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform instances by using the server project file format (.szip). When a project is exported to a server project file, you can use it to update an existing project or import it as a new project on another server. Server project files can be used in the following way:</p><ul><li>To import projects from one Teamwork Cloud server to another Teamwork Cloud server.</li><li>To import projects from the Teamwork Cloud server to the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform.</li><li>To import projects from one <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform to another <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6bbff92c-4d1e-40a0-9efe-709b8046ce9f"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">You cannot open server project files locally. To view the contents of a server project file, you must import it to the server as a new project or use it to update an existing project.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For more information, see the following topics:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f99710af-594e-43f5-8d82-e2f2aeae2c77" /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189142388 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00394: Displaying and navigating through changes

- page_id: `189142388`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189142388/Displaying+and+navigating+through+changes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:39:11.680+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools > Model Merge > Analyzing and managing merge results
- labels: ['displaying-changes', 'merged-results-navigation']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1421694493

1421694496

1421694494

You can customize the content of the **Merged Result** tree/list as well as navigate through changes in both the **Merged Results** tree/list and the **Specification** panel using the toolbar that is located at the top of the **Merge** window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The toolbar buttons for customizing the **Merged** **Results** tree/list and navigating through changes are described in the following tables.

| Button / Shortcut keys | Tooltip text | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Accept the selected change and go to the next change | Click to accept the selected element change and go to the next change (might be a property or the element). |
|  | Accept the selected change, its property changes and go to next change | Click to accept the selected element change together with the element property changes and go to the next changed element. |
|  | Accept the selected change, its property changes, all subelement changes and go to next change | Click to accept all changes from both contributors starting from the selected element and go to the next changed element (which is not a subelement). For example, if the root package Model selected, then all changes for the whole project will be accepted. |
|  | Reject the selected change and go to the next change | Click to reject the selected element change and go to the next change (might be a property or the element). |
|  | Reject the selected change, its property changes and go to next change | Click to reject the selected element change together with the element property changes and go to the next changed element. |
|  | Reject the selected change, its property changes, all subelement changes and go to next change | Click to reject all changes from both contributors starting from the selected element and go to the next changed element (which is not a subelement). For example, if the root package Model is selected, then all changes for the whole project will be rejected. |
| Alt + Home | Go To First Change | Click to select the first change.The button is not available if the first change is already selected. |
| Alt + Up arrow | Go To Previous Change | Click to select the previous change. |
| Alt + Down arrow | Go To Next Change | Click to select the next change. |
| Alt + End | Go To Last Change | Click to select the last change. The button is not available if the last change is already selected. |
| Alt + Page Down | Go To Next Conflict | Click to select the next conflict. The button is not available if there are no conflicts. |
|  | Annotate Merged Diagram | Click to turn on showing merged diagram annotations. |
|  | Report | Click to generate a Project Merge differences report in Microsoft Word format. |
| Options menu |  |  |
| Icon | Option | Description |
| > | Filter | Click to open the Select Element Type dialog. Clear the checkbox next to an element that you want to hide in the Merged Result tree/list. |
| > | Show Elements from Used projects | Click to show or hide elements from used projects in the Merged Result tree/list. |
| Show Source | Click to show or hide Source column in the Specification panel. By default, this column is shown. |  |
| Show Ancestor | Click to show or hide Ancestor column in the Specification panel. By default, this column is hidden. |  |
| Show Target | Click to show or hide Target column in the Specification panel. By default, this column is shown. |  |
| Show Merge Result Preview | Click to show or hide Show Merge Result Preview column in the Specification panel. This column shows final changes in the merged project and by default, this column is shown. |  |
| Summary/Legend | Click to show or hide the Summary/Legend panel. |  |
| Reset Windows Configuration | Select to place all tabs and windows to their default positions. |  |

1421694492

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189143220 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00395: Displaying and navigating through differences

- page_id: `189143220`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143220/Displaying+and+navigating+through+differences
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:39:48.829+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Comparing projects > Analyzing comparison results
- labels: ['displaying-changes', 'comparison-result-navigation']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can customize the content of the **Differences** tree as well as navigate through differences in both the **Difference**tree and the **Specification** panel using the toolbar that is located at the top of the **Difference Viewer**window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The toolbar buttons for customizing the **Differences** tree and navigating through differences are described in the following tables.

| Button / Shortcut keys | Tooltip text | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Expand | Click to expand all nodes in the Differences tree. |
|  | Collapse | Click to collapse all nodes in the Differences tree. |
| Alt+Home | Go To First Change | Click to select the first change. The button is not available, if the first change is already selected. |
| Alt+ up arrow | Go To Previous Change | Click to select the previous change. |
| Alt + down arrow | Go To Next Change | Click to select the next change. |
| Alt+End | Go To Last Change | Click to select the last change. The button is not available, if the last change is already selected. |
|  | Report | Click to generate a project comparison differences report in Microsoft Word format. |
| > | Filter | Click to open the Items Filter dialog. Clear the check-box next to an element that you need to hide in the Differences tree. |
| > | Reset Windows Configuration | Click to place all tabs and windows to their default positions. |

You can also filter the elements displayed in the **Differences** tree by change type. Use the **Display** drop-down list to specify a change type. Be advised that filtering elements by a particular change type displays both elements with the particular changes and elements with the particular owned element changes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE


<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141092 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00396: Displaying and suppressing compartments on shapes

- page_id: `189141092`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141092/Displaying+and+suppressing+compartments+on+shapes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:37:49.634+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Compartments
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To display or suppress compartments, do one of the following

- On the diagram, click the shape and click the *Compartments* smart manipulator. Then select or cancel the selection of the particular compartment, for example, Attributes, Operations. The Compartments smart manipulator exists on shapes that have compartment boxes. For example, pins, and initial nodes do not have the Compartments smart manipulator.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- On the diagram, select a shape and click the Suppress <compartment name> smart manipulator. [ATTACHMENT filename='supress_attributes.png']
- On the diagram, right-click the shape and select Symbol Properties . Then select or cancel the selection of the Suppress <compartment name> check box.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To display or suppress compartments, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li><p>On the diagram, click the shape and click the <em>Compartments</em> smart manipulator. Then select or cancel the selection of the particular compartment, for example, Attributes, Operations.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9656a37f-5122-411f-9ed7-4b5acae41ad2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Compartments smart manipulator exists on shapes that have compartment boxes. For example, pins, and initial nodes do not have the Compartments smart manipulator.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="compartment_smart_manipulator.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>On the diagram, select a shape and click the <em>Suppress &lt;compartment name&gt;</em> smart manipulator. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="supress_attributes.png" /></ac:image></li><li>On the diagram, right-click the shape and select <strong>Symbol Properties</strong>. Then select or cancel the selection of the <strong>Suppress </strong>&lt;compartment name&gt; check box.</li></ul><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189143418 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00397: Displaying dependency details

- page_id: `189143418`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143418/Displaying+dependency+details
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:40:12.698+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Model Visualizer > Package Dependency Diagram Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

637548468

637548470

637548469

In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package diagram' space='MD190'], the virtual dependencies (dependency links) are created. All virtual dependencies are stored in the **Virtual dependencies** package, which you can find in the model browser.

You can use the **Show Dependency Details** command to see the dependencies of a particular package. Dependency results are displayed in the dependency details panel (see the following example).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To display the dependency details

1. In the Model Browser or on the diagram pane, right-click the dependency with the details you want to display.
2. In the shortcut menu, select Related Elements > Show Dependency Details . The dependency details panel opens.

637548467

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package diagram' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a5a1a12c-b458-495c-af4e-26afa8b4cf54"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">637548468</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1628e6f6-9b5f-4f4e-892d-81bd055313d7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">637548470</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="191e2aa5-76bd-485a-8db1-a75512a9e7c3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">637548469</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD190" ri:content-title="Package diagram" /></ac:link>, the virtual dependencies (dependency links) are created. All virtual dependencies are stored in the <strong>Virtual dependencies</strong> package, which you can find in the model browser.</p><p>You can use the <strong>Show Dependency Details</strong> command to see the dependencies of a particular package. Dependency results are displayed in the dependency details panel (see the following example).</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Package_diagram_dep.details.png" /></ac:image></p><p>To display the dependency details</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Model Browser or on the diagram pane, right-click the dependency with the details you want to display.</li><li>In the shortcut menu, select <strong>Related Elements</strong> &gt; <strong>Show Dependency Details</strong>. The dependency details panel opens.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fe535a32-a1d5-49ac-99f9-f22b8fde4159"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">637548467</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Dependency" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Package diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189140382 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00398: Displaying element numbering

- page_id: `189140382`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189140382/Displaying+element+numbering
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:55.767+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Analysis diagrams > Relation Map
- labels: ['relation-map-element-numbering']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To show element numbering

1. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='options_button.png'] in the Diagram toolbar.
2. Select the Show Element Numbers option.

The element numbering appears before its name.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To show element numbering</p><hr /><ol><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Options" ac:alt="Options"><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the Diagram toolbar.</li><li>Select the <strong>Show Element Numbers </strong>option.</li></ol><p>The element numbering appears before its name.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Element numbering in the relation map" ac:alt="Element numbering in the relation map"><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_element_numbering.png" /></ac:image></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189140873 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00399: Displaying elements

- page_id: `189140873`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189140873/Displaying+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:37:16.956+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

210053124

210053139

210053129

The modeling tool includes a number of display commands that allow you to represent elements in different diagrams. However, displaying elements is only possible if they are already created somewhere in the model.The display commandsare interactive, and depend upon the selection on the diagram pane. The table below lists all of the commands which are available, and a short description of each. The table also provides a link to a more in depth description of its functionality, and lists the diagrams in which they can be used.

| Command names | Description | Usage in diagrams |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Display Related Elements | Opens the Display Related Elements dialog in which you can specify the relation type(s), their direction, scope, depth, and other options. All specified criteria define which elements and relations will be displayed on the diagram pane of the selected element. ]]> | All diagrams |
| Display Paths | Displays paths among shapes that already exist in the model. ]]> | All diagrams |
| Display Internal Structure | Displays internal structure inside a selected classifier shape that contains its own internal structure. ]]> | All diagrams |
| Display Parts/Ports | Opens the Display Parts/Ports dialog from where you can specify the exact properties and/or ports you want to display on the diagram pane. Read more > | Class, Composite Structure, Component, Package, Deployment, Object diagrams |
| Hide All Ports | Hides all ports of the selected shape(s) or diagram frame. ]]> | Class, Composite Structure, Component, Package, Deployment, Object diagrams |
| Display Inner Elements | Displays internal elements and paths inside selected shape or diagram frame. There are different procedures when trying to display inner elements in:The Package diagram. ]]>The Activity diagram. ]]>The State Machine diagram. ]]> | All diagrams |
| Display Provided/Required Interfaces | Displays provided or required interfaces of the selected port shape. ]]> | Class, Composite Structure, Component, Package, Deployment, Object diagrams |
| Display Pins | Opens the Select Pins dialog from where you can select the exact pins you want to display on the selected shape. ]]> | Activity diagram |
| Display Parameter Nodes | Opens the Select Parameters dialog from where you can select exactly which Activity Parameter Nodes you want to display on the frame of the Activity Diagram. ]]> | Activity diagram |
| Display Expansion Nodes | Displays the input or output Expansion Nodes on the Expansion Region border. ]]> | Activity diagram |
| Display Points | Opens the Select Points dialog in which you can select which Connection Point References you want to display on a submachine State shape. ]]> | State Machine diagram |
| Display Lifelines | Opens the Display Lifelines dialog from where you can select which lifelines you want to display. ]]> | Sequence diagramCommunication diagram |
| Display Messages | Opens the Select Messages dialog from where you can select which messages of the selected connector you want to display. ]]> | Communication diagram |

210053122

**Related pages**

****

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189142087 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00400: Displaying icon or image

- page_id: `189142087`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189142087/Displaying+icon+or+image
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:38:50.579+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Formatting symbols
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can display icon or image on the symbol or instead of the symbol.

The descriptions given in the following table will help you to understand this functionality better.

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Stereotype | Every element can be extended by applying a . For more information about applying stereotype properties, see . |
| Icon | Icon of stereotype.Icon is a small image displayed in the top-right corner of shape.To assign an icon to a stereotype in the window, define the Icon property. |
| Text | Stereotype name, displayed on the symbol. |
| Image | Image which can be assigned to element and displayed as icon or instead of element shape. To assign image to element in the element , assign the Image property. |

To change the icon visibility mode on the element shape, do one of the following

- Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='']**Symbol Properties** dialog and in the Show Stereotypes property specification cell, select the icon visibility mode.
- Select an element shape and click the Compartments button. From the menu, select Stereotypes and choose the icon visibility mode.

The stereotype visibility modes are described in the following table:

| Mode | Example | Icon and name visibility | Image visibility |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Icon and Text |  | Icon of stereotype displayed.Name of stereotype displayed. | Image of element is displayed in the corner of shape. |
| Icon |  | Icon of stereotype displayed in the corner of symbol.Name of stereotype is not displayed. | Image of element is displayed in the corner of shape. |
| Text Only |  | Icon of stereotype is not displayed.Name of stereotype is displayed. | Image of element is not displayed. |
| Shape Image and Text* |  | Icon of stereotype is displayed instead of shape.Name of stereotype is displayed. | Image of element is displayed instead of shape. |
| Shape Image* |  | Icon of stereotype is displayed instead of shape.Name of stereotype is not displayed. | Image of element is displayed instead of the shape. |
| Do Not Display |  | Icon of stereotype is not displayed.Name of stereotype is not displayed. | Image of element is not displayed. |

* - When the **Shape Image and Text** or **Shape Image** property is selected, all symbol compartmentsshould be suppressed in orderto display the icon of a stereotype instead of the element symbol. If the symbol contains port or pins, these ports or pins are displayed around that symbol.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Shape Image and Text** and **Shape Image** properties are not added to the **Path** element properties list.

If element has assigned both - image and stereotype icon - then image of element will be displayed on the shape.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

To assign image to element in the element <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>, assign the Image property.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p> </p><p>To change the icon visibility mode on the element shape, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>Open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Symbol Properties dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> and in the <strong>Show Stereotypes</strong> property specification cell, select the icon visibility mode.</li><li>Select an element shape and click the <em>Compartments</em> button. From the menu, select <strong>Stereotypes</strong> and choose the icon visibility mode.</li></ul><p> </p><p>The stereotype visibility modes are described in the following Text</td><td><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_st_1.png" /></ac:image></td><td><p>Icon of stereotype displayed.</p><p>Name of stereotype displayed.</p></td><td><p>Image of element is displayed in the corner of shape.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Icon</td><td><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_st_2.png" /></ac:image></td><td><p>Icon of stereotype displayed in the corner of symbol.</p><p>Name of stereotype is not displayed.</p></td><td><p>Image of element is displayed in the corner of shape.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Text Only</td><td><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_st_3.png" /></ac:image></td><td><p>Icon of stereotype is not displayed.</p><p>Name of stereotype is displayed.</p></td><td><p>Image of element is not displayed.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Shape Image <br />and Text*</p></td><td><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_st_4.png" /></ac:image></td><td><p>Icon of stereotype is displayed instead of shape.</p><p>Name of stereotype is displayed.</p></td><td><p>Image of element is displayed instead of shape.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Shape Image*</td><td><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_st_5.png" /></ac:image></td><td><p>Icon of stereotype is displayed instead of shape.</p><p>Name of stereotype is not displayed.</p></td><td><p>Image of element is displayed instead If the symbol contains port or pins, these ports or pins are displayed around that symbol.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ports_pins_tip.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0ea9a5db-aefc-41f6-b9b7-637f4c072eb5"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Shape Image and Text</strong> and <strong>Shape Image</strong> properties are not added to the <strong>Path</strong> element properties list.</p><p>If element has assigned both - image and stereotype icon - then image of element will be displayed on the shape.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e69d02c3-e690-401a-905b-f90fd93cdb1d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="84709ea2-4b5c-4f4b-8c48-c07a718d63dc"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Formatting symbols" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189140892 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00401: Displaying internal structure on structured classifiers

- page_id: `189140892`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189140892/Displaying+internal+structure+on+structured+classifiers
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:37:17.788+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Displaying elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

567827949

567827951

567827950

You can represent the internal structure of the structured classifier on the diagram pane in all diagrams. The internal structure of the selected shape can be represented only if the selected element contains its own internal structure. For example, in a Composite Structure diagram on a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part' space=''] shape or in a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space=''] on a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space=''] shape, you can display its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space=''], parts, and connectors.

The following procedure will show you how to display the internal structure of a selected classifier.

To display the internal structure of a selected classifier

1. On the diagram pane, select the classifier shape in which you want to display the internal structure.
2. Do one of the following: 
- Open the selected shape's shortcut menu, click **Display** > **Display Internal Structure**, select the name of the classifier, and then select a diagram containing the internal structure. 
- On the diagram toolbar click [IMAGE alt='' src=''], choose the **Display Internal Structure** command, select the name of the classifier, and then select a diagram containing the internal structure. After those steps are completed, the internal structure is displayed inside the selected classifier (see figure below).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

567827948

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying elements' space='']

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the **Composite structure diagram** sample model. To open this sample do one of the following:

- Download [ATTACHMENT filename='composite structure diagram.mdzip'] .
- Find in modeling tool <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\diagrams\composite structure diagram.mdzip.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="759be926-130e-4bd8-bad4-e29b6b07ae8f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">567827949</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="40b236d7-1746-41c5-b0c3-35bc7dca5af6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">567827951</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="250a1156-d994-436c-b302-70638f345c96"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">567827950</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can represent the internal structure of the structured classifier on the diagram pane in all diagrams. The internal structure of the selected shape can be represented only if the selected element contains its own internal structure. For example, in a Composite Structure diagram on a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Part" /></ac:link> shape or in a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Class diagram" /></ac:link> on a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Class" /></ac:link> shape, you can display its <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, parts, and connectors.</p><p>The following procedure will show you how to display the internal structure of a selected classifier.</p><p>To display the internal structure of a selected classifier</p><hr /><ol><li>On the diagram pane, select the classifier shape in which you want to display the internal structure.</li><li><p>Do one of the following:<br />- Open the selected shape's shortcut menu, click <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong>Display Internal Structure</strong>, select the name of the classifier, and then select a diagram containing the internal structure.<br />- On the diagram toolbar click <ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png" /></ac:image>, choose the <strong>Display Internal Structure</strong> command, select the name of the classifier, and then select a diagram containing the internal structure.</p><p>After those steps are completed, the internal structure is displayed inside the selected classifier (see figure below).</p></li></ol><p><ac:image ac:title="The Room Class owns an internal structure which is displayed on its shape."><ri:attachment ri:filename="class_with_internal_structure.png" /></ac:image></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c7683b79-f0ba-4b88-9e75-90a004979b99"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">567827948</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying elements" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The model used in the figures of this page is the <strong>Composite structure diagram</strong> sample model. To open this sample do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Download <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="composite structure diagram.mdzip"><ri:page ri:content-title="Sample models" /></ri:attachment></ac:link>.</li><li>Find in modeling tool <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples\diagrams\composite structure diagram.mdzip.</em></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141095 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00402: Displaying or hiding elements in compartments on shapes

- page_id: `189141095`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141095/Displaying+or+hiding+elements+in+compartments+on+shapes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:37:49.972+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Compartments
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use the **Compartment Edit**dialog to control the list of elements and properties visible in compartments on shapes. You can display or hide stereotypes, constraints, tagged values, attributes, operations, and others. If there are hidden properties on the boxable compartment, the **...** is displayed.

You can also display or hide the element specification properties, such as *Name*, *Is Active*, *Owner*, *To Do* in the **Compartment Edit**dialog under the **Element Properties** tab.

To open the **Compartment Edit** dialog from the diagram pane

- Select a shape and click the Compartments smart manipulator. On the menu, click the arrow to expand and then select Edit Compartments .
- Right-click a single or any number of shapes or classifier compartments (e.g. Properties, Operations, Receptions, Ports, and etc.), and from the shortcut menu, select **Edit Compartments**. Compartment Edit dialog of multiple shapesWhen you edit compartments of more than one shape at once, the**Compartment Edit** dialog works with the following rules:- If you select multiple shapes of the same element, the **Compartment Edit** dialog is the same as for a single shape, but changes are applied for all selected shapes after clicking **OK**. 
 - If you select multiple shapes of different elements, the **Compartment Edit** dialog shows a union of compartments and their items. 
 - If you select multiple shapes, but at least one of them hides the common compartment item while another shows it, the **Compartment Edit** dialog displays that item in italics in the **Element Properties** tab > Selected area. If you move the conflicting item to the Hidden area, it becomes non-italicized, and changes are applied for both shapes after clicking **OK**.
- On the Note shape, click the Edit Element Properties smart manipulator.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Use the <strong>Compartment Edit </strong>dialog to control the list of elements and properties visible in compartments on shapes. You can display or hide stereotypes, constraints, tagged values, attributes, operations, and others. If there are hidden properties on the boxable compartment, the <strong>...</strong> is displayed.</p><p>You can also display or hide the element specification properties, such as <em>Name</em>, <em>Is Active</em>, <em>Owner</em>, <em>To Do</em> in the <strong>Compartment Edit </strong>dialog under the <strong>Element Properties</strong> tab.</p><p><br /></p><p>To open the <strong>Compartment Edit</strong> dialog from the diagram pane</p><hr /><ul><li>Select a shape and click the <em>Compartments</em> smart manipulator. On the menu, click the arrow to expand and then select <strong>Edit Compartments</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Right-click a single or any number of shapes or classifier compartments (e.g. Properties, Operations, Receptions, Ports, and etc.), and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Edit Compartments</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8be6e385-af39-4175-a17e-56adb613602a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Compartment Edit dialog of multiple shapes</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When you edit compartments of more than one shape at once, the<strong> Compartment Edit</strong> dialog works with the following rules:</p><p> - If you select multiple shapes of the same element, the <strong>Compartment Edit</strong> dialog is the same as for a single shape, but changes are applied for all selected shapes after clicking <strong>OK</strong>.<br /> - If you select multiple shapes of different elements, the <strong>Compartment Edit</strong> dialog shows a union of compartments and their items.<br /> - If you select multiple shapes, but at least one of them hides the common compartment item while another shows it, the <strong>Compartment Edit</strong> dialog displays that item in italics in the <strong>Element Properties</strong> tab &gt; Selected area. If you move the conflicting item to the Hidden area, it becomes non-italicized, and changes are applied for both shapes after clicking <strong>OK</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>On the Note shape, click the <em>Edit Element Properties</em> smart manipulator.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189140874 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00403: Displaying parts and ports

- page_id: `189140874`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189140874/Displaying+parts+and+ports
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:37:17.444+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Displaying elements
- labels: ['display-parts', 'display-ports', 'hide-ports']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Example 3

567711113

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Example 3

567711119

210127422

**On this page**

****

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Example 3

567711117

##### Introduction

You can display ports, properties, and deep nested properties that have already been created in all [ATTACHMENT filename='uml_diagrams.png'].This functionality is driven by stereotypes, which allow you to display properties on which [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space=''] have been applied.If you have applied your own [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space=''] for the elements (e.g., electrical, mechanical, optical), you can use those stereotypeswhen selecting the properties you want to display on the diagram pane.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

For more information about how to create custom stereotypes and apply them, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML Profiling and DSL Guide' space=''].

##### Ways to display parts and ports

You can display ports and properties in the following ways:

44

###### Using the Display Parts/Ports dialog

You can use the**Display Parts/Ports**dialog to control how much of a structure should be displayed. The **Display Parts/Ports**dialog****opens automatically when creating a new[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space=''] for a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space=''] element that owns properties. It can also be accessed and opened in thestructurediagrams that have already been created.The**Display Parts/Ports** dialog allows you to:

- Preview all [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part' space=''] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space=''] , and other properties in one place.
- Manually or automatically select required [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part' space=''] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space=''] , and other properties you want to display.
- Manually or automatically choose deeply [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Nesting parts that are association ends' space=''] [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part' space=''] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space=''] , and other properties you want to display.
- Define layout options for [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part' space=''] .
- Specify layout options for [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space=''] .

The **Display Parts/Ports**dialog consists of the following areas:

- *Dialog toolbar* allows you to show or hide additional information about listed elements.
- *Element tree* displays all owned internal structure of context [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space=''].
- *Panel* is a context-sensitive area thatdepends on the selected property from the Element tree. It allows you to batch select [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part' space='']/[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space='']Portsin the Element tree. You canselect all, clear all, or choose only properties by type, stereotype, or aspect.
- *Options* area allows you to choose the layout of the selected parts and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space='']when they appear in the diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

****

The following procedure will guide you in working with the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog when you want to display required properties on the diagram pane.

To display Ports and Properties using the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog

1. Open the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog:
  - When creating a new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space=''] , the dialog opens automatically.
  - When editing an already created [ATTACHMENT filename='uml_diagrams.png']structure diagram , the dialog can be opened in the following ways: - Select either a diagram frame or single/multiple element shapes, and, o n the diagram toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and choose **Display Parts/Ports**. - Right-click either a diagram frame or single/multiple element shapes, and select **Display** > **Display Parts/Ports**.
2. (Optional) Enable buttons from the Display Parts/Ports dialog toolbar if you want to show or hide additional information about listed elements:
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to show the full path of elements.
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to show the full types of elements.
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to show the inherited elements.
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to show the inherited properties.
3. Choose the properties you want to display by using one or more of the following methods: WarningIf some check boxesare inactive in the element list,those elements are already displayed in the diagram.
  - Use the element treelocated on the left side of the dialog:-Manually select individual check boxesone by one. 
- Select recursively nested elements by holding down the Shift key while selecting the corresponding check box for the element. Example 1All properties (*socket1, socket2, socket3, powerIn*) of the *Ee* Property are selected recursively by holding down the Shift key while selecting the corresponding check box from the element treein the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - Use the context-sensitive panellocated on the right side of the dialog:- **Select Properties** group - displays all property types (including your own stereotypes) that are owned by the selected element from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog. 
- **Select Ports** group - displays all Port types (including your own stereotypes) that are owned by a selected element from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog.- **Select All** - selects all check boxes that are expanded from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog. 
- **Clear All** - clears all check boxes, even those that are collapsed (deep nested), from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog. Example 2All ports (*socket1, socket2, socket3, powerIn*) of the *Ee* Property are selected in the element tree by using the **Select Ports** group in the context-sensitive panelof the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Specify the layout options in the Options area:
  - Parts layout: select the Layout Parts check box if you want the Part shapes to be arranged in the automatic layout.
  - Usage of layout template: select the Use Layout Template check box if you want the layout template to be applied. When this check box is selected, the Part, which is included in the layout template, and all its Ports from the layout template are selected automatically.
  - Showing connectors: select the Show Connectors check box if you want the connectors to be displayed.
  - Ports layout on Parts shape: - Top/Bottom - displays Ports on the Part shape from top to bottom. - Left/Right - displays Ports on the Part shape from left to right.
5. Click **OK**.

After those steps, all parts, ports, and other properties that were selected in the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog are displayed on the diagram pane.

###### Using the diagram toolbar and the shortcut menu

In the structure diagrams that have already been created, you can display [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Properties' space=''], and hide all [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space=''] by using the commands from:

- The Display button on the diagram toolbar.
- The **Display** command group under the shortcut menu.

You can also use the**Display** button and **Display** command group from the shortcut menu as their functions are identical. All those commandsare interactive and depend upon the selection on the diagram pane. For example, if the Part shape is selected on the diagram pane, both menu contains onlythe commands that can be applied to the selected Part shape. If you select nothing in the diagram pane, both the menu showscommands that can be applied to the whole diagram.

These commands under the **Display** button and shortcut menu are comprised ofelements that are still not displayed on the diagram according to their:

- Metatypes.
- Stereotypes.
- Types stereotypes.

The following procedures will show you how to:

- How to display Ports, Properties using the **Display** button.
- 
- 

**button**To display Ports, Properties using the **Display** button

1. Select either a diagram frame or single/multiple element shapes.
2. O n the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='Display.png'] . [ATTACHMENT filename='display_related_elements_button_MD.png']
3. From the opened command menu, select the required properties to display: 
 - All Ports that are available to display are listed under the **Display Ports** command group. ImportantIf nothing is selected on the diagram pane, the**Display Ports**command group will contain commands that allow you to display two levels of structure: ports are displayed on the diagram frame and on the first level parts. - All Properties that are available to display are listed under the **Display Properties** command group. After selecting the required command from the **Display** button, the appropriate properties are displayed on the diagram pane.

**shortcut**To display Ports, Properties using the shortcut menu

1. Right-click either the diagram pane or single/multiple element shapes.
2. From the shortcut menu, select the Display command group. [ATTACHMENT filename='display_command_group_MD.png']
3. From the opened command menu, select the required properties to display: 
 - All Ports that are available to display are listed under the **Display Ports** command group. ImportantIf nothing is selected on the diagram pane, the **Display Ports** command group allows you to display two levels of structure: ports are displayed on the diagram frame and on the first level parts. - All Properties that are available to display are listed under the **Display Properties** command group. 
After selecting the required command from the Display command menu, the appropriate properties, Ports are displayed on the diagram pane.

**hide**To hide Ports

- Select either a diagram pane or any number of element shapes, and, o n the diagram toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and choose**Hide All Ports**.
- Right-click either a diagram pane or any number of element shapes, and select **Display** >**Hide All Ports**. 
After selecting the command, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space='']of the selected shape will be hidden from the diagram pane but not removed from the model. WarningIf the **Hide All Ports** command doesn't appear, the selection or diagramdoesn't contain any [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space='']that can be hidden. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Using the smart manipulator toolbar

When the properties are already displayed on the[ATTACHMENT filename='uml_diagrams.png']structure diagrams, you can display their direct ports using the **Display All Ports** button from the smart manipulator toolbar. It does not allow you to display deeply nested ports, only first-level ports. To display deeply nested ports, you should use the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog. Follow the steps below to learn how to display ports of a selected property shape.

To display Ports on shapes

1. Select the required element shape or a diagram frame.
2. From the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. 
All first-level Ports of the selected shape will be displayed on the diagram pane. WarningIf the **Display All Ports** button is not available, the selected element or diagramdoesn't own any Port. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Using the drag-and-drop operationin the Composite Structure diagram

The most efficient way to create a new property and display it with all [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space=''] in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space=''] is by dragging the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space=''] or Property elements. This method does not allow you to display deeply nested properties, only first-level properties. To display deeply nested elements, use the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog. The following procedures will show you:

- 
- How to display a Property shape and all its Ports by dragging the Property element.

drag_classYou can create a new Property by dragging the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='']element from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='']Model Browserto the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space=''] pane. The newly created Property will be typed by the dragged [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space=''] and displayed on the diagram pane. If this [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space=''] has [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space=''] defined, you can display them by using the long drag and selecting an appropriate option from the opened menu (see Example 3).

****

To create and display a Property by dragging a Class element

- Drag a single or a multiple Class element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane.
- Drag a single or a multiple Class element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane, and hold it down until the Tooltip appears (long drag).Drop it, and from the opened menu, select the **Create Property Symbol**command. 
The new Property is created in the model and displayed on the diagram pane. The newly created Property is typed by the dragged Class automatically.

To create and display a Property and display all its Ports by dragging a Class element

- Drag a single or a multiple Class element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane, and hold it down until the Tooltip appears (long drag).
- Drop it, and, from the opened menu, select the **Create Property Symbol and Display All Ports** command. The new Property is created in the model and displayed with all its Ports on the diagram pane. The newly created Property is typed by the dragged Class automatically.

#### INFO: Tip

Tip

After creating an element, it is beneficial to find its location in the model tree by performing one of the following: 
 - Right-click the shape, and, from the shortcut menu, choose the **Select in Containment Tree** command. 
 - Select the shape and press Alt+B.

#### TIP: Example 3

Example 3

After selecting the **Create Property Symbol and Display All Ports** command anddragging (long drag) the Class element to *Internal Structure of Workplace* diagram pane, itcreates a new Property typed by a*Duplex Power Outlet*Class and displays all Ports on it.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

drag_propertyYou can display a Property shape by dragging a Property element from the Model Browser to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space=''] pane. If this property has [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space=''] defined, you can display them by using the long drag and selecting an appropriate option from the opened menu (see Example 4).

The following procedures describe how to display Property shape and all its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space=''] by dragging the Property element.

To display a Property by dragging a Property element

- Drag a single or a multiple Property element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane.
- Drag a single or a multiple Property element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane, and hold it down until the Tooltip appears (long drag) . Drop it, and from the opened menu, select the**Create Property Symbol**command. The Property shape is displayed on the diagram pane.

To display a Property with all of its Ports by dragging a Property element

- Drag a single or a multiple Property element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane, and hold it down until the Tooltip appears (long drag).
- Drop it, and, from the opened menu, select the**Create Property Symbol and Display All Ports** command. The Property shape is displayed with all its Ports on that shape.

After selecting the**Create Property Symbol and Display All Ports**command, and dragging (long drag) the Property element to*Internal Structure of Workplace*diagram, it displays Property shape and all Ports on it.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

After completing the steps outlined above, the Property shape and/or all its Ports are displayed on the Composite Structure diagram.

##### Displaying properties of a Port

If you display properties of a Port, all properties of its type will be displayed inside the Port shape and part compartments will be enabled for that Port.

To enable Part compartments on the Port shape

1. Select a Port shape whose type has properties.
2. Do one of the following:
  - O n the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='Display.png'] .
  - From the shortcut menu, point to the Display command group.
3. Select the required properties to display.

The properties are displayed inside the Port shape. Part compartments are enabled for the Port.

The example below shows the *p1* Port both before and after displaying its type properties. The properties displayed on the Port shape are the *Pressure Status* Flow Property and the *suspensionSensor* Part Property, which are the properties of the selected Port type *iSuspension* Block. After the display of properties has been selected, compartments are automatically enabled for the *p1* Port.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

567711109

**Sample model**

The models used in the figures of this page are:

- The Composite structure diagram sample model. To open this sample do one of the following: download *[ATTACHMENT filename='composite structure diagram.mdzip']*or f ind in modeling tool <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\diagrams\composite structure diagram.mdzip.
- The Vehicle parts under ports sample model. To open this sample d *ownload [ATTACHMENT filename='Vehicle parts under ports.mdzip'].*

ac:name="minLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p><br /></p><h4>Using the Display Parts/Ports dialog</h4><p><span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="29785cb7-de15-492f-ae5a-25c23ea254d4">You can use the </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="29785cb7-de15-492f-ae5a-25c23ea254d4">Display Parts/Ports</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="29785cb7-de15-492f-ae5a-25c23ea254d4"> dialog</ac:inline-comment-marker> to control how much of a structure should be displayed. The <span> <strong>Display Parts/Ports </strong>dialog<strong> </strong> </span>opens automatically when creating a new<span class="confluence-link"> <span class="confluence-link"> <span> <span class="confluence-link"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram" /></ac:link></span></span></span></span> for a <span class="confluence-link"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Class" /></ac:link> </span> element that owns properties.</span> <span>It can also be accessed and opened in the </span><span class="confluence-link"><span><span><span>structure </span>diagram</span></span>s that have already been created</span>.<span> The<strong> Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog allows you to:</span></p><ul><li>Preview all <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Part" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Parts]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and other properties in one place.</li><li>Manually or automatically select required <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Part" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Parts]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and other properties you want to display.</li><li>Manually or automatically choose deeply <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Nesting parts that are association ends" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[nested]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Part" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ Parts]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and other properties you want to display.</li><li>Define layout options for <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Part" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Parts]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Specify layout options for <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ul><p>The <span> <strong>Display Parts/Ports </strong>dialog consists of the following areas:</span></p><ul><li><span> <span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span> <span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span> <span> <em>Dialog toolbar</em> allows you to show or hide additional information about listed elements.<br /></span> </span> </span> </span> </span> </span> </span> </span></li><li><span> <span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span> <span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span> <span> <em>Element tree</em> displays all owned internal structure of context <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Class" /></ac:link>.<br /></span> </span> </span> </span> </span> </span> </span> </span></li><li><span> <span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span> <span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span> <span> <em>Panel</em> is a context-sensitive area that<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> depends on the selected property from the Element tree. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">It allows you to batch select <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Part" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Parts]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>/<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:link-body>Ports<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></ac:link-body></ac:link><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span> <span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">in the Element tree. You can</span></span></span></span></span> </span>select all, clear all, or choose only properties by type, stereotype, or aspect.</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></li><li><span><span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span><em style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Options</em> area allows you to choose the layout of the selected parts and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Ports ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>when they appear in the diagram.</span> </span> </span> </span> </span> </span> </span> </span> <span> <span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span> <span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span> </span> </span> </span> </span> </span> <br />  <span> <span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span> <span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span></span></span></span><br /></span></li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display_Parts_Ports_dialog1.png" /></ac:image></p><p><strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></strong></p><p>The following procedure will guide you in working with the <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog when you want to display required properties on the diagram pane.</p><p><br /></p><p>To display Ports and Properties using the <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Open the <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog:</p><ul><li>When creating a new <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram" /></ac:link>, the dialog opens automatically.<span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span></li><li>When editing an already created <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="uml_diagrams.png" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">structure diagram</span></ac:link-body></ac:link>, the dialog can be opened in the following ways:<br />- Select either a diagram frame or single/multiple element shapes, and, o<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">n the diagram toolbar, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png" /></ac:image></span> and choose <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong>.</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br />- Right-click either a diagram frame or single/multiple element shapes, and select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong>.<br /><br /></span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span>(Optional) Enable buttons from the <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog toolbar if you want to show or hide additional information about listed elements:<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span><ul><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Display the full path of the elements"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_the_full_path_of_the_elements.png" /></ac:image></span> to show the full path of elements.<span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <br /></span></span></li><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Display the qualified name of type in the typed elements text"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_the_qualified_name_of_type_in_the typed_elements_text.png" /></ac:image></span> to show the full types of elements.</li><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Show Inherited"><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_inherited_button.png" /></ac:image></span> to show the inherited elements.<span class="confluence-link"> </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Show Private Inherited"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Show_private_inherited.png" /></ac:image></span> to show the inherited properties.</span><br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></li></ul></li><li><p>Choose the properties you want to display by using one or more of the following methods:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0ebed955-61f3-499b-9133-06607c2df4c4"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span>If some <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span>check boxes</span></span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span> are inactive in the element list, </span></span></span>those elements are already <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span>displayed</span></span></span> in the diagram.<br /></span></span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Use the element tree<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> located on <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">the left side of the dialog:</span></span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">-</span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Manually select individual check boxes<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">one by one.</span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span><br />- Select recursively nested elements by holding down the Shift key while selecting the corresponding check box for the element.<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <br /></span></span></span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="927433d1-8a3b-4ca2-ad2e-d567bde15d2a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 1</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">All properties (<em>socket1, socket2, socket3, powerIn</em>) of the <em>Ee</em> Property are selected recursively <span>by holding down the Shift key while selecting the corresponding check box</span> from the element tree</span></span><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> in the <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog.</span></span></p><ac:image ac:title="Recursively selected nested elements in the Display Parts/Ports dialog."><ri:attachment ri:filename="recursively_selected_nested_elements_MD.png" /></ac:image></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Use the context-sensitive panel<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> located on the right side of the dialog:</span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">- <strong>Select Properties</strong> group - displays all property types <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">(including <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> your own</span> stereotypes)</span> that are owned by the selected element <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog</span>.<br />- <strong>Select Ports</strong> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">group - displays all Port types <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">(including your own stereotypes)</span> that are owned by a selected element <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog</span>.</span><br /></span>- <strong>Select All</strong> - selects all check boxes that are expanded <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog.</span><br />- <strong>Clear All</strong> - clears all check boxes, even those that are collapsed (deep nested), <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">from the element tree</span> located on <span>the left side of the dialog.<br /></span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a9a648a8-07dd-4136-9c82-c33cbd61bacb"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 2</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">All ports (<em>socket1, socket2, socket3, powerIn</em>) of the <em>Ee</em> Property are selected <span>in <span><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">the element tree</span></span></span> by using the <span><strong>Select Ports</strong> <span>group</span></span> in the context-sensitive panel </span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">of the <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog.</span></p><ac:image ac:title="Using the context-sensitive panel of the Display Parts/Ports dialog when trying to select required properties."><ri:attachment ri:filename="selecting_ports_using_panel.png" /></ac:image></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span>Specify the layout options in the <strong>Options</strong> area:<br /><ul><li>Parts layout: select the <strong>Layout Parts</strong> check box if you want the Part shapes to be arranged in the automatic layout.</li><li>Usage of layout template: select the <strong>Use Layout Template</strong> check box if you want the layout template to be applied. When this check box is selected, the Part, which is included in the layout template, and all its Ports from the layout template are selected automatically.</li><li>Showing connectors: select the <strong>Show Connectors</strong> check box if you want the connectors to be displayed.</li><li>Ports layout on Parts shape:<br />- <strong>Top/Bottom</strong> - displays Ports on the Part shape from top to bottom.<br />- <strong>Left/Right </strong> - displays Ports on the Part shape from left to right.</li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Click <strong>OK</strong>.</span></span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">After those steps, all parts, ports, and other properties that were selected in the <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog are displayed on the diagram pane.<br /></span></span></p><h4>Using the diagram toolbar and the shortcut menu</h4><p class="confluence-link">In the structure diagrams that have already been created, you can display <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Properties" /></ac:link>, and hide all <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> by using the commands from:</p><ul><li class="confluence-link">The <strong>Display</strong> button on the diagram toolbar.</li><li class="confluence-link">The <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Display</strong> command group under the shortcut menu.</span></li></ul><p>You can also use <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span>the </span><strong>Display</strong> button</span> and <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Display</strong> command group from <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">the shortcut menu as</span></span> their functions are identical. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">All those commands</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span>are interactive and <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">depend upon the selection on the diagram pane. For example, if the Part shape is selected on the diagram pane, both menu contains <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">only </span>the commands that can be applied to the selected <span class="confluence-link">Part</span> shape. If you select nothing in the diagram pane, <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">both the menu</span> shows<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> commands that can be applied</span> to the whole diagram.</span></span></p><p>These <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link">commands under the <strong>Display</strong> button and shortcut menu are <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">comprised of </span></span></span>elements that are still not displayed on the diagram according to their:</p><ul><li>Metatypes.</li><li>Stereotypes.</li><li>Types stereotypes.</li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link">The following procedures will show you how to:</span></span></p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="button"><ac:link-body>How to display Ports, Properties using the <strong>Display</strong> button.</ac:link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="shortcut"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to display Ports, Properties using the shortcut menu.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="hide"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to hide Ports.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="151e0ef2-cf5f-42c8-9b7b-b70191dd1dcd"><ac:parameter ac:name="">button</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>To display Ports, Properties using the <strong>Display</strong> button</p><hr /><ol><li>Select either a diagram frame or single/multiple element shapes.</li><li>O<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">n the diagram toolbar, click </span><ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png" /></ac:image>.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:title="After selecting the Internal Structure of Workplace diagram pane and clicking the Display button, opened menu allows to display Properties and/or Ports."><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_related_elements_button_MD.png" /></ac:image></li><li><p>From the opened command menu, select the required properties to display:<br />        - All Ports that are available to display are listed under the <strong>Display Ports</strong> command group.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8ef07d2a-8928-47ac-9bee-363460e3bac9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7319cc0e-6a3c-4e44-9809-e9817c6a997b">If nothing is selected on the diagram pane, the </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7319cc0e-6a3c-4e44-9809-e9817c6a997b">Display Ports</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7319cc0e-6a3c-4e44-9809-e9817c6a997b"> command group will contain commands that allow you to display two levels of structure: ports are displayed on the diagram frame and on the first level parts.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>        - All Properties that are available to display are listed under the <strong>Display Properties</strong> command group.</p><p><span><span>After selecting the required command from the</span></span> <strong>Display</strong> button<span><span>, the appropriate properties are displayed on the diagram pane.</span></span></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f27456dd-5abb-464b-b57a-d14bee0cbd81"><ac:parameter ac:name="">shortcut</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>To display Ports, Properties using the shortcut menu</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click either the diagram pane or single/multiple element shapes.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, select the <strong>Display</strong> command group.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:title="The Display command group menu allows to display Properties and Ports of the PC Part shape." ac:alt="The Display command group menu allows to display Properties and Ports of the PC Part shape."><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_command_group_MD.png" /></ac:image></li><li><p>From the opened command menu, select the required properties to display:<br />        - All Ports that are available to display are listed under the <strong>Display Ports</strong> command group.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="327c3ff9-f8cc-4d9e-a0b5-0c763e6c790e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If nothing is selected on the diagram pane, the <strong>Display Ports</strong> command group allows you to display two levels of structure: ports are displayed on the diagram frame and on the first level parts.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>        - All Properties that are available to display are listed under the <strong>Display Properties</strong> command group.<br />After selecting the required command from the Display command menu, the appropriate properties, Ports are displayed on the diagram pane.<br /><br /></p></li></ol><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4794c2e6-db49-41b6-ac94-03bcb79efedc"><ac:parameter ac:name="">hide</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>To hide Ports</p><hr /><ul><li>Select either a diagram pane or any number of element shapes, and, o<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">n the diagram toolbar, click <ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png" /></ac:image>  and choose<strong> Hide All Ports</strong>.</span></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Right-click either a diagram pane or any number of element shapes, and select <strong>Display</strong> &gt;<strong> Hide All Ports</strong>.<br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">After selecting the command, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Ports ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>of the selected shape will be hidden from the diagram pane but not removed from the model</span>.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ff629db0-f28c-4b9f-aa61-48e542d03bba"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the <strong>Hide All Ports</strong> command doesn't appear, the selection or <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">diagram </span>doesn't contain any <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Ports
