]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>that can be hidden.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:title="The Hide All Ports command under the Display command group from the shortcut menu of the PC Part shape. " ac:alt="The Hide All Ports command under the Display command group from the shortcut menu of the PC Part shape. "><ri:attachment ri:filename="hide_all_ports_shortcut_menu.png" /></ac:image><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></p></li></ul><h4>Using the smart manipulator toolbar<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></h4><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span>When the properties are already displayed on the<span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="uml_diagrams.png" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">structure diagram</span>s</ac:link-body></ac:link>, you can display their direct <span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link">ports</span></span> using the <strong>Display All Ports</strong> button from the smart manipulator toolbar. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">It does not allow you to display deeply nested <span class="confluence-link">ports</span>, only <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">first-level</span></span></span></span></span></span> ports. To display deeply nested ports, you should use the <span class="confluence-link"><strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog</span>.</span> Follow the steps below to learn how to display ports of a selected property shape.</span></span></span></span></span></p><p>To display Ports on shapes</p><hr /><ol><li>Select<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span> the required</span></span> element shape or a diagram frame.</span> </span></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">  From the smart manipulator toolbar, click <ac:image ac:title="Display All Ports" ac:alt="Display All Ports"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_all_ports.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.<br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span>All <span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">first-level</span></span></span></span></span></span></span> Ports of <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">the selected shape</span> will be displayed on the diagram pane.</span></span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span><br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="feb6590f-3710-4154-9154-2c9ce8edfa7e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p> If the <strong>Display All Ports</strong> button is not available, the selected element or <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">diagram </span>doesn't own any Port.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">   <ac:image ac:title="The Display All Ports button on the smart manipulator toolbar of the PC Part shape. " ac:alt="The Display All Ports button on the smart manipulator toolbar of the PC Part shape. "><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_all_ports_SM_MD.png" /></ac:image> <br /></span></p></li></ol><h4>Using the drag-and-drop operation<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> in the Composite Structure diagram</span></h4><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The most efficient way to create a new property and display it with all <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram" /></ac:link> is by dragging the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Class" /></ac:link> or Property elements. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">This method does not allow you to display deeply nested properties, only <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">first-level</span> properties. To display deeply nested elements, use the <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The following procedures will show you:</span></span></span></span></span></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:link ac:anchor="drag_class"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to create a Property shape and how to display all its Ports when dragging the Class element.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:link ac:anchor="drag_property"><ac:link-body>How to display a Property shape and all its <span class="confluence-link">Ports</span> by dragging the Property element.</ac:link-body></ac:link></span></li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e9f47650-536d-46c6-8f68-c5881f221387"><ac:parameter ac:name="">drag_class</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">You can create a new Property by dragging the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Class" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Class ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>element <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">from the <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model Browser" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Model Browser</span> </ac:link-body></ac:link>to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram" /></ac:link> pane</span>. The newly created Property will be typed by the dragged <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Class" /></ac:link> and displayed on the diagram pane. If this <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Class" /></ac:link> has <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> defined, you can display them by using the long drag and selecting an appropriate option from the opened menu (see Example 3). <br /></span></span></p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p>To create and display a Property by dragging a Class element</p><hr /><ul><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Drag a single or a multiple Class element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane.</span></p></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Drag a single or a multiple Class element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane</span>, <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">and hold it down <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">until the Tooltip appears (long drag)</span>.</span></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span>Drop it, and from the opened menu, select the <strong>Create Property Symbol </strong>command.</span></span></span><br />The new Property is created in the model and displayed on the diagram pane. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The newly created Property is typed by the dragged Class automatically.</span></span></span></span> </span><br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></p></li></ul><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d33f1ab5-07e0-42c6-8681-25e8e07df1d5">To create and display a Property and display all its Ports by dragging a Class element</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><hr /><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Drag a single or a multiple Class element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane, and hold it down <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">until the Tooltip appears (long drag)</span>.</span></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Drop it, and, from the opened menu, select the <strong>Create Property Symbol and Display All Ports</strong> command.</span><br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The new Property is created in the model and displayed with all its Ports on the diagram pane. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The newly created Property is typed by the dragged Class automatically.</span></span></span><br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="35a694c8-df84-455d-8e7f-eaa249205f99"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Tip</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>After creating an element, it is beneficial to find its location in the model tree by performing one of the following:<br />        - Right-click the shape, and, from the shortcut menu, choose the <strong>Select in Containment Tree</strong> command.<br />        - Select the shape and press Alt+B.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d407cdbc-1a05-485b-9d7d-bd7aec91f448"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 3</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">After selecting the <strong>Create Property Symbol and Display All Ports</strong> command and </span></span>dragging (<span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">long drag</span></span>) the Class element to <em>Internal Structure of Workplace</em> diagram pane, it </span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">creates a new Property typed by a </span><em>Duplex Power Outlet</em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Class and displays all Ports on it.</span></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="dragging_class_on-diagram_pane.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="593bf531-1a65-4783-ac36-35a678036e74"><ac:parameter ac:name="">drag_property</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">You can display a Property shape by dragging a Property element from the Model Browser to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram" /></ac:link> pane. If this property has <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> defined, <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">you can display them by using the long drag and selecting an appropriate option from the opened menu (see Example 4).</span></span><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The following procedures describe how to display Property shape and all its <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> by dragging the Property element.</span></p><p>To display a Property by dragging a Property element</p><hr /><ul><li>Drag a single or a multiple Property element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane.</li><li>Drag a single or a multiple Property element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane, and hold it down <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">until the Tooltip appears (long drag)</span>. <span>Drop it, and from the opened menu, select the<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong> Create Property Symbol </strong></span>command.</span><br />The Property shape is displayed on the diagram pane.<br /><br /></li></ul><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="21aea531-8fce-41e3-a048-c759c72d59e2">To display a Property with all of its Ports by dragging a Property element</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><hr /><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Drag a single or a multiple Property element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane, and hold it down until the Tooltip appears (long drag). </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Drop it, and, from the opened menu, select the<strong> Create Property Symbol and Display All Ports</strong> command.</span><br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The Property shape is displayed with all its Ports on that shape.</span><br /></span></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="00be8f45-9eb5-4f84-b3c5-e89d3731db2e"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">After selecting the </span><strong>Create Property Symbol and Display All Ports</strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> command, and dragging (long drag) the Property element to</span><em> Internal Structure of Workplace</em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> diagram, it displays Property shape and all Ports on it.</span></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="dragging_property.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">After completing the steps outlined above, the Property shape and/or all its Ports are displayed on the Composite Structure diagram.</span></p><h3>Displaying properties of a Port</h3><p>If you display properties of a Port, all properties of its type will be displayed inside the Port shape and part compartments will be enabled for that Port.</p><p>To enable Part compartments on the Port shape</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a Port shape whose type has properties.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>O<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">n the diagram toolbar, click </span><ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png" /></ac:image>.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, point to the <strong>Display</strong> command group.</li></ul></li><li>Select the required properties to display.</li></ol><p>The properties are displayed inside the Port shape. Part compartments are enabled for the Port.</p><p>The example below shows the <em>p1</em> Port both before and after displaying its type properties. The properties displayed on the Port shape are the <em>Pressure Status</em> Flow Property and the <em>suspensionSensor</em> Part Property, which are the properties of the selected Port type <em>iSuspension</em> Block. After the display of properties has been selected, compartments are automatically enabled for the <em>p1</em> Port.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="part_compartments_on_port_shape.png" /></ac:image></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cec12941-2515-4aab-8522-c5a866c3eec5"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">567711109</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying elements" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Stereotype" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Applying Stereotypes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Nesting parts that are association ends" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Mapping ports on a Part type change" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Class diagram" /></ac:link>  </li><li><span class="confluence-link"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram" /></ac:link> </span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Component diagram" /></ac:link> </span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Package diagram" /></ac:link> </span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Deployment diagram" /></ac:link> </span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Object diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Object diagram ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The models used in the figures of this page are:</p><ul><li>The <strong>Composite structure diagram </strong>sample model. To open this sample do one of the following: download<span class="confluence-link"><em> <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="composite structure diagram.mdzip"><ri:page ri:content-title="Sample models" /></ri:attachment></ac:link> </em>or f</span>ind in modeling tool <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples\diagrams\composite structure diagram.mdzip.</em></li><li>The <strong>Vehicle parts under ports</strong> sample model. To open this sample d<span class="confluence-link"><em>ownload <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="Vehicle parts under ports.mdzip"><ri:page ri:content-title="Sample models" /></ri:attachment></ac:link>.</em></span></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141209 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00404: Displaying parts and ports according to aspects

- page_id: `189141209`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141209/Displaying+parts+and+ports+according+to+aspects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:37:59.229+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Diagram aspects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

581472387

581472391

581472390

Before using this feature, make sure:

- The diagram with aspect is created. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram aspects' space='']>]]>
- The part and/or port stereotype names matches the aspect name. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='How aspects match with parts and ports' space='']>]]>

When displaying Parts/Ports in the aspect diagram, e.g., Electrical *Data Transfer System* Internal Block Diagram, you can display all the parts/ports of an appropriate aspect, e.g., Electrical, by using the **All By Aspect** button. After selecting this button, all parts or ports that match the diagram's aspect can be displayed. The figure below demonstrates the usage of this button in the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog when you are trying to display all electrical aspect ports of the selected *control Panel* part.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The All By Aspect button usage in the Display Parts/Ports dialog. The illustration displays concepts from SysML Plugin.

581472386

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the **Diagram aspects** sample model, which you can find:

- Download [ATTACHMENT filename='diagram aspects.mdzip'].
- Find in the modeling tool: *<modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML\diagram aspects.mdzip.*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bcd8e89b-1f9f-408a-b6f6-affd9427300f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">581472387</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3df8ea34-f6f7-4cf8-a5ad-f360b21222b8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">581472391</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4cee8920-b70e-421a-aeb8-05dcb0da208a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">581472390</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before using this feature, make sure:</p><ul><li>The diagram with aspect is created. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagram aspects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[The main workflow of using aspects >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>The part and/or port stereotype names matches the aspect name. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="How aspects match with parts and ports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Matching rules >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p>When displaying Parts/Ports in the aspect diagram, e.g., Electrical <em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Data Transfer System</em> Internal Block Diagram, you can display all the parts/ports of an appropriate aspect, e.g., Electrical, by using the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">All By Aspect</strong> button. After selecting this button, all parts or ports that match the diagram's aspect can be displayed. The figure below demonstrates the usage of this button in the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog when you are trying to display all electrical aspect ports of the selected <em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">control Panel</em> part.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="all_by_aspect.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The All By Aspect button usage in the Display Parts/Ports dialog. The illustration displays concepts from SysML Plugin.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a59f1e42-587f-49d8-9347-83280f66505c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">581472386</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The model used in the figures of this page is the <strong>Diagram aspects</strong> sample model, which you can find:</p><ul><li><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Download <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="diagram aspects.mdzip"><ri:page ri:content-title="Sample models" /></ri:attachment></ac:link>. </span></span></span></span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link">Find in the modeling tool: <em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">samples\SysML</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">\<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">diagram aspects</span>.mdzip.</span></span></em></span></span></span><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></span></span></span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189140903 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00405: Displaying paths

- page_id: `189140903`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189140903/Displaying+paths
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:37:18.332+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Displaying elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Warning

1107335505

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Warning

1107335508

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Warning

1107335507

This functionality allows you to visualize relationships that are created between elements in the model, but are not displayed on the diagram pane. There are two options to display paths among the element shapes:

- 
- 

displayedTo display paths between selected element shapes

1. On a diagram pane, select two or more elements.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Right-click to open the shortcut menu, and select Display > Display In-Between Paths .
  - On the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='Display.png'] and select Display In-Between Paths .

- 
  - On the main menu, click the **Analyze** > **Display In-Between Paths**. The **Display In-Between Paths** command is available only when two or more shapes are selected.

The existing paths between selected element shapes are displayed on the diagram pane.

#### WARNING: Warning

Warning

The paths won't be displayed if: 
 - The selected shape doesn't have any relationships with the shapes displayed on the diagram. 
 - All paths are already displayed on the diagram pane. 
Paths to self also will not be displayed.

not_displayedTo display all existing paths of the selected shape

1. On a diagram pane, select one or more elements.
2. Do one of the following: All the existing paths among the selected shape and other shapes on the diagram pane are displayed. If the element has a path to self, this path will be displayed as well.
  1. 
    - Right-click to open the shortcut menu, and select Display > Display All Paths .
    - On the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='Display.png'] and select Display All Paths .
  - 
    - On the main menu, click the Analyze > Display All Paths .

#### TIP: Example of the Display All Paths command

Example of the Display All Paths command

This example presents the workflow on representing the paths between shapes on the diagram pane when they are already created in the model.

The Class shapes without any paths between them are represented in the *Room infrastructure model* Class diagram. In this case, paths between those classes exist in the Containment tree under the Relations node (see the figure below).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If you want to visualize how the *Workplace* Class is related with other shapes displayed on the diagram pane, you can do so easily by using the**Display All Paths** command. One way to reach this command is from the shortcut menu of the selected shape (see the figure below).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

After selecting the**Display All Paths** command, all paths between the *Workplace* Class and other shapes on the diagram pane are displayed (see the figure below). The diagram shows that the *Room* consists of between one and an arbitrary number of *Workplaces*. The *Workplace* consists of *Electric Extension* and *Works Station*. Also, the *Workplace* can contain *Duplex Power Outlet*, *External HDD2*, *Digital Camera*, and *External_HDD*.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To display paths among element shapes automatically when displaying elements

1. On the main menu, select Options > Environment .
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Environment Options' space='']**Environment Options** dialog , find the Display Paths on Element Drop option and set it's value to true .
3. Click OK .
4. Select the elements in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space=''] and drag them on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space='']. The shapes and paths between them are displayed. WarningThe paths won't be displayed if: 
 - The selected shape doesn't have any relationships with the shapes displayed on the diagram. 
 - All paths are already displayed on the diagram pane.

1107335503

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with paths and relationships' space='']

**Related page in Developer Guide**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Paths' space='']

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the **Composite structure diagram** sample model. To open this sample do one of the following:

- Download *[ATTACHMENT filename='composite structure diagram.mdzip'].*
- Find in modeling tool <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\diagrams\composite structure diagram.mdzip.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="37dd8375-c197-4b6f-bf42-a27572384477"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1107335505</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="176da447-7b42-4d75-bab2-b4002a3a64ce"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1107335508</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="073d1c89-9923-40a6-a69a-5d355d870cf2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1107335507</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>This functionality allows you to visualize relationships that are created between elements in the model, but are not displayed on the diagram pane. There are two options to display paths among the element shapes:</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="displayed"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Displaying paths only between selected element shapes.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="not_displayed"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Displaying all existing paths among the shapes.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4a5c1a99-dc96-41e3-bbf0-54960c3255f7"><ac:parameter ac:name="">displayed</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To display paths between selected element shapes</p><hr /><ol><li><p>On a diagram pane, select two or more elements.</p></li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Right-click to open the shortcut menu, and select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong>Display In-Between Paths</strong>.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png" /></ac:image> and select <strong>Display In-Between Paths</strong>.</li></ul></li></ol><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">On the main menu, click the <strong>Analyze</strong> &gt; <strong>Display In-Between Paths</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="df015cf7-dece-418b-bf0b-e0e34099a919"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Display In-Between Paths</strong> command is available only when two or more shapes are selected.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li></ul><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">The existing paths between selected element shapes are displayed on the diagram pane. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="13128f02-7f4d-4518-a6cf-efd94ab17c80"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">The paths won't be displayed if:<br />       - The selected shape doesn't have any relationships with the shapes displayed on the diagram.<br />       - All paths are already displayed on the diagram pane.<br />Paths to self also will not be displayed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b5110f86-ce4d-44a9-86c7-8b422d41d784"><ac:parameter ac:name="">not_displayed</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To display all existing paths of the selected shape</p><hr /><ol><li>On a diagram pane, select one or more elements.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ol><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li>Right-click to open the shortcut menu, and select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong>Display All Paths</strong>.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png" /></ac:image> and select <strong>Display All Paths</strong>.</li></ul></li></ol><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li>On the main menu, click the <strong>Analyze</strong> &gt; <strong>Display All Paths</strong>.</li></ul></li></ul>All the existing paths among the selected shape and other shapes on the diagram pane are displayed. If the element has a path to self, this path will be displayed as well.</li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fe1f11cf-477d-4351-82cb-af2526300618"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example of the Display All Paths command</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">This example presents the workflow on representing the paths between shapes on the diagram pane when they are already created in the model.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">The Class shapes without any paths between them are represented in the <em>Room infrastructure model</em> Class diagram. In this case, paths between those classes exist in the Containment tree under the Relations node (see the figure below).</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="relations_node_and_class_shapes.png" /></ac:image></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">If you want to visualize how the <em>Workplace</em> Class is related with other shapes displayed on the diagram pane, you can do so easily by using the<strong> Display All Paths</strong> command. One way to reach this command is from the shortcut menu of the selected shape (see the figure below).</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-11-3 18:13:4.png" /></ac:image></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">After selecting the<strong> Display All Paths</strong> command, all paths between the <em>Workplace</em> Class and other shapes on the diagram pane are displayed (see the figure below). The diagram shows that the <em>Room</em> consists of between one and an arbitrary number of <em>Workplaces</em>. The <em>Workplace</em> consists of <em>Electric Extension</em> and <em>Works Station</em>. Also, the <em>Workplace</em> can contain <em>Duplex Power Outlet</em>, <em>External HDD2</em>, <em>Digital Camera</em>, and <em>External_HDD</em>.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-11-3 18:13:18.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To display paths among element shapes automatically when displaying elements</p><hr /><ol><li>On the main menu, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>.</li><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Environment Options" /><ac:link-body><strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>, find the <strong>Display Paths on Element Drop</strong> option and set it's value to <em>true</em>.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select the elements in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link> and drag them on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target">The shapes and paths between them are displayed.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="99aac734-b4b5-4ee4-afa4-790fc249f3f4"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The paths won't be displayed if:<br />       - The selected shape doesn't have any relationships with the shapes displayed on the diagram.<br />       - All paths are already displayed on the diagram pane.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="977a11dd-971c-41cd-8a46-e2cd416913fb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1107335503</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="three_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul class="childpages-macro"><li><p class="with-breadcrumbs"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying elements" /></ac:link></p></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with paths and relationships" /></ac:link></li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related page in Developer Guide</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Paths" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Paths]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The model used in the figures of this page is the <strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Composite structure diagram</span></strong> sample model. To open this sample do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Download<span class="confluence-link"><em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span> <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="composite structure diagram.mdzip"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2022x" ri:content-title="Sample models" /></ri:attachment></ac:link>.<br /></span></span></em></span></li><li>Find in modeling tool <em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">samples\diagrams</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">\<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">composite structure diagram</span>.mdzip.</span></span></em></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189142095 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00406: Displaying rake icon

- page_id: `189142095`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189142095/Displaying+rake+icon
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:38:50.823+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Formatting symbols > Displaying icon or image
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can use [IMAGE alt='' src=''] for faster navigation in the model. The rake icon on the symbol indicates that the appropriate element is decomposed and has an inner structure. Double-click the element with rake icon to open the internal diagram.

The rake icon is shown on:

- Call Behavior actions that can refer to another Activity diagram.
- Interaction Uses that can refer to another Sequence diagram.
- Packages that can refer to another Package diagrams.
- Parts that can refer to another Internal Block diagram.
- Use cases can be realized by other behavior diagrams such as Activity, State Machine and Sequence.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To hide rake icon

1. Right-click the symbol and select Symbol Properties or press Alt+Enter.
2. Set the Show Rake Icon property value to false .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can use <ac:image ac:title="Rake" ac:alt="Rake"><ri:attachment ri:filename="rake.png" /></ac:image>  for faster navigation in the model. The rake icon on the symbol indicates that the appropriate element is decomposed and has an inner structure. Double-click the element with rake icon to open the internal diagram.</p><p>The rake icon is shown on:</p><ul><li>Call Behavior actions that can refer to another Activity diagram.</li><li>Interaction Uses that can refer to another Sequence diagram.</li><li>Packages that can refer to another Package diagrams.</li><li>Parts that can refer to another Internal Block diagram.</li><li>Use cases can be realized by other behavior diagrams such as Activity, State Machine and Sequence.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:title="Rake icon on symbol" ac:alt="Rake icon on symbol"><ri:attachment ri:filename="rake_icon.png" /></ac:image></p><p>To hide rake icon</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the symbol and select <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> or press Alt+Enter.</li><li>Set the <strong>Show Rake Icon</strong> property value to <em>false</em>.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189140896 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00407: Displaying related elements

- page_id: `189140896`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189140896/Displaying+related+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:37:17.975+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Displaying elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Example 3

567913020

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Example 3

567913022

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Example 3

567913021

When trying to extend your model or visualize additional elements on the diagram pane, you can represent symbols that are related to a selected symbol via relationships.This functionality allows for displaying shapes and paths among those shapes which have already been created in the model.

After selecting the **Display Related Elements** command, the **Display Related Elements** dialog opens. This dialog consists of the following sections (see the figure below):

- Element box (1) shows the name of the selected element shape of which you are going to display its related elements.
- Relations area (2) lists all relationship types which connect the selected element shape with other elements.
- Expand Relations area (3) allows you to select the direction of the relationships which you want to display on the diagram pane.
- Scope area (4) allows you to select the search scope of the elements related to the selected element.
- Depth area (5) allows you to specify the search range for the selected relationships.
- Always create new symbols option (6) allows you to create a symbol even if a related element already has a symbol in the diagram.
- Layout related elements option (7) lays out new symbols by using the default diagram layout.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The following procedure will guide you through using the **Display Related Elements** command and dialog when you want to represent all related elements via selected relationship types of the selected shape on the diagram pane. The functionality works for the entire diagram, single shapes, or numerous selected shapes.

To display related elements

1. Select a diagram frame or any number of shapes on the diagram pane.
2. Open the **Display Related Elements** dialog in one of the following ways:
  - Right-click to open the shortcut menu, and select Display > Display Related Elements .
  - On the diagram toolbar click [ATTACHMENT filename='display_related_elements.png'] , and select Display Related Elements .
  - On the main menu, click **Analyze** > **Display Related Elements**. ImportantThe **Display Related Elements** dialog cannot be opened if the selected element shape or diagram doesn't contain any related elements. Example 1A quicker way to display related elements of the selected shape is to use its shortcut menu, and select **Display** > **Display Related Elements**. The example below demonstrates the shortcut menu of the Class named *Shipment*. It is showing the need to display related elements of the *Shipment* Class in the *Product Shipment object diagram*.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. In the opened dialog, specify:
  - In the Relations area (2), select check boxes of the listed relationship types.
  - In the Expand Relations area (3), select the direction of the relationships: - Incoming - in which the element participates as client. - Outgoing - in which the element participates as supplier. - Both- in which the element participates as both client and supplier.
  - In the Scope area (4), choose the location in which the related elements should be found: - Whole Project - search in the whole model. - Package - search in the package under which the selected element is created.
  - In the Depth area (5), select the search range for the relationships: - Indefinite - search for all possible relationships. - Definite - search for a specified level of relationships.
4. Select the check box of the Always create new symbols option to create a symbol even when the related element already has a symbol displayed on the diagram pane.
5. Select the check box of the **Layout related elements**option to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Layout' space=''] new symbols by using the default diagram [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Layout' space='']. NoteThe position of the selected element shape can be changed after selecting the **Layout related elements** check box. Example 2For example, if you would like to display all related elements of the *Shipment* Class that are connected through the Generalization relationship, you can find them according to the following criteria:- The direction of the generalization relation is both incoming and outgoing. 
- The related elements to be searched for are in the whole project. 
- The search depth of the relationship is one level.If you want to layout related elements after the display, but don't want to create new symbols which are already displayed in the *Product Shipment object diagram*.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
6. Click **OK**. 
According to the specified relationship type, scope, depth, direction, layout, and creation options, all related elements of the selected shape are represented as shapes and paths on the diagram pane. Example 3After opening the **Display Related Elements** dialog (Example 1), and defining the search criteria in it (Example 2), two Classes (*VendorShipment* and *CustomerShipment*) related with the Shipment Class via the Generalization relationship are displayed on the *Product Shipment object diagram* pane. The figure below is the result of following the steps described in Example 1 and Example 2.[IMAGE alt='' src='']

567913019

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Layout' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements in the diagram' space=''] (Open API)

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the **Inventory Control System** sample model. To open this sample do one of the following:

- Download [ATTACHMENT filename='Inventory Control System.mdzip']
- Find in modeling tool <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\case studies\Inventory Control System.mdzip.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cd0de44d-5c52-4f80-86ba-5a1f0ff0f08d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">567913020</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3219c483-b7a5-487a-a500-a3747eab5873"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">567913022</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aaf9f520-5faf-4bec-8e92-1e996989277e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">567913021</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>When trying to extend your model or visualize additional elements on the diagram pane, you can represent symbols that are related to a selected symbol via relationships.This functionality allows for displaying shapes and paths among those shapes which have already been created in the model.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>After selecting the <strong>Display Related Elements</strong> command, the <strong>Display Related Elements</strong> dialog opens. This dialog consists of the following sections (see the figure below):</p><ul><li><em>Element</em> box (1) shows the name of the selected element shape of which you are going to display its related elements.</li><li><em>Relations</em> area (2) lists all relationship types which connect the selected element shape with other elements.</li><li><em>Expand Relations</em> area (3) allows you to select the direction of the relationships which you want to display on the diagram pane.</li><li><em>Scope</em> area (4) allows you to select the search scope of the elements related to the selected element.</li><li><em>Depth</em> area (5) allows you to specify the search range for the selected relationships.</li><li>Always create new symbols option (6) allows you to create a symbol even if a related element already has a symbol in the diagram.</li><li><em>Layout related elements</em> option (7) lays out new symbols by using the default diagram layout.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:title="The seven areas in the Display Related Elements dialog." ac:alt="The seven areas in the Display Related Elements dialog."><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_related_elements_dialog_areas.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The following procedure will guide you through using the <strong>Display Related Elements</strong> command and dialog when you want to represent all related elements via selected relationship types of the selected shape on the diagram pane. The functionality works for the entire diagram, single shapes, or numerous selected shapes.</p><p> </p><p>To display related elements</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a diagram frame or any number of shapes on the diagram pane.</li><li><p>Open the <strong>Display Related Elements</strong> dialog in one of the following ways:</p><ul><li>Right-click to open the shortcut menu, and select<strong> Display</strong> &gt; <strong>Display Related Elements</strong>.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar click <ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_related_elements.png" /></ac:image>, and select <strong>Display Related Elements</strong>.</li><li><p>On the main menu, click <strong>Analyze</strong> &gt; <strong>Display Related Elements</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8506d135-0ccf-4672-bb3e-67a2276a84cf"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Display Related Elements</strong> dialog cannot be opened if the selected element shape or diagram doesn't contain any related elements.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="71ae0b19-4229-488a-b7c0-35a3f7dd9599"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 1</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>A quicker way to display related elements of the selected shape is to use its shortcut menu, and select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong>Display Related Elements</strong>. The example below demonstrates the shortcut menu of the Class named <em>Shipment</em>. It is showing the need to display related elements of the <em>Shipment</em> Class in the <em>Product Shipment object diagram</em>.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="The Display Related Elements command under the Display command group in the shortcut menu of the Shipment Class." ac:alt="The Display Related Elements command under the Display command group in the shortcut menu of the Shipment Class."><ri:attachment ri:filename="shortcut_menu_display_related_elements.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul></li><li>In the opened dialog, specify:<br /><ul><li>In the Relations area (2), select check boxes of the listed relationship types.</li><li>In the Expand Relations area (3), select the direction of the relationships:<br />- <strong>Incoming</strong> - in which the element participates as client.<br />- <strong>Outgoing</strong> - in which the element participates as supplier.<br />- <strong>Both-</strong>in which the element participates as both client and supplier.</li><li>In the Scope area (4), choose the location in which the related elements should be found:<br />-<strong> Whole Project</strong> - search in the whole model.<br />- <strong>Package </strong>- search in the package under which the selected element is created.</li><li>In the Depth area (5), select the search range for the relationships:<br />-<strong> Indefinite</strong> - search for all possible relationships.<br />- <strong>Definite</strong> - search for a specified level of relationships.</li></ul></li><li>Select the check box of the <strong>Always create new symbols</strong> option to create a symbol even when the related element already has a symbol displayed on the diagram pane.</li><li><p>Select the check box of the <strong>Layout related elements </strong>option to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Layout" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[layout]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> new symbols by using the default diagram <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Layout" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[layout]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ca55d086-99f0-4658-b091-e71aff3855e1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The position of the selected element shape can be changed after selecting the <strong>Layout related elements</strong> check box.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9d05eaf9-951d-4f91-a2e3-71340aa6934f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 2</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>For example, if you would like to display all related elements of the <em>Shipment</em> Class that are connected through the Generalization relationship, you can find them according to the following criteria:</p><p>- The direction of the generalization relation is both incoming and outgoing.<br />- The related elements to be searched for are in the whole project.<br />- The search depth of the relationship is one level.</p><p>If you want to layout related elements after the display, but don't want to create new symbols which are already displayed in the <em>Product Shipment object diagram</em>.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="The Display Related Elements dialog of the Shipment Class." ac:alt="The Display Related Elements dialog of the Shipment Class."><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_related_elements_dialog_of_class_shipment.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />According to the specified relationship type, scope, depth, direction, layout, and creation options, all related elements of the selected shape are represented as shapes and paths on the diagram pane.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8d1e5e9e-6684-4fd6-b528-046e6f11e36c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 3</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>After opening the <strong>Display Related Elements</strong> dialog (Example 1), and defining the search criteria in it (Example 2), two Classes (<em>VendorShipment</em> and <em>CustomerShipment</em>) related with the Shipment Class via the Generalization relationship are displayed on the <em>Product Shipment object diagram</em> pane. The figure below is the result of following the steps described in Example 1 and Example 2.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="VendorShipment and CustomerShipment Classes related with the Shipment Class via the Generalization relationship are displayed on the the Product Shipment object diagram pane." ac:alt="VendorShipment and CustomerShipment Classes related with the Shipment Class via the Generalization relationship are displayed on the the Product Shipment object diagram pane."><ri:attachment ri:filename="related_elements_of_the_shipment_class_displayed_on_the_diagram_pane.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f98c1d05-b3bb-46e6-ba17-e4ac3025074d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">567913019</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul class="childpages-macro"><li class="with-breadcrumbs"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying elements" /></ac:link></li><li><p class="with-breadcrumbs"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Layout" /></ac:link></p></li><li><p class="with-breadcrumbs"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying related elements in the diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Displaying related elements in the diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (Open API)</p></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The model used in the figures of this page is the <strong>Inventory Control System</strong> sample model. To open this sample do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Download <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="Inventory Control System.mdzip"><ri:page ri:content-title="Sample models" /></ri:attachment><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Inventory Control System.mdzip.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><em> </em></li><li>Find in modeling tool<em> &lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples\case studies\Inventory Control System.mdzip.</em></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189140670 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00408: Displaying row elements hierarchically

- page_id: `189140670`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189140670/Displaying+row+elements+hierarchically
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:37:07.918+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Tables > Basic tasks in tables > Hierarchy in tables
- labels: ['nested-instances', 'display-nested-elements', 'display-nested-instances', 'display-mode', 'complete-tree', 'comapct-tree', 'specify-query', 'hierarchy-in-table']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Hierarchical tables are very useful for managing and reviewing data. Hierarchy in a table is a tree-like structure where all the rows are listed according to the elements containment.

**Important**

The hierarchy in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance table' space=''] is displayed according to an instance value that is a slot value of another Instance Specification. Composite instances are displayed.

##### Displaying elements hierarchically in a tableDisplaying elements hierarchically

To display elements hierarchically

1. In the table toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
2. Under the Display Mode option choose: The hierarchy is created in the table by listing all the elements as they are shown in the Containment tree.
  - **Complete tree** to display row elements in a hierarchy where all the owners are represented in separate rows. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - **Compact tree**to display row elements in a hierarchy where the common owners are grouped together in a separate row. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Displaying elements in custom hierarchyDisplaying row elements in a custom hierarchy

To display elements in a custom hierarchy

1. In the Criteria area, next to the Scope box, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='scope_query.png'] icon . [ATTACHMENT filename='specify_query_button.png']
2. Specify the expression to collect table elements:

- Select Containment to display the decomposition of elements related through the Containment relationship.
- Select Structure to display the decomposition of Part Properties related through the Composition relationship.
- Select Custom to display the decomposition of elements related through any criteria of your choice.

If you have selected Custom, please [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying criteria for querying model' space=''].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Specifying expression in the Query dialog

##### Expanding/collapsing nodes in a hierarchical table

To expand/collapse nodes in a hierarchical table

- Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] / [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in the name column. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
- Select the specific row, hold Ctrl, and then press the right/left arrow key on the keyboard.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189140667 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00409: Displaying/hiding element numbers in a row

- page_id: `189140667`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189140667/Displaying+hiding+element+numbers+in+a+row
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:37:07.802+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Tables > Basic tasks in tables > Working with rows
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When creating a new table, elements represented as rows are automatically numbered in the name column. The following procedure is important for those who modify existing tables created before Version 18.4.

To display/hide element numbers in the table

1. Open the Specification window of the table.
2. Set the **Show Element Number** property value to *true* to display element numbers, or *false* to hide them*.*The element numbers are displayed in the name columns before the element name (see the following example). [IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When creating a new table, elements represented as rows are automatically numbered in the name column. The following procedure is important for those who modify existing tables created before Version 18.4.</p><p>To display/hide element numbers in the table</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Specification</strong> window of the table.</li><li><p>Set the <strong>Show Element Number</strong> property value to <em>true</em> to display element numbers, or <em>false</em> to hide them<em>.<br /></em>The element numbers are displayed in the name columns before the element name (see the following example).</p><p><ac:image ac:title="The Requirement numbers are displayed in the Magic Library Requirement table." ac:alt="The Requirement numbers are displayed in the Magic Library Requirement table."><ri:attachment ri:filename="element_number_in_table.png" /></ac:image></p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189145559 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00410: Distributing custom diagrams

- page_id: `189145559`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189145559/Distributing+custom+diagrams
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:46:17.459+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Warning

1523698130

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Warning

1523698132

1523698127

**On this page**

**3**

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Warning

1523698131

A [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing diagrams' space=''] can be exchanged with other usersin a *.zip* file format, as any other resource, for manual import into the modeling tool using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Manager' space='']. Alternatively, you can export/import the custom diagram descriptor (the .*xml*file) using the **Customize Diagrams** dialog.

##### Distributing custom diagrams using Resource Manager

You need to build a*.zip*file containing a diagram descriptor file (e.g., *descriptor.xml*) and a Resource Manager descriptor file (e.g., *MDR_Custom Diagram_MagicDraw_74842_descriptor.xm**l*.)to be able to share your own custom diagram. For this, you need to use the **Development Tools** plugin. Once you generate and share the file, the diagram customization can then be imported into the modeling tool by another user via the **Resource Manager**for future use and accessibility.

#### WARNING: Warning

Warning

You can build a *.zip* file for custom diagram distribution only after you have [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new diagram type' space='']created a newdiagram type or edited an existing standard diagram.

To create a *.zip* file using the **Development Tools** plugin

1. On your local file system, create the following folders structure: ...\<Custom Diagram Resource>\data\defaults\data\diagrams . [IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Launch the modeling tool.
3. Do one of the following:
  - 
    1. In the main menu, go to **Help** > **About**.
    2. Next to Configuration Files , click the file location path. The configuration files folder opens.
    3. Under <configuration files>\data\diagrams, locate your custom diagram folder containing a descriptor .
  - 
    1. In the main menu, go to**Diagrams** >**Customize**.
    2. In the **Customize Diagrams** dialog, select the custom diagram whose descriptor file you want to export and click the**Export** button. WarningBefore the export operation, create a new folder on your local file system and give it the name of your custom diagram, such as *Custom Diagram*. The exported descriptor file needs to be renamed to *descriptor.xml*and saved in this newly-created folder.
4. Copy and paste the custom diagram folder together with the descriptor file into the folders structure you created in thefirststep. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] Distributing ProfilesIf you have specified Profiles when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing diagrams' space=''], it is highly recommended that you distribute them together with your custom diagram. For this, create the *Profiles* folder under the custom diagram resource root folder and place all required Profiles in it before generating a *.zip*archive.You need to mark each Profile distributed together with a custom resource as**Standard/System Profile** (**File** >**Project Properties** > **More** >**Standard/System Profile**) to store it locally. Profiles stored on the server cannot be used for a custom diagram.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. Download and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installing modeling tools and plugins' space='IL2024xR2'] the Development Tools plugin.
6. Restart the modeling tool.
7. In the main menu, click Tools > Development Tools > Build Custom Resource .
8. In the Custom Resource Building Wizard , set the custom diagram folder as the Resource Folder .
9. Click Next to skip the other steps and then click Finish . The resource ( . zip file) is built. It can now be distributed and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Manager' space=''].

#### INFO: Info

Info

A created custom diagram resource (.*zip*) can be bundled into the resource distribution file (.*rdzip*) using the **Development Tools** plugin for distribution alongside other resources. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Building a resource distribution file' space='']>]]>

##### Distributing custom diagrams using Customize Diagrams dialog

You can export the custom diagram descriptor (.*xml*) file directly into the**Customize Diagrams** dialog. The exported file can then be shared with others and imported into the modeling tool by another user via the same **Customize Diagrams** dialog using the **Import**command.

#### WARNING: Warning

Warning

You can export an .*xml*file for custom diagram distribution only after you have[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new diagram type' space='']or edited an existing standard diagram.

To export a custom diagram descriptor

1. In the main menu, go to Diagrams > Customize .
2. Select a custom diagram from the list and click the Export button. [ATTACHMENT filename='exporting_diagram_descriptor.png']

The .*xml* file is exported. You can now distribute it.

To import a custom diagram descriptor into the modeling tool

1. In the main menu, go to Diagrams > Customize .
2. Click the **Import** button and point to a descriptor (.*xml*)**on your local file system. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

The *.xml* file is imported, and a new diagram type appears under **Custom** **Diagrams**in the **Customize Diagrams** dialog.****

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b277f74b-e3a3-427b-a2c3-cacc84dd1cd6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1523698130</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e5065f0a-667b-4025-a1ec-446a822b81e4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1523698132</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f1ab2bcd-1e4b-44c3-b64e-a0452d78669c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1523698127</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e0c159d6-10b5-4b2a-a6db-feb87db6c1ef"><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><br /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="97826136-737d-4280-9c80-6a6996e8c1b8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1523698131</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>A <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customizing diagrams" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[custom diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="885cb35b-65e1-4587-be5b-555938d081bc">can be exchanged with other users </ac:inline-comment-marker>in a <em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.zip</em> file format, as any other resource, for manual import into the modeling tool using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Manager" /></ac:link>. Alternatively, you can export/import the custom diagram descriptor (the .<em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">xml </em>file) using the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Customize Diagrams</strong> dialog. </p><h3>Distributing custom diagrams using Resource Manager</h3><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="60547a15-a3ad-448d-af43-5e98a7e8f3ff">You need to build a </ac:inline-comment-marker><em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="60547a15-a3ad-448d-af43-5e98a7e8f3ff">.zip</ac:inline-comment-marker></em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="60547a15-a3ad-448d-af43-5e98a7e8f3ff"> file containing a diagram descriptor file (e.g., <em>descriptor.xml</em></ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="60547a15-a3ad-448d-af43-5e98a7e8f3ff">) and a Resource Manager descriptor file (e.g., <em>MDR_Custom Diagram_MagicDraw_74842_descriptor.xm</em><em>l</em><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">.) </span>to be able to share your own custom diagram.</ac:inline-comment-marker> For this, you need to use the <strong>Development Tools</strong> plugin. Once you generate and share the file, the diagram customization can then be imported into the modeling tool by another user via the <strong>Resource Manager </strong>for future use and accessibility. </p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cf5b8bcc-78e7-416b-a481-a4cb9bb09c9e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can build a <em>.zip</em> file for custom diagram distribution <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e138a9a9-2a19-4a0a-b561-37fa6c4738c6">only after</ac:inline-comment-marker> you have <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating new diagram type" /><ac:link-body>created a new<span> diagram type</span></ac:link-body></ac:link> or edited an existing standard diagram.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create a <em>.zip</em> file using the <strong>Development Tools</strong> plugin</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="4ad3836a-5451-4c2d-9781-c716582b08f0">On your local file system,</ac:inline-comment-marker> create the following folders structure: <em>...\&lt;Custom Diagram Resource&gt;\data\default<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="176a7421-9e59-4b0a-8345-332a8a3bd37b">s\data\diagrams</ac:inline-comment-marker></em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="176a7421-9e59-4b0a-8345-332a8a3bd37b">.</ac:inline-comment-marker><br /><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="176a7421-9e59-4b0a-8345-332a8a3bd37b"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="custom_diagram_resource_folders_structure.png" /></ac:image><br /></ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>Launch the modeling tool. </li><li>Do one of the following: <ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3c2d772e-abb8-40d8-a463-f017fa5bbb44"><br /></ac:inline-comment-marker><ol><li><ac:inline-comment-marker
````
